User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I need a secure, personal login (username/password/MFA) so that I can access my family member's care information confidentially and safely.
Description of User Story: The Nemicare Family Portal provides Approved Family Contacts with a secure, personal login experience comprising username/password credentials and multi-factor authentication (MFA) via email-based OTP. The registration flow begins when the facility sends an email invitation containing a unique, time-limited link to set up portal access. Upon clicking the link, the family contact is directed to a Set Your Password screen with a split layout featuring a care photo on the left and the form on the right, branded with the Nemi Care logo. The contact enters and confirms a new password meeting complexity requirements (minimum length, uppercase, lowercase, number, special character). After password creation, the contact proceeds to complete their profile information and selects a linked resident. On subsequent logins, the contact enters their email and password on the Sign In screen, then verifies identity via a six-digit OTP sent to their registered email before gaining access. A HIPAA compliance badge in the footer reinforces the security posture of the portal. All authentication events are logged for audit purposes.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the Approved Family Contact enters a password meeting all complexity requirements (minimum 8 characters, uppercase, lowercase, number, special character), Then the system must accept the password and proceed.
   Else if the password does not meet one or more complexity requirements, Then the system must display specific inline validation messages identifying which requirements are not met.
   Else if the New Password and Confirm Password fields do not match, Then the system must display a "Passwords do not match" error and block submission.

2. If the invitation link is valid and has not expired, Then the system must display the Set Your Password screen.
   Else if the invitation link has expired, Then the system must display a "Link expired" message with instructions to contact the facility for a new invitation.
   Else if the invitation link has already been used, Then the system must display a message indicating the account has already been set up and redirect to Sign In.

3. If the Approved Family Contact enters valid credentials on the Sign In screen, Then the system must send an OTP to the registered email and navigate to the Enter OTP screen.
   Else if the credentials are invalid, Then the system must display an "Invalid email or password" error without revealing which field is incorrect.

4. If the contact enters the correct OTP within the validity window, Then the system must authenticate the session and grant portal access.
   Else if the OTP is incorrect, Then the system must display an "Invalid OTP" error and allow retry.
   Else if the OTP has expired, Then the system must display an "OTP expired" message and offer a "Resend OTP" option.

5. If the contact's account is locked after multiple failed login attempts, Then the system must display an account locked message with instructions to reset the password or contact the facility.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that an Approved Family Contact can click the invitation link, set a password "SecureP@ss1", confirm it, and proceed to the profile setup step successfully.
2. Verify that after setting a password, the contact can sign in with email "johndoe@example.com" and password "SecureP@ss1", receive a six-digit OTP on the registered email, enter it, and access the portal.
3. Verify that the "Remember me" checkbox on the Sign In screen retains the email address for the next login session.
4. Verify that the HIPAA compliance badge is displayed in the footer of the Sign In, Enter OTP, and Set Your Password screens.

Negative:
5. Verify that entering a password "abc" that does not meet minimum length requirements displays an inline error specifying "Password must be at least 8 characters".
6. Verify that entering "SecureP@ss1" as New Password and "SecureP@ss2" as Confirm Password displays a "Passwords do not match" error.
7. Verify that entering an incorrect email "wrong@example.com" or wrong password displays "Invalid email or password" without revealing which field is wrong.
8. Verify that entering an incorrect OTP "000000" displays an "Invalid OTP" error message.
9. Verify that clicking an expired invitation link (older than 72 hours) displays a "Link expired" message.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Sign In screen displays a split layout with a care photo on the left and the login form on the right, with the Nemi Care logo at the top.
11. Verify that the Password field on the Sign In screen includes an eye icon toggle to show/hide the password.
12. Verify that the Enter OTP screen displays six individual digit input boxes for the OTP with auto-focus advancing to the next box after each digit entry.
13. Verify that the "Continue" and "Set Password" buttons are styled as blue primary buttons spanning the form width.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that clicking the same invitation link a second time after password has been set redirects to the Sign In screen with an "Account already activated" message.
15. Verify that a contact who closes the browser mid-registration can resume by clicking the invitation link again if the link has not expired.
16. Verify that entering an OTP with leading spaces or trailing spaces is trimmed and validated correctly.

Security:
17. Verify that the password is not visible in the URL, network request payload is sent over HTTPS, and the password field masks input by default.
18. Verify that after 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the account is temporarily locked for 15 minutes.
19. Verify that OTP codes expire after 5 minutes and cannot be reused after successful verification.
20. Verify that session tokens are invalidated after logout and cannot be replayed to gain unauthorized access.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Sign In screen loads completely within 2 seconds including the care photo, logo, and form elements.
Verify that OTP delivery to the registered email completes within 5 seconds of clicking "Continue" on the Sign In screen.
Verify that OTP verification and session creation complete within 2 seconds of clicking "Verify & Continue".
Verify that the Set Your Password screen loads within 2 seconds when accessed via the invitation link.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact (e.g., an adult child), I need the ability to view and toggle between multiple linked residents (e.g., two parents) after a single sign-in, so that I can manage all my family members efficiently from one account.
Description of User Story: The resident switching feature allows an Approved Family Contact who is linked to multiple residents (e.g., both parents in the same or different facilities) to toggle between them after a single sign-in without re-authenticating. After login and OTP verification, the contact lands on either the last-viewed resident's dashboard or a resident selection screen if no preference is stored. A resident switcher component is accessible from the main navigation or header area, presenting each linked resident as a card displaying the resident's name, photo, room number, and program type badge (ADH or ALF). Selecting a different resident refreshes the dashboard and all portal sections to reflect that resident's care data, medications, vitals, appointments, and billing. The currently active resident is visually highlighted in the switcher. The system maintains separate data contexts for each resident, ensuring no cross-contamination of information between linked profiles.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the Approved Family Contact is linked to multiple residents, Then the resident switcher must display all linked residents with their name, room number, and program type.
   Else if the contact is linked to only one resident, Then the resident switcher must not be displayed, and the portal must load that single resident's data directly.

2. If the contact selects a different resident from the switcher, Then the system must refresh all portal sections to display the selected resident's data within the same session.
   Else if the contact selects the already-active resident, Then the system must not perform a refresh and must keep the current view.

3. If a linked resident's status changes to inactive or discharged, Then the system must remove that resident from the switcher and display a notification explaining the change.
   Else if all linked residents are inactive, Then the system must display a message indicating no active residents are available and provide facility contact information.

4. When the contact switches residents, Then the system must log the switch event in the audit trail with the timestamp and both the source and target resident identifiers.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that after logging in, an Approved Family Contact linked to Devon Michael Lane (Room 204, ADH) and Linda Michael Lane (Room 201, ALF) sees both residents in the resident switcher.
2. Verify that selecting Linda Michael Lane from the switcher updates the dashboard to show Linda's vitals, medications, and appointments.
3. Verify that switching back to Devon Michael Lane from Linda restores Devon's complete care data on the dashboard.
4. Verify that the currently viewed resident is highlighted with a distinct visual indicator (e.g., border color or "Currently Viewing" label) in the switcher.

Negative:
5. Verify that a contact linked to only one resident does not see the resident switcher component anywhere in the portal.
6. Verify that when a linked resident is discharged, the switcher removes that resident and displays a notification "This resident is no longer active".
7. Verify that attempting to access a switched resident's data via URL manipulation after the resident is unlinked returns an access-denied error.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the resident switcher displays each resident as a card with photo, name, room icon with room number, and a program type badge (ADH/ALF).
9. Verify that the resident switcher is accessible from the header/navigation area and opens as a dropdown or modal overlay.
10. Verify that each resident card includes a "View" button to switch to that resident's dashboard.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a contact linked to 5 residents can scroll through all resident cards in the switcher without truncation.
12. Verify that switching residents during an active data load (e.g., while medications are loading) cancels the previous request and loads the new resident's data cleanly.
13. Verify that if two browser tabs are open and the contact switches residents in one tab, the other tab continues displaying the original resident until manually refreshed.

Security:
14. Verify that the portal does not expose any data from Resident A when viewing Resident B's dashboard, including in network responses.
15. Verify that the audit log records each resident switch event with the contact's user ID, timestamp, and source/target resident IDs.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that switching between linked residents refreshes the dashboard and all visible data within 2 seconds.
Verify that the resident switcher component loads all linked resident cards within 1 second of being opened.
Verify that switching residents does not cause a full page reload but uses in-place data refresh.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I need to view basic patient demographics (e.g., age, room number) and current program enrollment status (e.g., ADHS Level II), so that I stay informed about the foundational aspects of their care.
Description of User Story: The patient demographics section of the Family Portal dashboard displays key demographic and enrollment information for the currently selected resident. The demographics panel shows the resident's full name, date of birth, age (calculated dynamically), gender, room number, and a profile photo. The program enrollment status section displays the resident's current program type (e.g., ADH, ALF), program level (e.g., ADHS Level II), enrollment date, and current enrollment status (Active, On Hold, Discharged). This information is presented in a summary card or panel format that is always visible on the dashboard, providing family contacts with at-a-glance foundational care context. The data is read-only for family contacts and is sourced from the facility's patient management system, updating automatically when the facility staff makes changes.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the dashboard loads for the selected resident, Then the system must display the resident's full name, date of birth, calculated age, gender, room number, and profile photo in the demographics panel.

2. If the resident has an active program enrollment, Then the enrollment section must display the program type, level, enrollment date, and "Active" status.
   Else if the resident's enrollment is on hold, Then the enrollment section must display "On Hold" status with the hold reason if available.
   Else if the resident has been discharged, Then the enrollment section must display "Discharged" status with the discharge date.

3. If the resident's profile photo is available, Then the system must display the photo in the demographics panel.
   Else if no profile photo is available, Then the system must display a default avatar placeholder with the resident's initials.

4. If the resident's date of birth is available, Then the system must calculate and display the current age dynamically.
   Else if the date of birth is not recorded, Then the age field must display "N/A".
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the demographics panel for Devon Michael Lane displays full name "Devon Michael Lane", date of birth "03/15/1945", age "81", gender "Male", room number "Room 204", and a profile photo.
2. Verify that the enrollment section displays program type "ADH", program level "ADHS Level II", enrollment date "01/10/2025", and status "Active".
3. Verify that after the facility updates the room number from 204 to 210, refreshing the dashboard shows "Room 210".
4. Verify that the resident's age recalculates correctly on their birthday (e.g., changes from 80 to 81).

Negative:
5. Verify that the demographics panel fields (name, DOB, room number) are not editable by the family contact and no edit icons or buttons are displayed.
6. Verify that when the enrollment status is "Discharged", the enrollment section displays the discharge date and the dashboard limits access to historical data only.
7. Verify that when the resident's profile photo fails to load, a default avatar with initials "DL" is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the demographics panel is displayed as a summary card at the top of the dashboard with the profile photo on the left, name and demographics in the center, and enrollment status on the right.
9. Verify that the enrollment status badge uses color coding: green for "Active", yellow for "On Hold", and grey for "Discharged".
10. Verify that the demographics panel is responsive and stacks vertically on mobile screen widths.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a resident with no program level assigned displays the program type only (e.g., "ADH") without a blank level field.
12. Verify that a resident born on February 29 (leap year) has their age calculated correctly on non-leap years.

Security:
13. Verify that demographics data is served over HTTPS and the API endpoint requires a valid authenticated session token.
14. Verify that the family contact can only view demographics for residents they are linked to, and direct API calls for other residents return a 403 Forbidden response.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the demographics panel including profile photo, demographic fields, and enrollment status loads within 1.5 seconds of dashboard navigation.
Verify that the age calculation renders instantly on page load without a visible delay or placeholder flicker.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I need to view a high-level summary of attendance, activities participated in (e.g., 'Mom attended devotional activity today'), and overall engagement, so that I can gauge their daily well-being and social interaction.
Description of User Story: The engagement summary section on the Family Portal dashboard provides Approved Family Contacts with a high-level view of their loved one's daily attendance, activities, and overall engagement at the facility. The attendance summary shows whether the resident was present today, their check-in and check-out times, and a month-to-date attendance count or percentage. The activities section lists the activities the resident participated in during the current day, each displayed as a descriptive line item (e.g., "Attended devotional activity", "Participated in art therapy session"). An overall engagement score or indicator provides a visual representation of the resident's social interaction level over a configurable period (e.g., weekly or monthly). The section is updated as facility staff records attendance and activity participation throughout the day, giving family contacts near-real-time insight into their loved one's daily life at the facility.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the dashboard loads for the current day, Then the engagement summary must display attendance status, activity list, and engagement indicator.

2. If the resident has been checked in today, Then the attendance section must show "Present" with the check-in timestamp.
   Else if the resident has not arrived yet but is scheduled, Then the attendance section must show "Expected" with the scheduled arrival time.
   Else if the resident is not scheduled today, Then the attendance section must show "Not Scheduled" for the day.

3. If the resident participated in one or more activities today, Then the activities section must list each activity with a descriptive label.
   Else if the resident has not participated in any activities yet, Then the activities section must display "No activities recorded yet today".

4. If the resident has been checked in and checked out, Then the attendance section must display both check-in and check-out times.
   Else if the resident is still checked in, Then the attendance section must display only the check-in time with status "Currently at facility".

5. If the engagement score data is available, Then the engagement indicator must display the score with a visual gauge or progress bar.
   Else if engagement data is insufficient (e.g., less than 3 days of data), Then the indicator must display "Not enough data to calculate engagement" instead of a misleading score.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the attendance summary shows "Present", check-in time "9:00 AM", and check-out time "3:30 PM" for a resident who attended today.
2. Verify that the activities section lists "Attended devotional activity" and "Participated in art therapy session" for a resident who completed both activities.
3. Verify that the month-to-date attendance displays "18 of 22 days" for a resident who has attended 18 out of 22 scheduled days.
4. Verify that after staff records a new "Music therapy" activity at 2:00 PM, refreshing the dashboard adds "Participated in music therapy" to the activities list.
5. Verify that the engagement indicator displays a weekly engagement score as a visual progress bar or percentage.

Negative:
6. Verify that when the resident is not scheduled today, the attendance section displays "Not Scheduled" without check-in/check-out fields.
7. Verify that when no activities have been recorded for the day, the activities section displays "No activities recorded yet today".
8. Verify that when engagement data has fewer than 3 days of history, the engagement indicator displays "Not enough data to calculate engagement".

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the attendance summary displays a green badge for "Present", a yellow badge for "Expected", and a grey badge for "Not Scheduled".
10. Verify that each activity in the activities list is displayed as a separate line item with a descriptive icon (e.g., music note, art palette).
11. Verify that the engagement indicator is displayed as a circular progress gauge or horizontal bar with a percentage label.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that a resident who is checked in but not yet checked out shows "Currently at facility" instead of a blank check-out time.
13. Verify that a resident who attended 10 activities in one day has all 10 listed without truncation, with scrolling if needed.
14. Verify that the month-to-date attendance count resets correctly on the first day of a new month.

Security:
15. Verify that the engagement summary API endpoint returns data only for residents linked to the authenticated family contact.
16. Verify that activity details do not expose clinical notes or staff-only comments in the family portal view.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the engagement summary section including attendance, activities, and engagement indicator loads within 2 seconds of dashboard navigation.
Verify that refreshing the dashboard updates the activities list with newly recorded activities within 2 seconds.
Verify that the month-to-date attendance calculation renders within 1 second without visible computation delay.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to receive an email invitation from the facility to set up my portal access.
Description of User Story: The email invitation feature enables facility administrators to send a secure, branded invitation email to an Approved Family Contact to initiate their portal registration. The facility staff enters the contact's name and email address in the administration panel and triggers the invitation. The system generates a unique, time-limited invitation link and sends a professionally formatted email containing the Nemi Care branding, a welcome message, the contact's name, the linked resident's name, instructions for setting up their account, and a prominent "Set Up Your Account" call-to-action button containing the invitation link. The email also includes facility contact information for support. The invitation link expires after a configurable period (e.g., 72 hours) for security. The system tracks the invitation status (Sent, Opened, Completed, Expired) so facility staff can monitor registration progress and resend invitations as needed.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the facility administrator enters a valid email address and clicks "Send Invitation", Then the system must generate a unique invitation link and deliver the email within 60 seconds.
   Else if the email address format is invalid, Then the system must display an "Invalid email format" validation error and block sending.
   Else if an active invitation already exists for the same email, Then the system must prompt whether to resend the existing invitation or cancel.

2. If the invitation link is clicked within the validity period, Then the system must direct the contact to the Set Your Password screen.
   Else if the invitation link has expired, Then the system must display a "Link expired" message with a facility contact number for assistance.

3. If the family contact completes registration using the invitation link, Then the system must update the invitation status to "Completed" and deactivate the link.

4. If the email delivery fails (bounce or rejection), Then the system must update the invitation status to "Failed" and notify the administrator.
   Else if the email is delivered but not opened within 48 hours, Then the system must flag the invitation for administrator follow-up.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a facility administrator can send an invitation to "johndoe@example.com" for resident Devon Michael Lane, and the email is delivered within 60 seconds.
2. Verify that the invitation email contains the Nemi Care logo, welcome text "Welcome to Nemicare Family Portal", the resident's name "Devon Michael Lane", and a "Set Up Your Account" button.
3. Verify that clicking the "Set Up Your Account" button in the email navigates to the Set Your Password screen with the invitation token in the URL.
4. Verify that the administrator can view invitation status as "Sent" immediately after sending and "Completed" after the contact finishes registration.
5. Verify that the administrator can resend an invitation to "johndoe@example.com" and the previous invitation link is invalidated.

Negative:
6. Verify that entering an invalid email "johndoe@" displays an "Invalid email format" error and blocks the send action.
7. Verify that clicking an expired invitation link (after 72 hours) displays a "This invitation link has expired" message.
8. Verify that attempting to send a duplicate invitation to an email with an active pending invitation prompts a confirmation dialog.
9. Verify that an invitation sent to a non-existent email address updates the status to "Failed" and notifies the administrator.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the invitation email is professionally formatted with Nemi Care branding, readable fonts, and a mobile-responsive layout.
11. Verify that the "Set Up Your Account" button in the email is styled as a prominent blue button matching the portal's design language.
12. Verify that the invitation management screen displays a table with columns for Contact Name, Email, Resident, Status, Sent Date, and a Resend action button.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that sending an invitation and the contact completing registration within 5 minutes correctly transitions status from "Sent" to "Completed".
14. Verify that an invitation link opened in multiple browser tabs only allows registration to complete in one tab, with others showing "Already activated".

Security:
15. Verify that the invitation link token is unique, cryptographically random, and cannot be guessed or enumerated.
16. Verify that the invitation link is transmitted over HTTPS and the email does not expose any PHI about the resident beyond their name.
17. Verify that expired invitation tokens return a 410 Gone response when accessed via direct API call.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the invitation email is sent and delivered to the recipient's inbox within 60 seconds of the administrator clicking "Send Invitation".
Verify that the invitation management screen loads invitation statuses for up to 500 contacts within 2 seconds.
Verify that clicking the invitation link loads the Set Your Password screen within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to set up my password during first-time login using the invitation link.
Description of User Story: The first-time password setup screen is presented when an Approved Family Contact clicks the invitation link received via email. The screen uses a split layout with a care photo on the left and the password form on the right, consistent with the portal's design language. The heading reads "Set Your Password" and the form contains two fields: New Password (required, with eye icon toggle) and Confirm Password (required, with eye icon toggle). A blue "Set Password" button submits the form. Password complexity requirements are enforced inline: minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character. Upon successful password creation, the system activates the account and redirects the contact to the onboarding flow (Complete Your Profile). The invitation link is single-use and is deactivated once the password is set successfully.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the Approved Family Contact enters a password that meets all complexity requirements and the Confirm Password matches, Then the system must create the account password and redirect to the Complete Your Profile screen.
   Else if the password does not meet the minimum length of 8 characters, Then the system must display "Password must be at least 8 characters".
   Else if the password lacks an uppercase letter, Then the system must display "Password must contain at least one uppercase letter".
   Else if the New Password and Confirm Password do not match, Then the system must display "Passwords do not match".

2. If the invitation link is valid and unused, Then the system must render the Set Your Password screen.
   Else if the invitation link has already been used, Then the system must display "This link has already been used. Please sign in with your credentials" and provide a link to the Sign In page.
   Else if the invitation link has expired, Then the system must display "This link has expired. Please contact your facility for a new invitation".

3. If the password is set successfully, Then the system must deactivate the invitation link and log the account creation event in the audit trail.

4. If the server encounters an error during password creation, Then the system must display a generic error message "Something went wrong. Please try again" and allow retry without losing the entered data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the invitation link opens the "Set Your Password" screen with New Password and Confirm Password fields and a blue "Set Password" button.
2. Verify that entering "MySecure@Pass1" in both fields and clicking "Set Password" creates the account and redirects to the Complete Your Profile screen.
3. Verify that the eye icon toggle on the New Password field switches between masked (dots) and visible (plain text) display.
4. Verify that after successful password setup, the invitation link is deactivated and the account status changes to "Active".

Negative:
5. Verify that entering "short1" (less than 8 characters) as the password displays "Password must be at least 8 characters".
6. Verify that entering "alllowercase1!" (no uppercase) displays "Password must contain at least one uppercase letter".
7. Verify that entering "ALLUPPERCASE1!" (no lowercase) displays "Password must contain at least one lowercase letter".
8. Verify that entering "NoNumber!Pass" (no digit) displays "Password must contain at least one number".
9. Verify that entering "NoSpecial1Pass" (no special character) displays "Password must contain at least one special character".
10. Verify that entering "MySecure@Pass1" as New Password and "MySecure@Pass2" as Confirm Password displays "Passwords do not match".
11. Verify that clicking "Set Password" with both fields empty displays required field validation errors.

UI Scenarios:
12. Verify that the Set Your Password screen uses a split layout with a care photo on the left half and the form on the right half.
13. Verify that the heading "Set Your Password" is displayed prominently above the form fields.
14. Verify that the "Set Password" button is styled as a full-width blue primary button.
15. Verify that password complexity requirements are listed below the New Password field as a checklist that updates in real time as the user types.

Edge Cases:
16. Verify that pasting a password from the clipboard into the New Password field triggers the same complexity validation as typing.
17. Verify that a password exactly 8 characters long meeting all other complexity rules (e.g., "Abcde1!x") is accepted.
18. Verify that submitting the form during a temporary network interruption displays a retry-friendly error without losing the entered password.

Security:
19. Verify that the password is transmitted over HTTPS and is never logged in plain text in network requests or server logs.
20. Verify that the invitation token in the URL cannot be reused after successful password setup.
21. Verify that brute-force attempts on the password setup endpoint are rate-limited after 10 requests per minute.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Set Your Password screen loads within 2 seconds when accessed via the invitation link.
Verify that password complexity validation feedback appears within 300 milliseconds of keystroke.
Verify that the "Set Password" submission and redirect to the Complete Your Profile screen completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to login using valid credentials.
Description of User Story: The Sign In screen provides a secure authentication entry point for Approved Family Contacts to access the Nemicare Family Portal. The screen uses a split layout with a care-related photo on the left side and the login form on the right side. The Nemi Care logo is displayed at the top. The form heading reads "Sign In Your Account" and includes an Email address field (required, marked with asterisk), a Password field (required, with eye icon toggle to show/hide), a "Remember me" checkbox to persist the email for future visits, and a "Forgot Password?" link for password recovery. A blue "Continue" button submits the credentials. Upon successful credential validation, the system proceeds to the OTP verification step. The HIPAA compliance badge is displayed in the footer, reinforcing the portal's security commitment. Invalid credentials trigger an error message that does not reveal whether the email or password was incorrect.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the Approved Family Contact enters a valid email and correct password and clicks "Continue", Then the system must validate credentials and redirect to the Enter OTP screen.
   Else if the email does not exist in the system, Then the system must display "Invalid email or password" without specifying which field is wrong.
   Else if the email is valid but the password is incorrect, Then the system must display "Invalid email or password" without specifying which field is wrong.

2. If the Email field is empty when "Continue" is clicked, Then the system must display "Email is required" validation error.
   Else if the Password field is empty when "Continue" is clicked, Then the system must display "Password is required" validation error.
   Else if the Email format is invalid (e.g., missing @ symbol), Then the system must display "Please enter a valid email address".

3. If the "Remember me" checkbox is checked, Then the system must store the email locally and pre-populate it on the next visit.
   Else if "Remember me" is unchecked, Then the system must clear any previously stored email.

4. If the account has been locked due to multiple failed attempts, Then the system must display "Your account has been temporarily locked. Please try again after 15 minutes or reset your password".

5. If the account has not completed initial password setup, Then the system must display "Please complete your account setup using the invitation link sent to your email".
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that entering valid email "johndoe@example.com" and password "MySecure@Pass1" and clicking "Continue" redirects to the Enter OTP screen.
2. Verify that checking "Remember me" and logging in causes the Email field to be pre-populated with "johndoe@example.com" on the next visit.
3. Verify that the eye icon on the Password field toggles the password visibility between masked and plain text on each click.
4. Verify that clicking "Forgot Password?" navigates to the Forgot Password screen.

Negative:
5. Verify that entering email "wrong@example.com" (non-existent) with any password displays "Invalid email or password".
6. Verify that entering valid email "johndoe@example.com" with incorrect password "WrongPass123" displays "Invalid email or password".
7. Verify that clicking "Continue" with an empty Email field displays "Email is required".
8. Verify that clicking "Continue" with an empty Password field displays "Password is required".
9. Verify that entering "johndoe" (no @ or domain) in the Email field displays "Please enter a valid email address".
10. Verify that after 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the system displays "Your account has been temporarily locked" message.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the Sign In screen displays a split layout with a care photo on the left and the login form on the right.
12. Verify that the Nemi Care logo is displayed at the top of the form section.
13. Verify that the Email field is marked with an asterisk (*) indicating it is required.
14. Verify that the Password field is marked with an asterisk (*) and includes an eye icon on the right side.
15. Verify that the "Continue" button is styled as a full-width blue primary button.
16. Verify that the HIPAA compliance badge is displayed in the footer area below the form.

Edge Cases:
17. Verify that entering an email with leading/trailing spaces "  johndoe@example.com  " is trimmed and accepted as valid.
18. Verify that a contact who has not completed password setup sees a message directing them to use the invitation link.
19. Verify that pressing Enter on the keyboard while focused on the Password field submits the form just like clicking "Continue".

Security:
20. Verify that the password is never displayed in the browser URL or query parameters.
21. Verify that login requests are sent over HTTPS with the password in the request body, not as URL parameters.
22. Verify that after successful login and OTP verification, the session cookie is set with Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite attributes.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Sign In page loads completely (photo, logo, form fields) within 2 seconds.
Verify that credential validation and redirect to the OTP screen completes within 2 seconds of clicking "Continue".
Verify that the "Remember me" pre-population of the Email field occurs instantly on page load without visible delay.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to reset the password if I forgot the password.
Description of User Story: The Forgot Password flow allows an Approved Family Contact who cannot remember their password to securely reset it via email verification. The flow begins from the "Forgot Password?" link on the Sign In screen, which navigates to a dedicated Forgot Password screen. This screen uses the same split layout (care photo left, form right) and displays the heading "Forgot Password?" with instructional text "Enter your mail ID to receive OTP". The contact enters their registered email address in the Email field (required) and clicks the blue "Continue" button. The system sends a six-digit OTP to the registered email. The contact enters the OTP on the verification screen, and upon successful verification, is directed to the Set Your Password screen where they create a new password with the same complexity requirements as initial setup. After the password is reset successfully, the contact is redirected to the Sign In screen to log in with the new credentials.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the Approved Family Contact enters a registered email on the Forgot Password screen and clicks "Continue", Then the system must send a six-digit OTP to that email and navigate to the OTP entry screen.
   Else if the email is not registered in the system, Then the system must display "If this email is registered, you will receive an OTP shortly" to avoid confirming whether the email exists.

2. If the contact enters the correct OTP within the validity period, Then the system must redirect to the Set Your Password screen.
   Else if the OTP is incorrect, Then the system must display "Invalid OTP. Please try again".
   Else if the OTP has expired, Then the system must display "OTP expired" and offer a "Resend OTP" option.

3. If the contact enters a new password meeting all complexity requirements and the confirmation matches, Then the system must reset the password and redirect to the Sign In screen with a "Password reset successfully" confirmation message.
   Else if the new password is the same as the current password, Then the system must display "New password must be different from the current password".

4. If the contact attempts more than 5 OTP verification failures in the forgot password flow, Then the system must lock the reset attempt for 15 minutes.

5. If the Email field is empty when "Continue" is clicked, Then the system must display "Email is required" validation error.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Forgot Password?" on the Sign In screen navigates to the Forgot Password screen with heading "Forgot Password?" and text "Enter your mail ID to receive OTP".
2. Verify that entering registered email "johndoe@example.com" and clicking "Continue" sends a six-digit OTP to the email and shows the OTP verification screen.
3. Verify that entering the correct OTP redirects to the Set Your Password screen where the contact can create a new password.
4. Verify that after setting a new password "NewSecure@Pass2", the contact is redirected to the Sign In screen and can log in with the new password.
5. Verify that clicking "Resend OTP" on the verification screen sends a new OTP and invalidates the previous one.

Negative:
6. Verify that entering an unregistered email "unknown@example.com" displays "If this email is registered, you will receive an OTP shortly" without confirming email existence.
7. Verify that entering an incorrect OTP "999999" displays "Invalid OTP. Please try again".
8. Verify that entering an expired OTP (after 5 minutes) displays "OTP expired" with a "Resend OTP" option.
9. Verify that setting a new password identical to the current password displays "New password must be different from the current password".
10. Verify that clicking "Continue" with an empty Email field displays "Email is required".

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the Forgot Password screen uses the same split layout (care photo left, form right) as the Sign In screen.
12. Verify that the Email field is marked with an asterisk (*) and the "Continue" button is styled as a full-width blue primary button.
13. Verify that the OTP verification screen displays six individual digit input boxes consistent with the login OTP screen design.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that requesting a password reset, not completing it, and requesting again generates a new OTP and invalidates the first.
15. Verify that completing the password reset flow in under 30 seconds from start to finish works without any timeout errors.
16. Verify that navigating back from the OTP screen to the Forgot Password screen and re-entering the email sends a fresh OTP.

Security:
17. Verify that the Forgot Password flow does not reveal whether an email address is registered in the system.
18. Verify that OTP codes for password reset expire after 5 minutes and cannot be reused.
19. Verify that after 5 consecutive incorrect OTP attempts in the reset flow, the system locks the reset process for 15 minutes.
20. Verify that the password reset flow is transmitted entirely over HTTPS.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Forgot Password screen loads within 2 seconds of clicking the "Forgot Password?" link.
Verify that OTP delivery to the registered email completes within 5 seconds of clicking "Continue".
Verify that password reset confirmation and redirect to the Sign In screen completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, after entering my credentials I should receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on my registered email and should be able to resend otp.
Description of User Story: The OTP verification step is the second factor of authentication in the Nemicare Family Portal login flow. After the Approved Family Contact enters valid credentials on the Sign In screen and clicks "Continue", the system generates a six-digit numeric OTP and sends it to the contact's registered email address. The Enter OTP screen uses the same split layout (care photo left, form right) and displays the heading "Enter OTP" with a message "OTP sent to johndoe@example.com" (showing the masked or full email). Six individual digit input boxes are presented for OTP entry, with auto-focus advancing to the next box after each digit is entered. A "Didn't receive the code? Resend OTP" link below the input boxes allows the contact to request a new OTP, which invalidates the previous code. A blue "Verify & Continue" button submits the OTP for verification. The OTP expires after a configurable period (e.g., 5 minutes). Successful verification completes the login and grants access to the portal dashboard.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the contact submits valid credentials, Then the system must generate a six-digit numeric OTP, send it to the registered email, and display the Enter OTP screen.

2. If the contact enters the correct OTP within the validity period and clicks "Verify & Continue", Then the system must authenticate the session and redirect to the portal dashboard.
   Else if the OTP is incorrect, Then the system must display "Invalid OTP. Please check and try again" and allow re-entry.
   Else if the OTP has expired (after 5 minutes), Then the system must display "OTP has expired. Please click Resend OTP to receive a new code".

3. If the contact clicks "Resend OTP", Then the system must generate and send a new OTP, invalidate the previous OTP, and display a confirmation "A new OTP has been sent to your email".
   Else if the contact clicks "Resend OTP" more than 3 times within 10 minutes, Then the system must temporarily disable the resend option and display "Too many resend attempts. Please try again in 10 minutes".

4. If all six digit boxes are filled, Then the "Verify & Continue" button must become enabled.
   Else if fewer than six digits are entered, Then the "Verify & Continue" button must remain disabled.

5. If the contact navigates away from the OTP screen and returns, Then the system must clear the previously entered digits and require fresh OTP entry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that after entering valid credentials and clicking "Continue", the Enter OTP screen displays with heading "Enter OTP" and message "OTP sent to johndoe@example.com".
2. Verify that entering the correct six-digit OTP "482915" and clicking "Verify & Continue" authenticates the user and redirects to the portal dashboard.
3. Verify that clicking "Resend OTP" sends a new OTP to the registered email and displays a "New OTP has been sent" confirmation.
4. Verify that entering digits in the OTP boxes causes auto-focus to advance from box 1 to box 6 sequentially.
5. Verify that after resending OTP, the previously sent OTP becomes invalid and only the new one works.

Negative:
6. Verify that entering an incorrect OTP "000000" and clicking "Verify & Continue" displays "Invalid OTP. Please check and try again".
7. Verify that waiting 5 minutes after receiving the OTP and then entering it displays "OTP has expired. Please click Resend OTP to receive a new code".
8. Verify that clicking "Resend OTP" more than 3 times within 10 minutes disables the resend link with a message "Too many resend attempts. Please try again in 10 minutes".
9. Verify that clicking "Verify & Continue" with only 4 of 6 digits entered keeps the button disabled and does not submit.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Enter OTP screen uses the same split layout (care photo left, form right) as the Sign In screen.
11. Verify that the six OTP input boxes are displayed as individual square fields in a horizontal row with equal spacing.
12. Verify that the "Didn't receive the code? Resend OTP" text is displayed below the input boxes as a clickable link.
13. Verify that the "Verify & Continue" button is styled as a full-width blue primary button matching the portal design.
14. Verify that the "Verify & Continue" button is visually disabled (greyed out) when fewer than 6 digits are entered.

Edge Cases:
15. Verify that pasting a six-digit OTP "482915" from the clipboard fills all six boxes correctly.
16. Verify that pressing Backspace on an OTP box moves focus to the previous box and clears the digit.
17. Verify that closing the browser after the OTP screen loads and reopening the login flow requires full re-authentication starting from the Sign In screen.
18. Verify that receiving two OTPs (original and resent) and using the original (now invalidated) fails verification.

Security:
19. Verify that OTP values are not exposed in the page source, browser console, or network response payloads.
20. Verify that each OTP is single-use and cannot be reused after successful verification.
21. Verify that the OTP generation uses a cryptographically secure random number generator.
22. Verify that after 5 consecutive failed OTP verification attempts, the session is terminated and the contact must re-enter credentials.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Enter OTP screen loads within 1 second after credential validation completes.
Verify that the OTP email is delivered to the inbox within 5 seconds of the screen loading.
Verify that OTP verification and dashboard redirect completes within 2 seconds of clicking "Verify & Continue".
Verify that clicking "Resend OTP" triggers delivery of the new OTP within 5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, during first-time setup I should be able to fill in my basic information personal Info & Address Info.
Description of User Story: The Complete Your Profile onboarding flow is a two-step form presented to Approved Family Contacts during first-time login after setting their password. Step 1 collects personal information with the heading "Complete Your Profile" and subtext "Fill in your basic information to continue". The fields include First Name (required), Last Name (required), Email (required, pre-populated from registration), Mobile No (required, with format hint), and Gender (required, dropdown selection). A blue "Next" button with a right arrow advances to Step 2. Step 2 collects address information with fields for Address Line 1 (required), Address Line 2 (optional), City (required, dropdown selection), and Zip Code (required). A "Back" link with a left arrow returns to Step 1, and a blue "Continue" button submits the complete profile. Both steps display progress indication so the contact knows where they are in the onboarding flow. The submitted information is stored in the contact's profile and can be edited later from account settings.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If all required fields on Step 1 (First Name, Last Name, Email, Mobile No, Gender) are filled with valid data, Then the system must allow advancement to Step 2.
   Else if any required field on Step 1 is empty, Then the system must highlight the empty fields and display "This field is required" for each.
   Else if the Mobile No format is invalid (e.g., contains letters or wrong digit count), Then the system must display "Please enter a valid mobile number".

2. If the Email field is pre-populated from registration, Then the field must be read-only and not editable during onboarding.
   Else if the Email field is editable and the contact changes it, Then the system must validate the new email format.

3. If all required fields on Step 2 (Address Line 1, City, Zip Code) are filled with valid data, Then the system must save the profile and proceed to the next onboarding step.
   Else if Address Line 1 is empty, Then the system must display "Address Line 1 is required".
   Else if the Zip Code format is invalid (e.g., not 5 digits), Then the system must display "Please enter a valid Zip Code".

4. If the contact clicks "Back" on Step 2, Then the system must return to Step 1 with all previously entered data intact.

5. If the contact closes the browser before completing the profile, Then the system must present the profile setup again on next login, pre-populated with any previously saved partial data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that Step 1 displays fields for First Name, Last Name, Email (pre-populated with "johndoe@example.com"), Mobile No, and Gender dropdown with a blue "Next" button.
2. Verify that entering First Name "John", Last Name "Doe", Mobile No "555-123-4567", selecting Gender "Male", and clicking "Next" advances to Step 2.
3. Verify that Step 2 displays fields for Address Line 1, Address Line 2, City dropdown, and Zip Code with a "Back" link and blue "Continue" button.
4. Verify that entering Address Line 1 "123 Main Street", selecting City "Miami", entering Zip Code "33101", and clicking "Continue" saves the profile and proceeds to the resident selection step.
5. Verify that clicking "Back" on Step 2 returns to Step 1 with "John", "Doe", "555-123-4567", and "Male" still populated.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking "Next" on Step 1 with First Name empty displays "This field is required" next to the First Name field.
7. Verify that entering Mobile No "555-ABC-4567" displays "Please enter a valid mobile number".
8. Verify that clicking "Continue" on Step 2 with Address Line 1 empty displays "Address Line 1 is required".
9. Verify that entering Zip Code "123" (less than 5 digits) displays "Please enter a valid Zip Code".
10. Verify that clicking "Next" on Step 1 without selecting a Gender displays "This field is required" for the Gender dropdown.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the "Complete Your Profile" heading and subtext "Fill in your basic information to continue" are displayed at the top of Step 1.
12. Verify that a step indicator (e.g., Step 1 of 2, Step 2 of 2) or progress bar is displayed on both steps.
13. Verify that the "Next" button displays a right arrow icon and the "Back" link displays a left arrow icon.
14. Verify that the Gender field renders as a dropdown with options including "Male", "Female", and "Other".
15. Verify that the City field renders as a searchable dropdown for easy selection.

Edge Cases:
16. Verify that entering a First Name with 50 characters is accepted and displayed correctly without truncation.
17. Verify that the Address Line 2 field can be left empty and the form still submits successfully.
18. Verify that closing the browser on Step 2 and logging back in resumes the onboarding at Step 1 with partial data pre-populated if previously saved.

Security:
19. Verify that profile data submission is sent over HTTPS and personal information is encrypted in transit.
20. Verify that the Email field is read-only during onboarding and cannot be modified via browser developer tools or API manipulation.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that Step 1 of the Complete Your Profile form loads within 1.5 seconds with all fields rendered.
Verify that transitioning from Step 1 to Step 2 completes within 1 second after clicking "Next".
Verify that the City dropdown loads all available options within 1 second when opened.
Verify that profile submission on Step 2 and progression to the next onboarding step completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, after completing profile setup I should be able to select which linked resident (family member) I want to view, seeing their name, room number, and program type (ADH/ALF), so that I can navigate to the correct resident's dashboard.
Description of User Story: The Select a Resident screen is presented as part of the onboarding flow after the Approved Family Contact completes their profile setup. The screen displays the heading "Select a Resident" with subtext "Choose which family member you'd like to view". Each linked resident is displayed as a card containing the resident's profile photo, full name, a room icon with room number, a program type badge (ADH or ALF), and a "View" button. For example, one card shows "Devon Michael Lane" with "Room 204" and an "ADH" badge, while another shows "Linda Michael Lane" with "Room 201" and an "ALF" badge. Clicking the "View" button on a resident card navigates the contact to that resident's dashboard, setting the selected resident as the active context for all portal sections. If only one resident is linked, the system may auto-select that resident or still present the screen for confirmation. The resident selection is remembered for subsequent logins.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the Approved Family Contact is linked to multiple residents, Then the Select a Resident screen must display a card for each linked resident with their photo, name, room number, program type badge, and a "View" button.
   Else if the contact is linked to only one resident, Then the system must either auto-navigate to that resident's dashboard or display the single resident card for confirmation.

2. If the contact clicks the "View" button on a resident card, Then the system must set that resident as the active context and navigate to their dashboard.

3. If a linked resident has no profile photo on file, Then the card must display a default avatar with the resident's initials.
   Else if a linked resident's room number is not assigned, Then the room field must display "Room TBD".

4. If the contact has previously selected a resident during onboarding, Then subsequent logins must default to the last-viewed resident's dashboard.
   Else if the contact has not made a selection yet, Then the system must present the Select a Resident screen.

5. If a linked resident has been discharged since the last login, Then the system must remove that resident's card and display a notification explaining the change.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that after completing profile setup, the Select a Resident screen displays with heading "Select a Resident" and subtext "Choose which family member you'd like to view".
2. Verify that Devon Michael Lane's card displays a profile photo, name "Devon Michael Lane", room icon with "Room 204", an "ADH" badge, and a "View" button.
3. Verify that Linda Michael Lane's card displays a profile photo, name "Linda Michael Lane", room icon with "Room 201", an "ALF" badge, and a "View" button.
4. Verify that clicking "View" on Devon Michael Lane's card navigates to Devon's dashboard showing his care data.
5. Verify that on subsequent login, the system defaults to the last-selected resident's dashboard without showing the selection screen.

Negative:
6. Verify that if a linked resident "Linda Michael Lane" is discharged, her card is removed from the Select a Resident screen and a notification "Linda Michael Lane is no longer an active resident" is displayed.
7. Verify that if all linked residents are inactive, the screen displays "No active residents are linked to your account. Please contact the facility for assistance".
8. Verify that attempting to access a discharged resident's dashboard via URL manipulation returns an error message.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that each resident card includes a profile photo (or default avatar with initials), name in bold, a room icon next to the room number, and a colored badge for program type (blue for ADH, green for ALF).
10. Verify that the "View" button on each card is styled as a clickable button with clear hover state.
11. Verify that the resident cards are displayed as a horizontal row on desktop and stack vertically on mobile screens.
12. Verify that the Select a Resident screen maintains the portal's consistent layout and branding.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a contact linked to only one resident either sees that single card for confirmation or is auto-redirected to their dashboard.
14. Verify that a resident card with no profile photo displays a default avatar showing initials "DM" for Devon Michael Lane.
15. Verify that a resident with no room assignment displays "Room TBD" on the card instead of a blank field.
16. Verify that a contact linked to 10 residents can scroll through all cards without layout issues.

Security:
17. Verify that the Select a Resident screen only displays residents that are officially linked to the authenticated contact's account.
18. Verify that clicking "View" on a resident card does not expose other residents' data in the network response.
19. Verify that the resident selection API endpoint validates that the contact has an active link to the requested resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Select a Resident screen loads all resident cards with photos within 2 seconds of completing profile setup.
Verify that clicking the "View" button navigates to the resident's dashboard within 2 seconds.
Verify that resident cards with default avatars render within 500 milliseconds when profile photos are unavailable.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should see a welcome/getting started screen after first login that introduces the portal features and guides me through initial setup.
Description of User Story: The Welcome/Getting Started screen is displayed to Approved Family Contacts upon their first login to the Nemicare Family Portal, after completing password setup and before or after the profile completion steps. The screen serves as an orientation guide, introducing the portal's key features and guiding the contact through the initial setup process. The screen displays a welcome heading (e.g., "Welcome to Nemicare Family Portal"), a brief introductory message, and a series of feature highlights presented as cards or tiles with icons and short descriptions. Feature highlights include: viewing care information and vitals, checking medications and schedules, viewing billing information, communicating with facility staff, and viewing appointments and activities. A step-by-step setup guide or progress tracker shows the onboarding steps (e.g., Set Password, Complete Profile, Select Resident) with the current step highlighted. A prominent "Get Started" or "Continue" button advances the contact to the next onboarding step. The welcome screen is shown only once during the first login and is not repeated on subsequent logins, though a link to revisit the guide may be available in the help or settings section.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the Approved Family Contact is logging in for the first time and has not seen the welcome screen, Then the system must display the Welcome/Getting Started screen with feature highlights and the onboarding progress tracker.
   Else if the contact has previously completed the welcome screen, Then the system must skip it and proceed to the dashboard or the next incomplete onboarding step.

2. If the contact clicks "Get Started" or "Continue", Then the system must mark the welcome screen as viewed and advance to the next onboarding step (Complete Your Profile).
   Else if the contact closes the browser before clicking "Continue", Then the system must show the welcome screen again on the next login.

3. If the onboarding progress tracker shows the "Set Password" step as completed, Then it must be displayed with a checkmark or completed state.
   Else if a step is upcoming, Then it must be displayed in a greyed-out or pending state.

4. If the contact has completed all onboarding steps and revisits the portal guide via help/settings, Then the system must display the feature highlights in a read-only informational mode without the onboarding progress tracker.

5. If the portal adds new features after the contact's initial onboarding, Then the system must optionally display a "What's New" notification on the next login rather than re-showing the full welcome screen.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that on first login, the Welcome screen displays with heading "Welcome to Nemicare Family Portal" and an introductory message.
2. Verify that feature highlight cards are shown for care information, medications, billing, communication with staff, and appointments.
3. Verify that the onboarding progress tracker shows "Set Password" as completed (checkmark), "Complete Profile" as the current step (highlighted), and "Select Resident" as upcoming (greyed out).
4. Verify that clicking "Get Started" advances to the Complete Your Profile step.
5. Verify that on the second login after completing onboarding, the welcome screen is not shown and the contact goes directly to the dashboard.

Negative:
6. Verify that if the contact closes the browser before clicking "Get Started", the welcome screen reappears on the next login.
7. Verify that the welcome screen cannot be skipped via URL manipulation — navigating directly to the dashboard URL redirects back to the welcome screen if onboarding is incomplete.
8. Verify that feature highlight content does not contain clickable links to portal sections that require onboarding completion.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that each feature highlight card displays an icon (e.g., heart icon for vitals, pill icon for medications, dollar icon for billing), a title, and a short 1-2 sentence description.
10. Verify that the onboarding progress tracker is displayed as a horizontal stepper with numbered steps and connecting lines, with completed steps showing checkmarks.
11. Verify that the "Get Started" button is styled as a prominent blue primary button centered below the feature highlights.
12. Verify that the welcome screen is responsive and displays feature cards in a grid on desktop and stacked vertically on mobile.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a contact who completes password setup but abandons onboarding sees the welcome screen on the next login with the progress tracker reflecting the correct current step.
14. Verify that revisiting the portal guide from help/settings displays feature highlights without the progress tracker or "Get Started" button.
15. Verify that the welcome screen renders correctly when the browser's zoom level is set to 150%.

Security:
16. Verify that the welcome screen content is served over HTTPS and does not expose any PHI or resident-specific information.
17. Verify that unauthenticated users cannot access the welcome screen URL and are redirected to the Sign In page.
18. Verify that the onboarding completion status cannot be manipulated via API calls to skip required setup steps.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Welcome/Getting Started screen loads within 2 seconds including all feature highlight cards and icons.
Verify that the onboarding progress tracker renders with the correct step states within 1 second.
Verify that clicking "Get Started" transitions to the next onboarding step within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view a dashboard summary showing Family Members count, Active Alerts count, Open Tickets count, and Next Visit date at the top of the dashboard, so that I get a quick overview of all important information.
Description of User Story: The Dashboard Summary section presents four prominent metric cards arranged in a horizontal row at the top of the Family Portal dashboard. Each card displays a distinct icon and a large numeric or date value: Family Members shows the count of linked residents, Active Alerts shows the number of unresolved alerts, Open Tickets shows the count of support tickets currently open, and Next Visit shows the date of the upcoming scheduled visit. The cards are color-coded for quick visual differentiation — for example, a red accent for alerts and a blue accent for tickets. Values are fetched from the backend upon page load and reflect real-time data. Clicking on a summary card navigates the user to the corresponding detail section (e.g., clicking Active Alerts navigates to the Alerts list). On mobile viewports the cards stack vertically in a two-by-two grid to maintain readability.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the dashboard, Then the system must display four summary cards: Family Members, Active Alerts, Open Tickets, and Next Visit.

2. If the Family Contact has no linked residents, Then the Family Members card must display "0".
   Else if the Family Contact has multiple linked residents, Then the card must display the total count of all linked residents.

3. If there are no active alerts for any linked resident, Then the Active Alerts card must display "0" with no error.
   Else if alerts exist but the alerts service is temporarily unavailable, Then the card must display a "Data unavailable" message instead of stale data.

4. If the Family Contact has no open support tickets, Then the Open Tickets card must display "0".

5. If no upcoming visit is scheduled, Then the Next Visit card must display "No upcoming visit" instead of a blank value.
   Else if the next visit date has passed without being marked complete, Then the card must display the overdue visit date with a visual overdue indicator.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that logging in as an Approved Family Contact with 2 linked residents, 3 active alerts, 1 open ticket, and a visit on 2026-04-10 displays "2", "3", "1", and "Apr 10, 2026" respectively on the four summary cards.
2. Verify that creating a new support ticket and refreshing the dashboard increments the Open Tickets card count by one.
3. Verify that resolving an alert reduces the Active Alerts card count by one on the next dashboard load.
4. Verify that clicking the Family Members card navigates to the family members detail section.

Negative:
5. Verify that when the alerts API is temporarily unavailable, the Active Alerts card displays "Data unavailable" rather than showing an incorrect count.
6. Verify that a Family Contact with no linked residents, no alerts, no tickets, and no scheduled visits sees "0", "0", "0", and "No upcoming visit" without errors.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that the four summary cards are arranged in a single horizontal row on desktop, each with a distinct color accent, icon, label, and large value.
8. Verify that on a mobile viewport (375px width) the summary cards stack into a responsive two-by-two grid without overlapping.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify that unlinking a resident while the dashboard is open causes the Family Members count to update correctly on the next refresh.
10. Verify that when a scheduled visit is cancelled, the Next Visit card updates to show the next available visit or "No upcoming visit".

Security:
11. Verify that the dashboard summary API endpoints return data only for residents linked to the authenticated Family Contact and reject requests for unlinked resident data.
12. Verify that unauthenticated requests to the dashboard summary API are rejected with a 401 status code.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that all four dashboard summary cards load and display their values within 2 seconds of navigating to the dashboard.
Verify that refreshing the dashboard retrieves updated summary values and re-renders all four cards within 1.5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view my loved one's photo, name, program type (ADH/ALF), relationship, facility name, room number, and Comfortability score on the dashboard, so that I can see their basic information at a glance.
Description of User Story: The Resident Info Card is a prominent dashboard component that displays essential identifying information about the currently selected linked resident. It features a circular profile photo thumbnail on the left, followed by the resident's full name displayed in a larger font, and below it a series of labeled detail fields: Program Type showing "ADH" (Adult Day Health) or "ALF" (Assisted Living Facility), Relationship indicating the family contact's relationship to the resident (e.g., Daughter, Son, Spouse), Facility Name showing which facility the resident is enrolled at, Room Number for ALF residents showing their assigned room, and Comfortability Score displayed as a numeric value or visual gauge indicating the resident's overall comfort level as assessed by staff. The card uses a clean layout with subtle borders and is positioned prominently below the summary cards. If the resident has no uploaded photo, a default avatar placeholder is shown.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the dashboard loads for the selected resident, Then the system must display the Resident Info Card with photo, name, program type, relationship, facility name, room number, and Comfortability score.

2. If the resident has an uploaded profile photo, Then the card must display the photo as a circular thumbnail.
   Else if no photo is uploaded, Then the card must display a default avatar placeholder.

3. If the resident is enrolled in ADH, Then the program type must display "ADH".
   Else if the resident is enrolled in ALF, Then the program type must display "ALF".

4. If the resident is in an ADH program with no assigned room, Then the room number field must display "N/A" or be hidden.
   Else if the room number data is unavailable, Then the field must display "Not Assigned".

5. If the Comfortability score has not been assessed yet, Then the score must display "Not Assessed" instead of a blank or zero value.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Resident Info Card for "Devon Lane" displays his photo, name "Devon Lane", program type "ADH", relationship "Son", facility name "Sunrise Adult Day Center", room number "N/A", and Comfortability score "8.2".
2. Verify that switching to resident "Linda Lane" updates the card to show her photo, name, program type "ALF", relationship "Daughter", facility name "Sunrise ALF", room number "112", and Comfortability score "7.5".
3. Verify that the Comfortability score displays as a visual gauge or numeric badge matching the backend value.

Negative:
4. Verify that a resident with no uploaded photo shows a default avatar placeholder without layout breaking.
5. Verify that when the resident profile API returns an error, the card displays a "Unable to load resident info" message with a retry option.
6. Verify that a resident with missing Comfortability score data shows "Not Assessed" instead of "0" or a blank field.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that the Resident Info Card displays a circular photo on the left with the name in bold large font and detail fields arranged in a structured layout with labels and values.
8. Verify that the card is fully responsive and readable on a mobile viewport without overlapping elements.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify that a resident whose program type changes from ADH to ALF reflects the updated program type on the next dashboard load.
10. Verify that a resident name containing special characters (e.g., "O'Brien-Smith") displays correctly without encoding issues.

Security:
11. Verify that the Resident Info Card API returns data only for residents linked to the authenticated Family Contact.
12. Verify that the resident photo URL is served over HTTPS and is not publicly accessible without authentication.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Resident Info Card including the profile photo loads within 2 seconds of the dashboard rendering.
Verify that switching between residents updates the Resident Info Card within 1.5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view an Overall Progress bar (e.g., 'Improving') for my loved one on the dashboard, so that I can track their general health trajectory.
Description of User Story: The Overall Progress indicator is a dashboard widget that provides a high-level summary of the resident's health trajectory over a defined period. It consists of a horizontal progress bar with a colored fill representing the current progress level and a textual label such as "Improving", "Stable", or "Declining" displayed alongside or within the bar. The progress level is determined by clinical assessments and care metrics aggregated by the backend. The color of the bar adjusts based on the status: green for "Improving", yellow or amber for "Stable", and red for "Declining". A tooltip or info icon provides a brief explanation of what the progress status is based on. The widget is positioned on the dashboard near the Resident Info Card for contextual viewing.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the dashboard loads, Then the system must display the Overall Progress bar with the current health trajectory label and corresponding color.

2. If the resident's aggregated clinical metrics indicate improvement, Then the progress bar must display a green fill with the label "Improving".
   Else if the metrics indicate no significant change, Then the bar must display a yellow fill with the label "Stable".
   Else if the metrics indicate a decline, Then the bar must display a red fill with the label "Declining".

3. If no clinical assessment data is available for the resident, Then the progress bar must display a gray fill with the label "No Data Available".

4. If the progress status changes between dashboard visits, Then the bar must reflect the updated status on the next load without caching the previous value.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Overall Progress bar for resident "Devon Lane" displays a green bar with the label "Improving" when clinical data indicates positive trends.
2. Verify that switching to resident "Linda Lane" with stable metrics updates the bar to yellow with the label "Stable".
3. Verify that hovering over the info icon displays a tooltip explaining the progress assessment criteria.

Negative:
4. Verify that when no clinical data exists for a newly admitted resident, the progress bar shows a gray fill with "No Data Available".
5. Verify that if the progress data API fails, the widget displays "Unable to load progress" with a retry link.

UI Scenarios:
6. Verify that the progress bar is horizontally oriented with a clear fill color, a percentage or status label inside or beside the bar, and rounded edges.
7. Verify that the progress bar is responsive and maintains proportional sizing on mobile viewports.

Edge Cases:
8. Verify that a resident whose status transitions from "Declining" to "Improving" between two dashboard visits shows the updated green bar and "Improving" label.
9. Verify that simultaneous progress updates from multiple clinical assessments result in a single consistent progress status on the dashboard.

Security:
10. Verify that the progress data API returns information only for residents linked to the authenticated Family Contact.
11. Verify that progress health data is transmitted over HTTPS and is not exposed in browser local storage in plain text.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Overall Progress bar renders with the correct color and label within 2 seconds of the dashboard loading.
Verify that the tooltip information appears within 500 milliseconds of hovering over the info icon.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view my loved one's current Pain Scale rating on the dashboard, so that I am aware of their comfort level.
Description of User Story: The Pain Scale widget on the dashboard displays the resident's most recent pain assessment rating, providing the family contact with visibility into their loved one's current comfort level. The widget shows a numeric pain score (typically on a 0-10 scale) along with a visual representation such as a color-coded bar, face icon scale, or gauge. A descriptive label accompanies the score, such as "No Pain" (0), "Mild" (1-3), "Moderate" (4-6), or "Severe" (7-10). The date and time of the most recent assessment are displayed below the score to indicate recency. The color scheme transitions from green (no pain) through yellow (mild), orange (moderate), to red (severe) for immediate visual comprehension. If no pain assessment has been recorded, the widget displays an appropriate placeholder message.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the dashboard loads, Then the system must display the Pain Scale widget with the most recent pain score, visual indicator, and assessment timestamp.

2. If the pain score is between 0 and 0, Then the widget must display a green indicator with the label "No Pain".
   Else if the score is between 1 and 3, Then the widget must display a green-to-yellow indicator with the label "Mild".
