The assigned provider section displays the provider's name alongside their profile avatar photo. For virtual appointments, a clickable telehealth link is displayed in place of or in addition to the address. The card footer contains action buttons for "Cancel Meet" and "Reschedule" for upcoming appointments. All information is fetched from the appointment record and displayed in a consistent, accessible card format.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When an appointment card renders, Then the system must display the date and time range in the card header section.

2. If the appointment program type is ADH, Then the system must display an "ADH" badge or label on the card.
   Else if the appointment program type is ALF, Then the system must display an "ALF" badge or label on the card.

3. If the appointment is in-person, Then the system must display the facility's full street address on the card.
   Else if the appointment is virtual, Then the system must display a clickable telehealth link on the card.
   Else if the appointment is virtual with an associated facility, Then the system must display both the telehealth link and the facility name.

4. If the assigned provider has a profile photo uploaded, Then the system must display their avatar alongside their name.
   Else if the assigned provider has no profile photo, Then the system must display a default avatar placeholder alongside their name.

5. If the appointment is upcoming, Then the card must display "Cancel Meet" and "Reschedule" action buttons.
   Else if the appointment is past, Then the card must not display action buttons for cancellation or rescheduling.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the appointment card displays date "29 Mar 2026" and time range "09:00 AM - 09:30 AM" in the header section.
2. Verify that the card displays resident name "Jane Doe", program type badge "ADH", and facility address "123 Care Lane, Suite 200, Springfield, IL 62701".
3. Verify that the assigned provider "Dr. Williams" is displayed with their profile avatar photo on the appointment card.
4. Verify that a virtual appointment card displays a clickable telehealth link labeled appropriately for joining the session.

Negative:
5. Verify that an appointment with no assigned provider displays "Provider TBD" with a default avatar placeholder.
6. Verify that an appointment with missing address data displays a fallback message such as "Address not available" rather than a blank space.
7. Verify that an appointment card with an expired telehealth link displays the link as inactive or with a note that the session has ended.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the appointment card layout displays date and time in the header, resident name and program badge in the body, address or telehealth link in the location section, and provider with avatar in the provider section.
9. Verify that the program type badge uses distinct colors or styles for ADH versus ALF to enable quick visual identification.
10. Verify that the provider avatar is displayed as a circular thumbnail next to the provider name with consistent sizing across all cards.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that an appointment card with an extremely long facility address wraps the text gracefully without breaking the card layout.
12. Verify that an appointment card for a resident with a very long name truncates or wraps the name without overlapping other card elements.

Security:
13. Verify that appointment card details including resident names and telehealth links are visible only to the authenticated Approved Family Contact; no sensitive data is exposed in the page source to unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that each appointment card renders with all details (including provider avatar image) within 1 second of the list loading.
Verify that a page displaying 20 appointment cards loads all card content within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to cancel an upcoming appointment using the 'Cancel Meet' button on the appointment card, so that I can manage my schedule changes.
Description of User Story: The Cancel Appointment feature allows an Approved Family Contact to cancel an upcoming appointment directly from the appointment card on the Appointments page. Each upcoming appointment card displays a "Cancel Meet" button. When clicked, the system presents a confirmation dialog asking the family contact to confirm the cancellation, optionally with a reason selection or text field. Upon confirmation, the system updates the appointment status to cancelled, removes it from the Upcoming list (or marks it as cancelled), and sends a cancellation notification to the facility staff and assigned provider. The cancelled appointment may still appear in the Past tab with a "Cancelled" status badge for historical reference.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact clicks the "Cancel Meet" button on an upcoming appointment card, Then the system must display a confirmation dialog before processing the cancellation.

2. If the family contact confirms the cancellation, Then the system must update the appointment status to "Cancelled" and remove it from the Upcoming list.
   Else if the family contact dismisses the confirmation dialog, Then the system must keep the appointment unchanged.

3. If the cancellation is processed successfully, Then the system must send a notification to facility staff and the assigned provider.
   Else if the cancellation fails due to a server error, Then the system must display an error message and keep the appointment in its current state.

4. If the appointment is within a configurable cancellation window (e.g., more than 2 hours before the appointment), Then the system must allow the cancellation.
   Else if the appointment is within the restricted cancellation window (e.g., less than 2 hours before), Then the system must display a warning that late cancellation policies may apply and still allow the cancellation with acknowledgment.

5. If a cancellation reason is provided, Then the system must store the reason with the cancellation record.
   Else if no reason is provided, Then the system must still process the cancellation but log it without a reason.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the family contact clicks "Cancel Meet" on an upcoming appointment, confirms in the dialog, and sees a success message with the appointment removed from the Upcoming list.
2. Verify that the cancelled appointment appears in the Past tab with a "Cancelled" status badge for historical reference.
3. Verify that facility staff and the assigned provider receive a cancellation notification when the appointment is cancelled.
4. Verify that the family contact provides a cancellation reason in the dialog and the reason is stored with the cancellation record.

Negative:
5. Verify that the family contact clicks "Cancel Meet" and then dismisses the confirmation dialog; the appointment remains unchanged in the Upcoming list.
6. Verify that the cancellation request fails due to a server error; system displays an error message and the appointment remains active.
7. Verify that the "Cancel Meet" button is not displayed on past appointment cards.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the "Cancel Meet" button is visible on each upcoming appointment card and styled as a secondary or destructive action button.
9. Verify that the confirmation dialog displays the appointment details (date, time, provider) and asks "Are you sure you want to cancel this appointment?" with Confirm and Cancel buttons.
10. Verify that the success message is displayed as a toast or inline notification after successful cancellation.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that cancelling an appointment that is less than 2 hours away displays a late cancellation warning and still allows the family contact to proceed.
12. Verify that two family contacts attempting to cancel the same appointment simultaneously results in one successful cancellation and the other receiving a notification that the appointment is already cancelled.

Security:
13. Verify that only the Approved Family Contact associated with the appointment can cancel it; attempts to cancel another contact's appointment are denied.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the cancellation confirmation dialog opens within 1 second of clicking the "Cancel Meet" button.
Verify that the cancellation is processed and the Upcoming list is updated within 2 seconds of confirming.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to reschedule an upcoming appointment using the 'Reschedule' button, so that I can adjust timing without creating a new request.
Description of User Story: The Reschedule Appointment feature allows an Approved Family Contact to modify the date and time of an existing upcoming appointment without creating a new appointment request. Each upcoming appointment card displays a "Reschedule" button. When clicked, the system opens a reschedule form or modal pre-populated with the current appointment details, allowing the family contact to select a new date, time window, and optionally change the appointment mode or provider preference. Upon submission, the reschedule request is sent to staff for approval. Once approved, the appointment record is updated with the new schedule and a confirmation notification is sent to the family contact. The original appointment details are retained in the appointment history for audit purposes.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact clicks the "Reschedule" button on an upcoming appointment card, Then the system must open a reschedule form pre-populated with current appointment details.

2. If the family contact selects a new date and time and submits the form, Then the system must send the reschedule request to staff for approval.
   Else if the family contact submits the form without changing any details, Then the system must display a message indicating no changes were detected.

3. If staff approve the reschedule request, Then the system must update the appointment with the new schedule and send a confirmation notification to the family contact.
   Else if staff decline the reschedule request, Then the system must notify the family contact that the reschedule was not approved and the original appointment remains.

4. If the selected new date and time conflicts with another existing appointment, Then the system must display a warning about the scheduling conflict.
   Else if the new date and time are available, Then the system must accept the reschedule request without warnings.

5. If the reschedule submission succeeds, Then the system must display a success message and close the form.
   Else if the submission fails due to a server error, Then the system must display an error message and retain the form data for retry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the family contact clicks "Reschedule" on an appointment for "29 Mar 2026 09:00 AM", changes the date to "02 Apr 2026 10:00 AM", submits, and sees a success message that the reschedule request has been sent.
2. Verify that after staff approval, the appointment on the Upcoming tab displays the updated date "02 Apr 2026" and time "10:00 AM - 10:30 AM".
3. Verify that the family contact receives a confirmation notification with the new appointment details after staff approval.
4. Verify that the original appointment details are retained in the system for audit and historical reference.

Negative:
5. Verify that the family contact opens the reschedule form and submits without changing any details; system displays "No changes detected" message.
6. Verify that staff decline the reschedule request; family contact receives a notification that the reschedule was not approved and the original appointment remains.
7. Verify that the reschedule submission fails due to a network error; system displays an error message and retains the form data.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the reschedule form opens as a modal pre-populated with the current appointment date, time, mode, and provider details.
9. Verify that the reschedule form includes date picker, time window selector, and optional fields for mode and provider preference changes.
10. Verify that the "Reschedule" button is visible on each upcoming appointment card, styled distinctly from the "Cancel Meet" button.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that rescheduling an appointment to a date/time that conflicts with another appointment displays a scheduling conflict warning.
12. Verify that rescheduling an appointment that is less than 24 hours away displays a notice about short-notice changes and still allows submission.

Security:
13. Verify that only the Approved Family Contact associated with the appointment can reschedule it; unauthorized users cannot access or modify the appointment schedule.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the reschedule form opens with pre-populated data within 1 second of clicking the "Reschedule" button.
Verify that the reschedule request submission completes within 2 seconds and displays the success message.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to fill in a 'Request Meet' form with Appointment Mode (In-Person/Virtual), Time Window, Family Member selection, Facility, Preferred Provider, and a message, so that I can provide all necessary details for scheduling.
Description of User Story: The Request Meet form is a modal dialog accessible from the Appointments page via the blue "+ Request Meet" button. The form contains the following fields: Appointment Mode as a required selection between In-Person and Virtual, a Date picker for selecting the preferred date, a Time Window field for indicating the preferred time range, a Family Member dropdown listing the family contact's associated residents for selection, a Facility dropdown listing available facilities, a Preferred Provider dropdown listing available providers at the selected facility, and a message textarea for additional notes or context. Required fields are marked with an asterisk (*). The form footer contains a "Cancel" button to close without submitting and a "Send Request >" button to submit the scheduling request. All fields include inline validation that triggers on submission attempt.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the "+ Request Meet" button, Then the system must open the Request Meet modal with all fields in their default empty state.

2. If all required fields (Appointment Mode, Date, Time Window, Family Member, Facility) are filled, Then the system must allow form submission.
   Else if any required field is left empty, Then the system must display inline validation errors on each missing field and prevent submission.

3. If the family contact selects a Facility, Then the Preferred Provider dropdown must populate with providers associated with that facility.
   Else if no Facility is selected, Then the Preferred Provider dropdown must remain disabled or display a prompt to select a facility first.

4. If the family contact selects "Virtual" as Appointment Mode, Then the system must note the preference for telehealth session setup.
   Else if the family contact selects "In-Person", Then the system must associate the Facility address with the request.

5. If the form submission succeeds, Then the system must display a success message and close the modal.
   Else if the submission fails, Then the system must display an error message and retain all form data for retry.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the family contact opens the Request Meet form, selects "Virtual" mode, date "2026-04-20", time window "02:00 PM - 03:00 PM", family member "John Doe", facility "Nemicare ALF Center", preferred provider "Dr. Patel", types "Requesting a behavioral health follow-up", and submits successfully.
2. Verify that selecting a facility populates the Preferred Provider dropdown with providers associated with that facility.
3. Verify that the family contact submits the form with only required fields filled (no preferred provider, no message) and the request is accepted.
4. Verify that the family contact selects "In-Person" mode and the submitted request includes the facility address.

Negative:
5. Verify that the family contact attempts to submit the form with the Appointment Mode field empty; system displays "Appointment Mode is required" inline validation error.
6. Verify that the family contact attempts to submit without selecting a Family Member; system displays a validation error on the Family Member field.
7. Verify that the Preferred Provider dropdown is disabled when no Facility is selected, preventing provider selection without a facility.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the Request Meet modal displays fields in the following order: Appointment Mode*, Date, Time Window, Family Member dropdown, Facility dropdown, Preferred Provider dropdown, and message textarea.
9. Verify that required fields are marked with an asterisk (*) and have placeholder text guiding the user.
10. Verify that the modal footer shows "Cancel" button on the left and "Send Request >" button (blue primary style) on the right.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that the family contact with only one associated resident sees the Family Member dropdown pre-selected with that single resident.
12. Verify that a facility with no available providers displays an appropriate message in the Preferred Provider dropdown such as "No providers available".

Security:
13. Verify that the form submission includes CSRF protection and that only authenticated Approved Family Contacts can access and submit the Request Meet form.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Request Meet modal opens with all dropdown options loaded within 1 second.
Verify that the Preferred Provider dropdown populates within 500 milliseconds of selecting a Facility.
Verify that form submission completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to search appointments by keyword on the Appointments page.
Description of User Story: The Appointments page includes a search bar positioned above the appointment cards list, below the tab navigation. The search bar allows the Approved Family Contact to type a keyword to filter the visible appointments in real time. The search matches against appointment attributes including resident name, provider name, facility name, program type, and appointment date. As the user types, the list dynamically filters to show only appointment cards that match the search term. Clearing the search bar restores the full list of appointments for the active tab. The search applies to whichever tab (Upcoming or Past) is currently active. A search icon and placeholder text such as "Search appointments" guide the user.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact types a keyword in the search bar, Then the system must filter the appointment list in real time based on the keyword.

2. If the search term matches one or more appointments, Then the system must display only the matching appointment cards.
   Else if the search term matches no appointments, Then the system must display a "No appointments found" message.

3. If the family contact clears the search bar, Then the system must restore the full appointment list for the active tab.

4. If the family contact switches tabs while a search is active, Then the system must clear the search term and display the full list for the newly selected tab.
   Else if the family contact remains on the same tab, Then the search filter must persist until manually cleared.

5. If the search is performed on the Upcoming tab, Then only future appointments must be searched.
   Else if the search is performed on the Past tab, Then only past appointments must be searched.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the family contact types "Dr. Smith" in the search bar and sees only appointments with provider "Dr. Smith" displayed.
2. Verify that the family contact types "ADH" and sees only appointments with program type "ADH" filtered.
3. Verify that the family contact types "Mar 2026" and sees only appointments in March 2026.
4. Verify that clearing the search bar restores the full appointment list for the active tab.

Negative:
5. Verify that the family contact types "xyznonexistent" and sees "No appointments found" message.
6. Verify that the family contact types a single special character "%" and the system handles it gracefully without errors.
7. Verify that searching on an empty appointment list (no appointments at all) displays "No appointments found" without errors.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the search bar is positioned above the appointment cards, below the Upcoming/Past tabs, with a search icon and placeholder text "Search appointments".
9. Verify that the filter icon is displayed next to the search bar for additional filtering options.
10. Verify that the search results update dynamically as the user types without requiring a submit action.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a search term with leading or trailing spaces is trimmed before matching and returns correct results.
12. Verify that the search is case-insensitive, matching "dr. smith" and "Dr. Smith" equally.

Security:
13. Verify that the search input is sanitized to prevent XSS injection and that search queries are not exposed in URL parameters.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that search filtering returns results within 500 milliseconds as the family contact types in the search bar.
Verify that searching across 100+ appointments completes without noticeable delay.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to click a telehealth link directly from the appointment card to join a virtual session.
Description of User Story: Each virtual appointment card on the Appointments page displays a clickable telehealth link that allows the Approved Family Contact to join the scheduled video session directly. The telehealth link is displayed as a styled hyperlink or button within the appointment card, labeled appropriately (e.g., "Join Virtual Session" or showing the meeting URL). Clicking the link opens the telehealth session in a new browser tab or launches the associated telehealth application. The link becomes active only at or near the scheduled appointment time based on a configurable window (e.g., 15 minutes before the start time). For appointments that are not yet within the join window, the link may be displayed but disabled with a tooltip indicating when it will become active.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the appointment card is for a virtual session, Then the system must display a telehealth link on the card.

2. If the current time is within the configurable join window (e.g., 15 minutes before the appointment start), Then the telehealth link must be active and clickable.
   Else if the current time is before the join window, Then the telehealth link must be displayed but disabled with a tooltip showing the activation time.

3. If the family contact clicks an active telehealth link, Then the system must open the telehealth session in a new browser tab or launch the telehealth application.
   Else if the family contact clicks a disabled telehealth link, Then the system must display a message indicating the session is not yet available.

4. If the appointment has already ended, Then the telehealth link must be marked as expired or inactive on the past appointment card.

5. If the telehealth link URL is valid and the session is active, Then the system must connect the family contact to the video session.
   Else if the telehealth link URL is invalid or the session has not been created, Then the system must display an error message and advise contacting facility staff.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a virtual appointment card displays a "Join Virtual Session" link that is clickable when the appointment is within 15 minutes of the start time.
2. Verify that clicking the active telehealth link opens the video session in a new browser tab.
3. Verify that after joining the session, the family contact is connected to the telehealth video visit with the provider.
4. Verify that the telehealth link is displayed on the appointment card with a recognizable icon (e.g., video camera icon).

Negative:
5. Verify that clicking a telehealth link before the join window displays a message "Session not yet available. Available at [time]".
6. Verify that a past appointment's telehealth link is displayed as expired and is not clickable.
7. Verify that an appointment with an invalid or missing telehealth URL displays an error message advising the family contact to contact staff.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the telehealth link is styled as a distinct clickable element (hyperlink or button) with a video icon, visually differentiated from the address field on in-person appointment cards.
9. Verify that the disabled state of the telehealth link includes a tooltip or label showing the time when it becomes active.
10. Verify that the telehealth link opens in a new tab (target="_blank") without navigating away from the Appointments page.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a family contact opens the Appointments page exactly at the join window start time and the telehealth link transitions from disabled to active without requiring a page refresh.
12. Verify that clicking the telehealth link on a mobile device opens the session in the device's default browser or associated telehealth app.

Security:
13. Verify that telehealth session links are unique, time-bound, and cannot be reused after the session ends.
14. Verify that the telehealth link is accessible only to the authenticated Approved Family Contact and the assigned provider for that appointment.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that clicking the telehealth link opens the session page in a new tab within 2 seconds.
Verify that the telehealth link activation state updates in real time without requiring a manual page refresh.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact and Provider, I need to receive unique, secure links to join a scheduled video visit session, so that the telehealth process is simple and immediate.
Description of User Story: The Telehealth Link Distribution system generates unique, secure links for each scheduled video visit session and delivers them to both the Approved Family Contact and the assigned Provider. When a virtual appointment is confirmed, the system creates a unique session URL tied to the specific appointment record. The link is included in the appointment confirmation notification and in the appointment reminder notifications. Each link contains a secure token that authenticates the participant and expires after the session concludes or after a configurable timeout period. The family contact and provider receive separate links that route them into the same session with their respective roles (participant vs. host). The links are delivered via email, SMS, and push notification channels based on each user's notification preferences. Clicking the link launches the telehealth session directly without requiring additional login or software installation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a virtual appointment is confirmed by staff, Then the system must generate unique, secure telehealth session links for the family contact and the provider.

2. If the family contact clicks their unique link during an active session, Then the system must connect them as a participant in the video visit.
   Else if the provider clicks their unique link during an active session, Then the system must connect them as the host of the video visit.

3. If the session link is clicked before the session start time (within the join window), Then the system must place the user in a waiting room or connect them to the session.
   Else if the session link is clicked after the session has ended, Then the system must display a message that the session has concluded and the link is expired.

4. If the user's notification preferences include email, Then the telehealth link must be included in the email notification.
   Else if the user's preferences include only SMS, Then the link must be delivered via SMS.
   Else if the user has push notifications enabled, Then the link must be included in the push notification.

5. If the telehealth link token is valid and not expired, Then the system must authenticate the user and grant session access.
   Else if the token is invalid or expired, Then the system must deny access and display an appropriate error message.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that upon virtual appointment confirmation, unique telehealth links are generated for both the Approved Family Contact and the assigned Provider.
2. Verify that the family contact receives the telehealth link in the appointment confirmation email with a "Join Session" button.
3. Verify that the provider receives a separate telehealth link that connects them as the session host.
4. Verify that clicking the telehealth link during the session window connects the user directly to the video visit without additional login or software installation.

Negative:
5. Verify that clicking an expired telehealth link after the session has ended displays "This session has concluded" message.
6. Verify that using a telehealth link with a tampered or invalid token displays an access-denied error.
7. Verify that a user who is not the assigned family contact or provider for the appointment cannot join the session using a link intended for another participant.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the telehealth link in email notifications is displayed as a prominent "Join Session" button with clear instructions.
9. Verify that SMS notifications contain a shortened, clickable telehealth URL with the appointment time context.
10. Verify that push notifications display the appointment time and a tap-to-join action that launches the telehealth session.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that if the appointment is rescheduled, new telehealth links are generated and the old links are invalidated.
12. Verify that both the family contact and provider can join the session simultaneously using their respective unique links without conflict.

Security:
13. Verify that each telehealth link contains a cryptographically secure token that is unique per session and per participant.
14. Verify that telehealth links expire after the session ends or after the configurable timeout, preventing unauthorized reuse.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that telehealth session links are generated and included in confirmation notifications within 30 seconds of appointment confirmation.
Verify that clicking the telehealth link connects the user to the video session within 3 seconds.

User Story: As a Clinician, I need the system to automatically create or link the telehealth session to a patient encounter record, so that documentation of the service provided is immediate and accurate.
Description of User Story: The Telehealth Encounter Linking system automatically creates or associates a patient encounter record when a telehealth session is initiated or completed. When a clinician starts a scheduled telehealth session, the system checks if a corresponding encounter record exists for the appointment. If no encounter exists, the system automatically creates one with pre-populated fields including patient name, date of service, appointment type (telehealth), provider name, and session start time. If an encounter already exists, the system links the telehealth session metadata (session ID, duration, participant list) to the existing record. Upon session completion, the system updates the encounter with the session end time, total duration, and a flag indicating the encounter was conducted via telehealth. This ensures clinical documentation is immediately available for the clinician to complete chart notes, assessments, and billing codes without manual encounter creation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a clinician starts a scheduled telehealth session, Then the system must check for an existing encounter record for the appointment.

2. If no encounter record exists, Then the system must automatically create one with pre-populated fields (patient name, date, appointment type "Telehealth", provider, start time).
   Else if an encounter record already exists, Then the system must link the telehealth session metadata (session ID, duration, participants) to the existing record.

3. If the telehealth session completes normally, Then the system must update the encounter with session end time, total duration, and a telehealth indicator flag.
   Else if the telehealth session disconnects unexpectedly, Then the system must log the disconnection event and update the encounter with a partial session flag and the last known duration.

4. If the encounter creation succeeds, Then the clinician must be able to access the encounter immediately for documentation.
   Else if the encounter creation fails, Then the system must log the error, notify the clinician, and provide an option to manually create the encounter.

5. If multiple telehealth sessions occur for the same appointment (e.g., reconnection after disconnection), Then the system must aggregate the session data into the single encounter record.
   Else if the session is a single continuous session, Then the system must record it as a single entry in the encounter.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when a clinician starts a telehealth session for patient "Jane Doe" on "2026-04-15", the system automatically creates an encounter record with patient name, date, type "Telehealth", and provider "Dr. Smith".
2. Verify that when an encounter already exists for the appointment, the telehealth session metadata (session ID, participants) is linked to the existing record.
3. Verify that upon session completion, the encounter record is updated with end time, total duration "30 minutes", and a telehealth flag.
4. Verify that the clinician can open the encounter record immediately after session completion and sees all telehealth session details pre-populated.

Negative:
5. Verify that when encounter creation fails due to a server error, the system logs the error, notifies the clinician, and provides an option to manually create the encounter.
6. Verify that when a telehealth session disconnects unexpectedly, the system logs the disconnection and updates the encounter with a partial session flag.
7. Verify that if the clinician starts a session for a cancelled appointment, the system does not create an encounter and displays an appropriate warning.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the encounter record displays a clear "Telehealth" badge or indicator distinguishing it from in-person encounters.
9. Verify that the encounter record shows telehealth-specific fields including session ID, duration, participant list, and session start/end times.
10. Verify that the clinician's documentation workspace is accessible directly from the encounter record with chart notes, assessments, and billing code fields.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that when a telehealth session is interrupted and reconnected, the system aggregates both session segments into a single encounter record with combined total duration.
12. Verify that simultaneous telehealth sessions for different patients by the same clinician create separate, correctly linked encounter records.

Security:
13. Verify that encounter records created via telehealth are subject to the same access controls as in-person encounters, accessible only to authorized clinicians and staff.
14. Verify that telehealth session metadata stored in the encounter record is encrypted and compliant with HIPAA requirements.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the encounter record is created or linked within 5 seconds of the telehealth session starting.
Verify that the encounter record is updated with session completion details within 10 seconds of the session ending.
Verify that the clinician can access the updated encounter record for documentation within 2 seconds of session completion.

User Story: As a Patient, I should be able to view statements and payment history under Billing.
Description of User Story: The Billing module provides patients with a centralized view of their financial interactions with the healthcare facility. Upon navigating to the Billing section, the patient sees two primary tabs or sections: Statements and Payment History. The Statements section displays a chronological list of all billing statements generated for the patient, each showing the statement date, total amount, amount paid, outstanding balance, and a status indicator (Paid, Partially Paid, Unpaid). The Payment History section lists all payments made by the patient or on their behalf, showing the payment date, amount, payment method, reference number, and the statement or invoice it was applied to. Both sections support date-range filtering and sorting by date or amount. A summary card at the top displays the current outstanding balance, total paid year-to-date, and the date of the last payment. Each statement row is clickable to view its full details, and each payment entry links back to the associated statement.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient navigates to the Billing section, Then the system must display the Statements and Payment History sections with current data.

2. If the patient has no billing statements on record, Then the system must display a "No statements available" message in the Statements section.
   Else if the patient has no payment history, Then the system must display a "No payments found" message in the Payment History section.
   Else if both sections have data, Then the system must load the Statements tab by default.

3. If a statement has a status of "Unpaid" and is past due, Then the system must highlight the statement row with a visual indicator such as a red badge or overdue label.
   Else if a statement is "Partially Paid", Then the system must display the remaining balance prominently.

4. If the billing data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display a "Unable to load billing data. Please try again later." message with a retry option.

5. If the patient applies a date-range filter that returns no results, Then the system must display "No records found for the selected date range" and offer a clear-filter option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Billing section displays the Statements and Payment History tabs with correct data loaded for the logged-in patient.
2. Verify that the Statements section lists three statements with dates, amounts, paid amounts, balances, and status badges matching the patient account data.
3. Verify that the Payment History section lists five payments with correct dates, amounts, payment methods, reference numbers, and linked statements.
4. Verify that the summary card shows the current outstanding balance of $250.00, year-to-date paid total of $1,200.00, and last payment date of March 15, 2026.
5. Verify that clicking a statement row navigates to the full statement details page.

Negative:
6. Verify that a patient with no statements sees "No statements available" and no empty table rows.
7. Verify that a patient with no payments sees "No payments found" in the Payment History section.
8. Verify that when the billing API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load billing data. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the Billing page displays a summary card at the top with outstanding balance, year-to-date paid, and last payment date, followed by tabbed sections for Statements and Payment History.
10. Verify that unpaid overdue statements are highlighted with a red "Overdue" badge and partially paid statements show the remaining balance in bold.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a patient with 100+ statements can scroll through the paginated list without performance issues.
12. Verify that a payment applied across two statements appears in Payment History with references to both statements.

Security:
13. Verify that a patient can only view their own billing data and cannot access another patient's statements or payment history through URL manipulation.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Billing page loads both the Statements and Payment History sections within 2 seconds for a patient with up to 50 statements and 100 payments.
Verify that applying a date-range filter refreshes the displayed list within 1.5 seconds.

User Story: As a patient, I should be able to view statements details in PDF format.
Description of User Story: The Statement Details PDF feature allows patients to view and download a detailed billing statement in PDF format directly from the Billing section. When a patient clicks on a statement row or a "View PDF" button associated with a statement, the system generates or retrieves a pre-rendered PDF document containing the complete statement details: facility name and logo, patient name and account number, statement date and period, itemized list of charges with service descriptions, dates of service, procedure codes, quantities, unit prices, and line totals, subtotal, applicable adjustments or discounts, taxes if any, total amount due, payments applied, and the remaining balance. The PDF opens in the browser's built-in PDF viewer within the portal or in a new tab, with options to download the file or print it. The PDF filename follows a consistent naming convention including the statement date and patient name for easy identification.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient clicks "View PDF" for a statement, Then the system must generate and display the PDF within the portal or a new browser tab.

2. If the PDF generation fails due to a server error, Then the system must display an error message "Unable to generate statement PDF. Please try again." with a retry option.
   Else if the statement has no itemized charges, Then the system must generate a PDF with a "No charges for this period" note and the zero-balance summary.

3. If the patient's browser does not support inline PDF viewing, Then the system must automatically trigger a download of the PDF file.

4. If the statement PDF contains Protected Health Information, Then the system must ensure the PDF is transmitted over an encrypted connection and is not cached by the browser.

5. If the patient requests a PDF for a very old archived statement, Then the system must retrieve it from the archive store and display a brief loading indicator during retrieval.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "View PDF" for a statement dated January 2026 opens a PDF showing the facility logo, patient name "Ralph Edwards", statement date, 5 itemized charges, subtotal $450.00, insurance adjustment -$150.00, total due $300.00, and balance $100.00.
2. Verify that the PDF download produces a file named "Statement-2026-01-15-RalphEdwards.pdf" with correct content.
3. Verify that clicking the print icon from the PDF viewer opens the browser print dialog with the statement pre-loaded.
4. Verify that a multi-page statement PDF with 30 line items renders all pages with consistent headers and page numbers.

Negative:
5. Verify that when the PDF generation service is down, clicking "View PDF" displays "Unable to generate statement PDF. Please try again." with a retry button.
6. Verify that a statement with zero charges generates a PDF containing a "No charges for this period" message.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that the "View PDF" button or icon is clearly visible next to each statement row in the Statements list.
8. Verify that while the PDF is being generated, a loading spinner or progress indicator is displayed to the patient.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify that a statement PDF for a period with one single charge item renders correctly without layout issues.
10. Verify that requesting a PDF for an archived statement from 2 years ago retrieves and displays the document successfully.

Security:
11. Verify that the PDF download URL is authenticated and cannot be accessed by unauthenticated users or shared via direct link.
12. Verify that the PDF is served over HTTPS with appropriate no-cache headers to prevent local caching of PHI.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the statement PDF generates and renders in the browser within 3 seconds for a statement containing up to 50 line items.
Verify that downloading the PDF file completes within 2 seconds for a document up to 5 pages.

User Story: As a patient, I should be able to do payment by adding cards details
Description of User Story: The Payment module allows patients to make payments toward their outstanding balances by entering credit or debit card details through a secure payment form. The payment flow begins when the patient clicks a "Make Payment" button from the Billing section or a specific statement. The payment form collects the cardholder name, card number, expiration date (MM/YY), CVV, and billing address. The patient can enter the payment amount, which defaults to the full outstanding balance but allows partial payments. A payment summary panel displays the statement or invoice being paid, the amount due, and the amount being submitted. Upon clicking "Submit Payment", the system securely transmits the card data to the payment gateway for authorization. A real-time validation ensures card number format, expiration date validity, and CVV length before submission. The card data entry fields use masking and PCI-DSS compliant input handling to protect sensitive information.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient clicks "Make Payment", Then the system must display the secure payment form with all required fields and the outstanding balance pre-populated.

2. If the patient enters a valid card number, valid expiration date, valid CVV, and clicks "Submit Payment", Then the system must process the payment and display a success confirmation with a transaction reference number.
   Else if the card number fails Luhn validation, Then the system must display "Invalid card number" and block submission.
   Else if the expiration date is in the past, Then the system must display "Card has expired" and block submission.
   Else if the CVV length does not match the card type (3 digits for Visa/Mastercard, 4 for Amex), Then the system must display "Invalid CVV" and block submission.

3. If the payment gateway declines the card, Then the system must display "Payment declined. Please check your card details or try a different card." without revealing the specific decline reason from the gateway.
   Else if the payment gateway times out, Then the system must display "Payment processing timed out. Please try again." and must not charge the card.

4. If the patient enters a payment amount greater than the outstanding balance, Then the system must display a warning "Amount exceeds outstanding balance" and block submission.
   Else if the patient enters zero or a negative amount, Then the system must display "Please enter a valid payment amount".

5. If the patient navigates away from the payment form before submitting, Then the system must not retain or cache any entered card details.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Make Payment" displays the payment form with cardholder name, card number, expiration date, CVV, and billing address fields, with the payment amount defaulting to the outstanding balance of $300.00.
2. Verify that entering valid Visa card details (4111 1111 1111 1111, expiry 12/28, CVV 123) and clicking "Submit Payment" processes the payment and shows a success confirmation with a transaction reference number.
3. Verify that a partial payment of $100.00 against a $300.00 balance is processed successfully and the remaining balance updates to $200.00.
4. Verify that the card number field masks input showing only the last four digits and auto-formats with spaces.

Negative:
5. Verify that entering an invalid card number (1234 5678 9012 3456) displays "Invalid card number" and blocks submission.
6. Verify that entering an expired date (01/24) displays "Card has expired" and blocks submission.
7. Verify that entering a payment amount of $0.00 displays "Please enter a valid payment amount".
8. Verify that entering an amount exceeding the outstanding balance displays "Amount exceeds outstanding balance".
9. Verify that a declined card shows "Payment declined. Please check your card details or try a different card."

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the payment form is displayed within a secure-looking container with a lock icon, card type logos (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), and clearly labeled input fields.
11. Verify that the payment summary panel shows the statement reference, amount due, and payment amount side by side for easy review.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that submitting payment during a network interruption shows "Payment processing timed out. Please try again." and no charge is applied.
13. Verify that navigating away from the payment form clears all entered card data from the browser.

Security:
14. Verify that card data fields use PCI-DSS compliant input handling and the card number is never sent in plain text in API requests.
15. Verify that the payment form is served over HTTPS and card details are transmitted to the payment gateway via a secure tokenization mechanism.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the payment form loads within 2 seconds after clicking "Make Payment".
Verify that the payment submission and gateway authorization complete within 5 seconds under normal conditions.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a payment confirmation after successfully submitting a payment
Description of User Story: The Payment Confirmation feature ensures that after an Approved Family Contact successfully submits a payment on behalf of a patient, the system generates and displays a comprehensive confirmation screen and sends a confirmation notification. The on-screen confirmation includes a green success banner, the transaction reference number, payment amount, payment date and time, last four digits of the card used, the statement or invoice the payment was applied to, and the updated remaining balance. A "Download Receipt" button allows the family contact to save the confirmation as a PDF receipt. Simultaneously, the system sends a payment confirmation email to the family contact's registered email address containing the same transaction details. The confirmation page also provides a "Return to Billing" button to navigate back to the billing overview and a "Print Receipt" option for physical record keeping.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a payment is successfully processed, Then the system must display the confirmation screen with all transaction details and send a confirmation email to the family contact.

2. If the payment was successful but the confirmation email fails to send, Then the system must still display the on-screen confirmation and retry the email delivery in the background.
   Else if the email address on file is invalid, Then the system must display a notice on the confirmation screen advising the user to update their email in profile settings.

3. If the family contact closes the confirmation screen before downloading the receipt, Then the receipt must remain accessible from the Payment History section.

4. If the PDF receipt generation fails, Then the system must display an error "Unable to generate receipt. You can access it later from Payment History." and log the failure for retry.

5. If the payment was applied to multiple statements, Then the confirmation must list each statement with the portion of the payment applied to it.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that after a successful payment of $200.00, the confirmation screen displays a green success banner, transaction reference "TXN-20260402-001", amount $200.00, date April 2, 2026, card ending 4242, and updated balance $100.00.
2. Verify that clicking "Download Receipt" produces a PDF file containing all transaction details matching the on-screen confirmation.
3. Verify that a confirmation email is received at the family contact's registered email within 2 minutes of payment, containing the transaction reference, amount, and date.
4. Verify that clicking "Return to Billing" navigates to the Billing page showing the updated outstanding balance reflecting the payment.
5. Verify that clicking "Print Receipt" opens the browser print dialog with a print-optimized receipt layout.

Negative:
6. Verify that if the confirmation email fails to send, the on-screen confirmation is still displayed with a notice "Confirmation email could not be sent. Please check your email settings."
7. Verify that if PDF receipt generation fails, the message "Unable to generate receipt. You can access it later from Payment History." is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the confirmation screen has a prominent green success banner at the top, transaction details in a structured card layout, and action buttons (Download Receipt, Print Receipt, Return to Billing) at the bottom.
9. Verify that the transaction details are organized clearly with labels and values aligned for readability.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a payment applied across two statements shows both statement references with respective amounts on the confirmation screen.
11. Verify that closing the browser after payment and reopening the portal allows the receipt to be accessed from Payment History.

Security:
12. Verify that the confirmation email does not include full card details and only shows the last four digits.
13. Verify that the receipt download URL is authenticated and cannot be accessed without a valid session.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the payment confirmation screen renders within 2 seconds after the payment gateway returns a successful response.
Verify that the confirmation email is delivered to the family contact's inbox within 60 seconds of payment completion.

User Story: As a patient, I should be able to view Payment history with details.
Description of User Story: The Payment History section provides patients with a comprehensive, chronologically ordered record of all payments made against their account. Each payment entry displays the payment date, payment amount, payment method (credit card, debit card, check, or cash), the last four digits of the card used (if applicable), the transaction reference number, the statement or invoice the payment was applied to, and the payment status (Completed, Pending, Refunded, Failed). Patients can expand each payment row to view additional details including the payer name, billing address used, and any notes attached to the payment. The section supports filtering by date range, payment method, and status, as well as sorting by date or amount in ascending or descending order. A search bar allows patients to look up specific payments by reference number. A total payments summary at the top shows the aggregate amount paid over the selected time period.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient navigates to Payment History, Then the system must display all payments in reverse chronological order with complete details.

2. If the patient has no payment records, Then the system must display "No payment history found" with a suggestion to visit the Billing section to make a payment.
   Else if the patient applies filters that return no results, Then the system must display "No payments match the selected filters" with a clear-filters option.

3. If a payment has a status of "Failed", Then the system must display the failure reason in the expanded details and highlight the row with a red indicator.
   Else if a payment has a status of "Refunded", Then the system must display the refund date and amount in the expanded details.
   Else if a payment has a status of "Pending", Then the system must display an estimated processing time.

4. If the payment was made by a family contact on behalf of the patient, Then the payer name in the expanded details must show the family contact's name.

5. If the patient searches for a reference number that does not exist, Then the system must display "No payment found with reference number [entered value]".
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that Payment History displays 10 payment records in reverse chronological order with correct dates, amounts, methods, reference numbers, and statuses.
2. Verify that expanding a payment row shows payer name "Ralph Edwards", billing address, and payment note "Monthly co-pay".
3. Verify that filtering by date range January 1-31, 2026 returns 3 payments totaling $450.00 in the summary.
4. Verify that filtering by status "Refunded" displays 1 payment with the refund date and refund amount in expanded details.
5. Verify that searching reference number "TXN-20260215-003" returns the matching payment entry.

Negative:
6. Verify that a new patient with no payments sees "No payment history found" with a link to the Billing section.
7. Verify that filtering by a date range with no payments displays "No payments match the selected filters" with a clear-filters button.
8. Verify that searching for a non-existent reference number displays "No payment found with reference number TXN-000000".

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the Payment History page displays a filter bar with date range, payment method dropdown, and status dropdown, a search bar for reference numbers, and a total payments summary card at the top.
10. Verify that payment status badges use color coding: green for Completed, yellow for Pending, red for Failed, and blue for Refunded.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a payment made by a family contact on behalf of the patient shows the family contact's name as the payer in expanded details.
12. Verify that a patient with 200+ payment records can paginate through the list without performance degradation.

Security:
13. Verify that payment history data is accessible only to the authenticated patient and their authorized family contacts, and API endpoints reject unauthorized access.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Payment History section loads the most recent 50 payments within 2 seconds.
Verify that applying filters and refreshing the payment list completes within 1.5 seconds for a patient with up to 200 payment records.

User Story: As a patient, I should be able to view my payment statements so that I can keep track of my healthcare expenses and payments made.
Description of User Story: The Payment Statements section provides patients with a detailed view of periodic payment statements that consolidate all charges, payments, adjustments, and balances for a defined billing period. Each payment statement shows the statement number, billing period (start and end dates), total charges for the period, total payments received, adjustments or credits applied, and the net balance. Patients can view a list of all payment statements organized by billing period in reverse chronological order. Clicking on a statement opens a detailed breakdown showing individual service line items with dates of service, descriptions, charge amounts, insurance payments, patient responsibility, and running balance. The section includes a "Download PDF" option for each statement and a summary dashboard showing year-to-date expenses, total payments, and current balance. Patients can use this feature to reconcile their healthcare spending, prepare tax documents, and verify insurance processing.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient navigates to Payment Statements, Then the system must display all available statements in reverse chronological order with period summaries.

2. If the patient has no payment statements generated yet, Then the system must display "No payment statements available at this time" with a brief explanation.
   Else if a statement has a zero balance, Then the system must display it with a "Paid in Full" badge.

3. If the patient clicks on a statement for a period with no service line items, Then the system must display the statement header with a "No charges for this period" note.
   Else if the statement contains insurance adjustments, Then each adjustment must be displayed as a separate line item with the adjustment type and amount.

4. If the PDF download fails, Then the system must display "Unable to download statement. Please try again." with a retry option.

5. If the patient views a statement from a previous year, Then the system must include a note indicating the statement is from a prior fiscal year and may be useful for tax documentation.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Payment Statements section lists 6 billing period statements in reverse chronological order, with the most recent period (March 2026) at the top.
2. Verify that a statement for February 2026 shows statement number "PS-2026-02", billing period Feb 1-28, total charges $800.00, insurance payments $600.00, adjustments $50.00, and net balance $150.00.
3. Verify that clicking the February 2026 statement shows 4 service line items with dates, descriptions, charges, insurance payments, and patient responsibility columns.
4. Verify that clicking "Download PDF" for a statement generates and downloads a PDF matching the on-screen details.
5. Verify that the summary dashboard shows year-to-date expenses $2,400.00, total payments $2,100.00, and current balance $300.00.

Negative:
6. Verify that a new patient with no billing history sees "No payment statements available at this time".
7. Verify that a PDF download failure displays "Unable to download statement. Please try again." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the Payment Statements page displays a year-to-date summary dashboard at the top, followed by a list of statement cards showing period, charges, payments, and balance.
9. Verify that statements with zero balance display a green "Paid in Full" badge and statements with outstanding balances display the amount in bold red.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a statement with only insurance adjustments and no patient charges displays correctly with a $0.00 patient responsibility.
11. Verify that viewing a statement from the previous year includes a note about prior fiscal year for tax purposes.

Security:
12. Verify that payment statement data is accessible only by the authenticated patient and not exposed to other patients via API parameter manipulation.
13. Verify that all statement data including PDF downloads are served over encrypted HTTPS connections.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Payment Statements list loads within 2 seconds for a patient with up to 24 monthly statements.
Verify that opening a statement detail view with up to 30 service line items renders within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact of a Medicaid-only patient, I should be able to view and download invoices.
Description of User Story: The Invoice module allows Approved Family Contacts of Medicaid-only patients to access and download invoices generated for the patient's care services. The invoice list displays all invoices associated with the patient, showing the invoice number, invoice date, billing period, total amount, Medicaid coverage amount, patient or family responsibility (if any), and invoice status (Paid, Pending, Processing). Each invoice can be expanded or clicked to view the full detail, which includes itemized services rendered during the billing period with service codes, descriptions, units, rates, and amounts. A "Download Invoice" button allows the family contact to download the invoice as a PDF. The system clearly labels Medicaid-covered amounts versus any remaining family responsibility. For Medicaid-only patients, most invoices show zero patient responsibility, but the family contact can still review the services provided and verify the Medicaid claims. A filter allows narrowing invoices by date range, status, and year.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When an Approved Family Contact navigates to the Invoice section, Then the system must display all invoices for the linked Medicaid-only patient in reverse chronological order.

2. If the patient has no invoices on record, Then the system must display "No invoices available for this patient".
   Else if the family contact is not linked to the patient, Then the system must deny access and display "You are not authorized to view invoices for this patient."

3. If an invoice is fully covered by Medicaid, Then the family responsibility column must display "$0.00" with a "Fully Covered" label.
   Else if a service line item is partially covered by Medicaid, Then the invoice must show the Medicaid payment and the remaining family responsibility for that item.
   Else if a service is denied by Medicaid, Then the invoice must display the denial reason and the full amount as family responsibility.

4. If the PDF download fails, Then the system must display "Unable to download invoice. Please try again later." with a retry option.

5. If the invoice status is "Processing", Then the system must display a disclaimer "Amounts are subject to change pending Medicaid adjudication."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that an Approved Family Contact sees 4 invoices for the Medicaid-only patient listed in reverse chronological order with correct invoice numbers, dates, and amounts.
2. Verify that clicking invoice "INV-2026-03-001" shows 6 itemized services with codes, descriptions, units, rates, and amounts, with Medicaid coverage matching the total.
3. Verify that clicking "Download Invoice" downloads a PDF named "Invoice-INV-2026-03-001.pdf" with all details matching the on-screen view.
4. Verify that all invoices for the Medicaid-only patient show $0.00 family responsibility with a "Fully Covered" label.
5. Verify that filtering by year 2026 and status "Paid" returns 2 invoices.

Negative:
6. Verify that a family contact not linked to the patient sees "You are not authorized to view invoices for this patient."
7. Verify that a patient with no invoices shows "No invoices available for this patient."
8. Verify that a failed PDF download displays "Unable to download invoice. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the Invoice list displays invoice number, date, period, total, Medicaid coverage, family responsibility, and status in a clean table format with status badges.
10. Verify that "Fully Covered" labels are displayed as green badges next to the $0.00 family responsibility.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that an invoice with a "Processing" status displays the disclaimer "Amounts are subject to change pending Medicaid adjudication." and does not allow final receipt generation.
12. Verify that a Medicaid denial on one service line shows the denial reason and shifts the amount to family responsibility while other covered services show $0.00.

Security:
13. Verify that only Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific patient can access the invoice data, and other family contacts or patients cannot view it.
14. Verify that invoice PDF URLs require authentication and expire after a short validity period.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Invoice list loads within 2 seconds for a patient with up to 36 monthly invoices.
Verify that downloading an invoice PDF completes within 3 seconds for an invoice containing up to 20 line items.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a monthly patient summary report alongside the invoice, showing changes in medications, vitals, alerts, and incidents over the last 30 days
Description of User Story: The Monthly Patient Summary Report is an automatically generated companion document delivered alongside the monthly invoice to Approved Family Contacts. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the patient's care and health status over the preceding 30 days, organized into clearly labeled sections: Medication Changes (new medications started, discontinued medications, dosage adjustments with dates and prescriber names), Vitals Summary (latest readings for blood pressure, heart rate, weight, blood glucose with trend indicators showing improvement, stable, or decline), Alerts (any clinical alerts triggered during the period such as fall risk changes, allergy reactions, or abnormal lab results), and Incidents (recorded incidents including falls, behavioral events, or emergency transfers with dates, descriptions, and resolution status). The report is available as a downloadable PDF from the invoice detail page and is also sent as an email attachment with the monthly invoice notification. A "View Summary" button on the invoice page opens the report inline for quick review.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the monthly invoice is generated, Then the system must automatically generate the patient summary report for the same 30-day period and make it accessible from the invoice detail page.

2. If there were no medication changes during the 30-day period, Then the Medication Changes section must display "No medication changes during this period".
   Else if there were no vitals recorded, Then the Vitals Summary section must display "No vitals recorded during this period".
   Else if there were no alerts, Then the Alerts section must display "No alerts during this period".
   Else if there were no incidents, Then the Incidents section must display "No incidents during this period".

3. If a vital sign shows a declining trend, Then the trend indicator must display a red downward arrow with "Decline" label.
   Else if a vital sign is stable, Then the trend indicator must display a green horizontal arrow with "Stable" label.
   Else if a vital sign is improving, Then the trend indicator must display a green upward arrow with "Improving" label.

4. If the report PDF generation fails, Then the system must display "Summary report is being generated. Please try again shortly." and queue regeneration.

5. If the invoice email delivery fails, Then the system must retry delivery and make the report available on the portal for manual download.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the invoice detail page for March 2026 displays a "View Summary" button that opens the patient summary report covering March 1-31, 2026.
2. Verify that the Medication Changes section shows 2 new medications (Lisinopril started March 5, Metformin dosage increased March 18) with prescriber Dr. Smith.
3. Verify that the Vitals Summary shows blood pressure 130/85 with "Stable" trend, heart rate 78 with "Improving" trend, and weight 165 lbs with "Stable" trend.
4. Verify that the Alerts section shows 1 alert: "Fall risk level changed from Low to Moderate on March 12, 2026."
5. Verify that the Incidents section shows 1 incident: "Minor fall in dining area on March 20, 2026 — Resolved, no injury."
6. Verify that downloading the report produces a PDF with all four sections matching the on-screen content.
7. Verify that the monthly invoice email includes the summary report as a PDF attachment.

Negative:
8. Verify that when there are no medication changes, the section displays "No medication changes during this period."
9. Verify that when there are no incidents, the section displays "No incidents during this period."
10. Verify that if the report PDF fails to generate, the message "Summary report is being generated. Please try again shortly." is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the summary report is organized with clearly labeled section headers (Medication Changes, Vitals Summary, Alerts, Incidents) and uses icons and color-coded trend indicators.
12. Verify that the "View Summary" button is prominently placed on the invoice detail page next to the "Download Invoice" button.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a patient with no activity in any category during the 30-day period receives a report with all four sections showing "No [category] during this period."
14. Verify that a report covering a period where the patient was transferred between facilities includes data from both facilities.

Security:
15. Verify that the patient summary report is accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the patient and is not viewable by other portal users.
16. Verify that the report PDF attachment in the email is encrypted or the email is sent over a secure channel to protect PHI.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the monthly patient summary report loads inline within 3 seconds when the "View Summary" button is clicked.
Verify that the report PDF download completes within 3 seconds for a report containing data across all four sections.

User Story: As a Patient, I should be able to View my Personal Details
Description of User Story: The Personal Details section within the Profile page allows patients to view their personal information stored in the system. Upon navigating to the Profile page, the patient sees a read-only display of their personal details including full name (first, middle, last), date of birth, gender, Social Security Number (masked, showing only last four digits), primary phone number, secondary phone number, email address, and full residential address (street, city, state, ZIP code). The information is organized in a clean card or form layout with clearly labeled fields. A profile photo or avatar is displayed at the top of the page alongside the patient's name. The data is sourced from the facility's patient records and is presented in a non-editable format since patient demographic changes must go through the facility's administrative process. A "Request Update" link or button is available for patients to initiate a request if any information is incorrect.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient navigates to the Profile page, Then the system must display all personal details in read-only format sourced from the facility records.

2. If the patient does not have a profile photo on file, Then the system must display a default avatar icon with the patient's initials.
   Else if the profile photo file is corrupted or unavailable, Then the system must display the default avatar and not show a broken image.

3. If any personal detail field is empty or not on record, Then the system must display "Not provided" or "N/A" for that field instead of leaving it blank.

4. If the patient clicks "Request Update", Then the system must open a pre-filled request form or provide contact information for the facility's administrative team.

5. If the profile data fails to load due to a server error, Then the system must display "Unable to load personal details. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Profile page displays the patient's full name "Ralph Edwards", date of birth, gender, masked SSN "***-**-1234", phone number, email, and residential address.
2. Verify that the profile photo for "Ralph Edwards" is displayed at the top of the page alongside the name.
3. Verify that the SSN field shows only the last four digits with the rest masked.
4. Verify that clicking "Request Update" opens a request form or shows facility contact information for making corrections.

Negative:
5. Verify that when a profile photo is not on file, a default avatar with the patient's initials "RE" is displayed.
6. Verify that when the secondary phone number is not on record, the field displays "Not provided".
7. Verify that when the profile data service is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load personal details. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the personal details are displayed in a structured card layout with clearly labeled fields (Full Name, DOB, Gender, SSN, Phone, Email, Address) and no editable input fields.
9. Verify that the profile photo or avatar is prominently displayed at the top of the page with the patient's name next to it in a large font.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a patient with a very long address (100+ characters) has the address displayed fully without truncation or layout breaking.
11. Verify that a patient whose records have recently been updated by the facility sees the updated information on next login.

Security:
12. Verify that the patient can only view their own personal details and cannot access another patient's profile through URL or API manipulation.
13. Verify that the full SSN is never transmitted to the browser and only the masked version is sent by the API.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Profile page with personal details loads within 2 seconds including the profile photo.
Verify that the "Request Update" form or contact information loads within 1 second after clicking the link.

User Story: As a Patient, I should be able to view my Insurance details
Description of User Story: The Insurance Details section within the Profile page allows patients to view their insurance coverage information stored in the facility's system. The section displays the primary insurance details including insurance provider name, plan name, policy number, group number, subscriber name, subscriber relationship to patient, effective date, and expiration date. If the patient has secondary insurance, it is displayed in a separate card below the primary insurance with the same fields. Each insurance entry shows a status indicator (Active, Expired, Pending Verification) with color coding. A copy of the insurance card (front and back images) is displayed if available in the system. The insurance information is presented in read-only format as updates must be processed through the facility's administrative team. A "Request Update" button allows patients to flag outdated insurance information for review.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient navigates to Insurance Details, Then the system must display all insurance records on file in read-only format with status indicators.

2. If the patient has no insurance on file, Then the system must display "No insurance information on record. Please contact the facility to add your insurance details."
   Else if only primary insurance is on file, Then the system must display the primary insurance card only with no empty secondary section.

3. If the insurance has expired, Then the system must display a red "Expired" badge and a recommendation to contact the facility for renewal.
   Else if the insurance is pending verification, Then the system must display a yellow "Pending Verification" badge.
   Else if the insurance is active, Then the system must display a green "Active" badge.

4. If insurance card images are not on file, Then the system must display a placeholder with "Insurance card images not available".

5. If the insurance data fails to load, Then the system must display "Unable to load insurance details. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Insurance Details section displays primary insurance: provider "Blue Cross Blue Shield", plan "PPO Gold", policy number "BCB-123456", group "GRP-789", subscriber "Ralph Edwards (Self)", effective date January 1, 2026, expiration December 31, 2026, with an "Active" badge.
2. Verify that secondary insurance is displayed in a separate card with complete details.
3. Verify that insurance card front and back images are displayed and can be enlarged by clicking.
4. Verify that clicking "Request Update" opens a request form directed to the facility's insurance team.

Negative:
