5. Verify that a patient with no insurance on file sees "No insurance information on record. Please contact the facility to add your insurance details."
6. Verify that when insurance card images are missing, the placeholder "Insurance card images not available" is shown.
7. Verify that when the insurance data service is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load insurance details. Please try again later."

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the insurance section displays insurance entries as separate cards with labeled fields, status badges, and insurance card image thumbnails.
9. Verify that active insurance badges are green, expired badges are red, and pending verification badges are yellow.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a patient with three insurance records (primary, secondary, tertiary) has all three displayed in order.
11. Verify that an insurance record with a missing group number displays "N/A" for the group field.

Security:
12. Verify that insurance details including policy numbers are accessible only to the authenticated patient and are not exposed to other users.
13. Verify that insurance card images are served over HTTPS with proper authentication and are not publicly accessible.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Insurance Details section loads within 2 seconds including insurance card images.
Verify that enlarging an insurance card image renders the full-resolution version within 1 second.

User Story: As a Patient, I should be able to change my password.
Description of User Story: The Change Password feature within the Settings section allows patients to update their account password for security purposes. The page presents a form with three fields: Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password. The current password field requires the patient to verify their identity before allowing a change. The new password must meet the system's password policy requirements: minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one digit, and one special character. A real-time password strength indicator shows the strength level (Weak, Fair, Strong, Very Strong) as the patient types. The Confirm New Password field validates that it matches the New Password field. Upon successful password change, the system displays a success message, invalidates all other active sessions for the account, and sends a confirmation email to the patient's registered email address. A "Show/Hide" toggle on each password field allows the patient to view the entered text.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the patient submits the change password form, Then the system must validate the current password, enforce the password policy on the new password, and confirm the new password matches.

2. If the current password entered is incorrect, Then the system must display "Current password is incorrect" and block the password change.
   Else if the new password does not meet the minimum requirements, Then the system must display specific guidance on which requirements are not met (e.g., "Must include at least one special character").
   Else if the Confirm New Password does not match the New Password, Then the system must display "Passwords do not match".

3. If the new password is the same as the current password, Then the system must display "New password must be different from the current password" and block submission.

4. If the patient has failed to enter the correct current password three times, Then the system must temporarily lock the change password feature for 15 minutes and display a lockout message.

5. If the password change is successful, Then the system must invalidate all other active sessions and send a confirmation email.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Change Password page displays Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password fields with a "Change Password" submit button.
2. Verify that entering the correct current password, a new password "SecureP@ss2026!", and confirming it results in a successful password change with a success message.
3. Verify that the password strength indicator shows "Weak" for "pass", "Fair" for "Pass1234", "Strong" for "Pass@1234", and "Very Strong" for "S3cure!P@ss#2026".
4. Verify that a confirmation email is sent to the patient's registered email after a successful password change.
5. Verify that all other active sessions are invalidated after the password change.

Negative:
6. Verify that entering an incorrect current password displays "Current password is incorrect" and blocks the change.
7. Verify that a new password "short" (missing uppercase, digit, special character, and under 8 characters) displays all missing requirement messages.
8. Verify that mismatched Confirm New Password displays "Passwords do not match".
9. Verify that setting the new password identical to the current password displays "New password must be different from the current password".
10. Verify that three failed current password attempts lock the feature for 15 minutes with a lockout message.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that each password field has a "Show/Hide" toggle icon that switches between masked and plain text display.
12. Verify that the password strength indicator is displayed below the New Password field with color-coded levels (red for Weak, orange for Fair, green for Strong, dark green for Very Strong).

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a password exactly 8 characters long meeting all requirements is accepted.
14. Verify that a password with 128 characters (maximum length) meeting all requirements is accepted without error.

Security:
15. Verify that the current password is validated server-side and is not compared client-side.
16. Verify that the new password is hashed before storage and the plain text is never logged or stored.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Change Password form submission and validation complete within 2 seconds.
Verify that session invalidation across all active sessions completes within 3 seconds after a successful password change.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view basic facility information
Description of User Story: The Facility Information section within Settings provides Approved Family Contacts with a read-only view of basic details about the healthcare facility where their linked family member(s) receive care. The page displays the facility name, facility type (e.g., Assisted Living Facility, Adult Day Health Center), full address with a map pin or embedded map preview, main phone number, fax number, email address, website URL, administrator or director name, operating hours for each day of the week, and the facility's license or certification number. If the family contact has members at multiple facilities, a facility selector or tabs allow switching between facility profiles. The information helps family contacts plan visits, contact the facility, and verify operational details. All information is sourced from the facility's administrative records and is not editable by the family contact.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact navigates to Facility Information, Then the system must display all available facility details in read-only format.

2. If the family contact has members at only one facility, Then the system must display that facility's information without a facility selector.
   Else if the family contact has members at multiple facilities, Then the system must display a facility selector with all linked facilities.

3. If a facility detail field is not available (e.g., fax number not provided), Then the system must display "Not available" for that field.

4. If the map service fails to load, Then the system must display the full text address without the map preview and not show a broken map widget.

5. If the facility is closed on a particular day, Then the operating hours must display "Closed" for that day instead of hours.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Facility Information page displays "Cambridge Home Health" as the facility name, type "Assisted Living Facility", full address, phone "(617) 555-0100", email, website, administrator "Dr. Sarah Johnson", and operating hours.
2. Verify that the embedded map preview shows the correct location pin for the facility address.
3. Verify that a family contact with members at "Cambridge Home Health" and "Sunrise Adult Day Center" can switch between facility profiles using tabs.
4. Verify that operating hours show Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM and Saturday-Sunday "Closed".
5. Verify that clicking the website URL opens the facility website in a new tab.

Negative:
6. Verify that when the fax number is not on record, the field displays "Not available".
7. Verify that when the map service fails to load, the facility address is still displayed as text without a broken map widget.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the Facility Information page is organized with the facility name and type as a header, followed by contact details, operating hours in a table format, and the map preview.
9. Verify that the facility selector (for multi-facility contacts) is displayed as a dropdown or tab bar at the top of the page.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a family contact linked to three different facilities can view information for all three by switching between them.
11. Verify that a facility with 24/7 operating hours displays "Open 24 Hours" for each day.

Security:
12. Verify that facility information is accessible only to authenticated Approved Family Contacts linked to that facility and is not publicly exposed.
13. Verify that the family contact cannot modify any facility details through the UI or API.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Facility Information page loads within 2 seconds including the embedded map preview.
Verify that switching between facility profiles loads the new facility's data within 1.5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to update my contact details (phone number, email, address) in my profile so that the facility always has current information.
Description of User Story: The Contact Details Update feature allows Approved Family Contacts to edit and save their own contact information directly from their profile page. The editable fields include primary phone number, secondary phone number, email address, and full residential address (street address, apartment/unit, city, state, ZIP code). When the family contact navigates to their profile and clicks "Edit" or an edit icon next to the contact section, the fields become editable with the current values pre-populated. Real-time validation checks phone number format, email format, and address completeness as the user types. A "Save" button submits the changes, and the system validates all fields before persisting the update. Upon successful save, a confirmation message is displayed and the facility's records are updated to reflect the new contact information. If the email address is changed, the system sends a verification email to the new address before finalizing the update. A "Cancel" button discards all changes and returns the fields to read-only mode with the original values.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks "Edit" on the contact details section, Then the system must make the phone, email, and address fields editable with current values pre-populated.

2. If the family contact saves a valid phone number update, Then the system must persist the change and display a success confirmation.
   Else if the phone number format is invalid, Then the system must display "Please enter a valid phone number" and block saving.
   Else if the phone number field is left empty, Then the system must display "Phone number is required" and block saving.

3. If the email address is changed, Then the system must send a verification email to the new address and display "A verification email has been sent to your new email address. Please verify to complete the change."
   Else if the verification email is not confirmed within 24 hours, Then the system must revert to the original email address.

4. If the address fields are incomplete (missing city, state, or ZIP), Then the system must highlight the missing fields and display "Please complete all address fields".

5. If the family contact clicks "Cancel" after making changes, Then the system must discard all unsaved changes and restore the original values.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Edit" makes phone number, email, and address fields editable with the current values pre-populated.
2. Verify that updating the phone number to "(555) 123-4567" and clicking "Save" persists the change and shows a success message.
3. Verify that changing the email to "newemail@example.com" triggers a verification email and displays the verification pending message.
4. Verify that updating the full address (street, city, state, ZIP) and saving persists all address fields correctly.
5. Verify that clicking "Cancel" after editing discards all changes and restores the original contact details.

Negative:
6. Verify that entering an invalid phone format "123" displays "Please enter a valid phone number" and blocks saving.
7. Verify that leaving the email field empty displays "Email is required" and blocks saving.
8. Verify that submitting an address with missing ZIP code highlights the field and displays "Please complete all address fields".
9. Verify that entering an invalid email format "notanemail" displays "Please enter a valid email address".

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that in read-only mode, the contact details are displayed with labels and values, and an "Edit" button or icon is visible.
11. Verify that in edit mode, the fields have input borders, the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are displayed, and real-time validation errors appear below invalid fields.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that if the email verification is not completed within 24 hours, the email reverts to the original address.
13. Verify that saving contact details with no actual changes still shows a success message without errors.

Security:
14. Verify that the family contact can only update their own contact details and cannot modify another user's profile through API manipulation.
15. Verify that the email verification link expires after 24 hours and cannot be reused.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that saving updated contact details completes within 2 seconds with a success confirmation displayed.
Verify that the verification email is sent within 30 seconds of saving a new email address.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view my profile photo, email, phone no, DOB, Gender and full address on the profile page.
Description of User Story: The Profile Overview section provides Approved Family Contacts with a read-only display of their personal information on the profile page. At the top of the page, the family contact's profile photo is displayed prominently as a circular avatar. Below the photo, the following personal details are shown in a structured layout: full name, email address, phone number, date of birth (DOB), gender, and full residential address (street, city, state, ZIP code). The information is sourced from the family contact's account record and is presented in a clean, card-based layout with clearly labeled fields. If a profile photo has not been uploaded, a default avatar with the user's initials is displayed. The profile overview serves as the landing section when the family contact navigates to their profile page, providing a quick summary of their identity and contact information before any other sections such as linked family members or account settings.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact navigates to the Profile page, Then the system must display the profile photo, email, phone, DOB, gender, and full address in read-only format.

2. If the family contact has a profile photo uploaded, Then the system must display the uploaded photo as a circular avatar.
   Else if no profile photo is on file, Then the system must display a default circular avatar with the user's initials.
   Else if the profile photo fails to load, Then the system must fall back to the initials avatar without showing a broken image.

3. If any personal detail field is not on record, Then the system must display "Not provided" for that field.

4. If the profile data fails to load due to a server error, Then the system must display "Unable to load profile. Please try again later." with a retry option.

5. If the date of birth format differs from the system locale, Then the system must display it in the portal's standard date format (MM/DD/YYYY).
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Profile page displays the profile photo of "Ralph Edwards" as a circular avatar at the top of the page.
2. Verify that the email "ralph.edwards@email.com", phone "(555) 987-6543", DOB "04/15/1965", gender "Male", and full address "123 Oak Street, Cambridge, MA 02139" are displayed below the photo.
3. Verify that all personal detail fields are read-only and not editable directly from this view.

Negative:
4. Verify that a family contact without a profile photo sees a default avatar with initials "RE".
5. Verify that when the gender field is not on record, it displays "Not provided".
6. Verify that when the profile service is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load profile. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that the Profile page layout shows the circular profile photo at the top center, followed by the full name in a large font, and personal details in a card layout with labeled fields.
8. Verify that the profile photo avatar has a consistent circular shape with a subtle border or shadow.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify that a family contact with a very long full name and address has all text displayed completely without truncation.
10. Verify that the DOB is displayed in MM/DD/YYYY format regardless of the stored format.

Security:
11. Verify that the family contact can only view their own profile and cannot access another user's profile data.
12. Verify that the profile photo URL is authenticated and not publicly accessible.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Profile page including the profile photo loads within 2 seconds.
Verify that the profile data renders without layout shift or flicker on page load.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view all linked family members with its status badge on my profile page showing each resident's name, program type (ADH/ALF), relationship, facility name, room number, and service type, so that I have a complete overview of my connections.
Description of User Story: The Linked Family Members section on the Approved Family Contact's profile page displays a comprehensive list of all residents linked to the contact's account. Each linked member entry is presented as a card or row containing the resident's full name, program type badge (ADH for Adult Day Health or ALF for Assisted Living Facility), the relationship to the family contact (e.g., Father, Mother, Spouse, Sibling), the facility name where the resident receives care, the room number assigned to the resident, and the service type (e.g., Full-Time Residential, Part-Time Day Program, Respite Care). A status badge is displayed on each entry indicating the current linkage or identity verification status such as "Verified", "Needs Attention", "Verify Identity", or "Pending Approval". The list is organized with active verified members first, followed by those requiring attention. The section provides a holistic view enabling the family contact to manage and monitor all their care connections from a single location.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact views the Linked Family Members section, Then the system must display all linked residents with name, program type, relationship, facility, room number, service type, and status badge.

2. If a linked member's status is "Verified", Then the system must display a green "Verified" badge.
   Else if a linked member's status is "Verify Identity", Then the system must display a yellow or blue "Verify Identity" badge with a clickable action.
   Else if a linked member's status is "Needs Attention", Then the system must display an orange "Needs Attention" badge.
   Else if a linked member's status is "Pending Approval", Then the system must display a gray "Pending Approval" badge.

3. If the family contact has no linked members, Then the system must display "No family members linked to your account. Contact the facility to set up family connections."

4. If a linked member's room number has changed, Then the system must display the current room number from the latest facility records.

5. If a linked member has been discharged or is no longer at the facility, Then the system must display a "Discharged" badge and move the entry to the bottom of the list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Linked Family Members section displays "Devon Michael Lane" with program type "ADH", relationship "Son", facility "Cambridge Home Health", room "204", and a "Verify Identity" badge.
2. Verify that "Linda Michael Lane" is displayed with program type "ALF", room "201", and a "Needs Attention" badge.
3. Verify that verified members appear above members with "Verify Identity" or "Needs Attention" badges in the list.
4. Verify that each linked member card shows all six data points: name, program type, relationship, facility, room number, and service type.

Negative:
5. Verify that a family contact with no linked members sees "No family members linked to your account. Contact the facility to set up family connections."
6. Verify that a linked member who has been discharged shows a "Discharged" badge and appears at the bottom of the list.

UI Scenarios:
7. Verify that linked members are displayed as individual cards with the resident name as the header, program type as a colored badge (blue for ADH, purple for ALF), and status badge in the top-right corner.
8. Verify that the relationship, facility name, room number, and service type are displayed as labeled rows within each card.

Edge Cases:
9. Verify that a family contact with 10 linked members can scroll through all entries without layout issues.
10. Verify that a linked member whose room number was recently changed displays the updated room number.

Security:
11. Verify that the family contact can only view linked members they are authorized to see and cannot access other residents' data.
12. Verify that the linked member data API enforces authorization and rejects requests for unlinked residents.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Linked Family Members section loads within 2 seconds for a family contact with up to 10 linked residents.
Verify that status badges and program type indicators render correctly without layout flicker on page load.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to see the count of Open Tickets, next visit date, family members on my profile page.
Description of User Story: The Profile Summary Cards section at the top of the Approved Family Contact's profile page displays three key metric cards providing an at-a-glance overview of important information. The "Family Members" card shows the total count of residents linked to the family contact's account. The "Open Tickets" card displays the number of currently open support or service tickets associated with the family contact or their linked members. The "Next Visit" card shows the date of the next scheduled visit to the facility. Each card is visually styled as a compact summary tile with a prominent numeric value or date, a descriptive label, and an optional icon. The cards are arranged horizontally at the top of the profile page, providing immediate context before the family contact scrolls to detailed sections below. Clicking a card navigates to the relevant detailed section (e.g., clicking "Open Tickets" navigates to the tickets list, clicking "Family Members" scrolls to the linked members section).
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Profile page loads, Then the system must display the three summary cards (Family Members, Open Tickets, Next Visit) with current data.

2. If the family contact has no linked family members, Then the Family Members card must display "0".
   Else if the family contact has no open tickets, Then the Open Tickets card must display "0".
   Else if no visit is scheduled, Then the Next Visit card must display "No upcoming visit".

3. If the data for any card fails to load, Then the card must display a loading indicator or "—" placeholder instead of incorrect data.

4. If the family contact clicks the Open Tickets card, Then the system must navigate to the tickets list filtered to show open tickets.
   Else if the family contact clicks the Family Members card, Then the system must scroll to or navigate to the Linked Family Members section.
   Else if the family contact clicks the Next Visit card, Then the system must navigate to the visits or scheduling section.

5. If a ticket is resolved after the page loads, Then the Open Tickets count must update on next page refresh to reflect the new count.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Profile page displays three summary cards at the top: "Family Members" showing "2", "Open Tickets" showing "3", and "Next Visit" showing "April 10, 2026".
2. Verify that clicking the "Open Tickets" card navigates to the tickets list showing 3 open tickets.
3. Verify that clicking the "Family Members" card scrolls to the Linked Family Members section on the Profile page.
4. Verify that clicking the "Next Visit" card navigates to the visits or scheduling section.

Negative:
5. Verify that a family contact with no linked members sees "0" on the Family Members card.
6. Verify that a family contact with no open tickets sees "0" on the Open Tickets card.
7. Verify that a family contact with no scheduled visits sees "No upcoming visit" on the Next Visit card.
8. Verify that when the ticket data service is unavailable, the Open Tickets card shows "—" as a placeholder.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the three summary cards are arranged horizontally at the top of the Profile page with distinct icons, labels, and prominent values.
10. Verify that each card has a hover effect indicating it is clickable and that the cursor changes to a pointer.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that resolving a ticket and refreshing the Profile page decrements the Open Tickets count from "3" to "2".
12. Verify that adding a new linked family member and refreshing the page increments the Family Members count.

Security:
13. Verify that the summary card data is specific to the logged-in family contact and does not include data from other users' accounts.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the three profile summary cards load with correct data within 1.5 seconds of page load.
Verify that clicking a summary card navigates to the relevant section within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to click an 'Edit' button on my profile page to update my personal information (name, phone, email, DOB, address).
Description of User Story: The Profile Edit feature allows Approved Family Contacts to modify their personal information by clicking an "Edit" button on the profile page. When the "Edit" button is clicked, the profile fields transition from read-only display to editable input fields pre-populated with current values. The editable fields include first name, last name, phone number, email address, date of birth, and full residential address (street, city, state, ZIP code). Each field has real-time validation: name fields require alphabetic characters only, phone requires valid format, email requires valid email format, DOB requires a valid date in the past, and address fields require completeness. A "Save" button submits the changes after validation, and a "Cancel" button discards changes and returns to read-only mode. Upon saving, the system displays a success notification, updates the profile data, and if the email was changed, triggers a verification email to the new address. The profile photo is not editable through this flow and has its own separate upload mechanism.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the "Edit" button, Then the system must transition all personal detail fields to editable mode with current values pre-populated.

2. If all edited fields pass validation and the family contact clicks "Save", Then the system must persist the changes and display a success notification.
   Else if any field fails validation, Then the system must highlight the invalid fields with error messages and block saving.

3. If the email address is changed, Then the system must send a verification email and display "Verification email sent to [new email]. Please verify to complete the change."
   Else if the email verification is not completed within 24 hours, Then the system must revert to the previous email address.

4. If the name fields contain non-alphabetic characters (numbers, special characters), Then the system must display "Name must contain only alphabetic characters".
   Else if the DOB is set to a future date, Then the system must display "Date of birth must be a past date".
   Else if the DOB makes the user under 18 years old, Then the system must display "User must be at least 18 years of age".

5. If the family contact clicks "Cancel" after making changes, Then the system must discard all unsaved edits and restore original values without any API call.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "Edit" button transitions all personal detail fields to editable inputs with current values for "Ralph Edwards" pre-populated.
2. Verify that updating the phone number to "(555) 222-3333" and clicking "Save" persists the change and displays a success notification.
3. Verify that changing the email to "ralph.new@email.com" triggers a verification email and shows "Verification email sent to ralph.new@email.com."
4. Verify that updating the address to "456 Elm Street, Boston, MA 02101" and saving persists all address fields correctly.
5. Verify that clicking "Cancel" after editing the name discards changes and shows the original name "Ralph Edwards".

Negative:
6. Verify that entering "Ralph123" in the first name field displays "Name must contain only alphabetic characters".
7. Verify that entering a future date for DOB displays "Date of birth must be a past date".
8. Verify that entering an invalid email "ralph@" displays "Please enter a valid email address" and blocks saving.
9. Verify that leaving the phone number field empty displays "Phone number is required".
10. Verify that entering a DOB that makes the user 15 years old displays "User must be at least 18 years of age".

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that in read-only mode the "Edit" button is displayed at the bottom of the profile section, and in edit mode "Save" and "Cancel" buttons replace it.
12. Verify that editable fields have visible input borders, and validation errors appear in red text below the respective fields.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that saving with no actual changes made still shows a success notification without errors.
14. Verify that if the email verification is not completed within 24 hours, the email reverts to the original "ralph.edwards@email.com".

Security:
15. Verify that profile updates are applied only to the authenticated family contact's own account and cannot modify another user's profile.
16. Verify that the "Save" action transmits data over HTTPS and the API rejects unauthenticated or cross-account update requests.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that clicking the "Edit" button transitions the profile fields to editable mode within 1 second.
Verify that saving profile changes completes and displays the success notification within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view my loved one's current medication list in the portal
Description of User Story: The Medication List feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a comprehensive view of their loved one's current and historical medications within the Family Portal. The screen displays a resident selector at the top showing the selected resident's name (e.g., "Devon Lane") with the ability to switch between linked residents. Below the selector, a tabbed interface presents three categories: Active, Pending, and Past medications. The Active tab (default) shows all currently prescribed medications in a table format with columns for Medication name, Rx (prescription number), Start Date, End Date, Diagnosis Code, Provider (displayed with an avatar), and Status shown as a color-coded badge ("Ongoing" in green). Each medication row is clickable for additional detail. A search bar allows the family contact to quickly locate a specific medication by name, and filter controls enable narrowing results by provider, status, or diagnosis code. The Pending tab lists medications awaiting approval or pharmacy fulfillment, and the Past tab shows discontinued or completed medication courses with their end dates and reasons for discontinuation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Medication section, Then the system must display the Active tab by default with all current medications loaded.

2. If the resident has active medications on record, Then the system must display them in a table sorted by start date descending.
   Else if the resident has no active medications, Then the system must display "No active medications found for this resident."
   Else if the resident has no medications in any category, Then all three tabs must show empty states with appropriate messages.

3. If the family contact switches to the Pending tab and pending medications exist, Then the system must display medications awaiting approval or fulfillment with their pending status.
   Else if no pending medications exist, Then the system must display "No pending medications at this time."

4. If the family contact enters a search term that matches no medications, Then the system must display "No medications match your search" with an option to clear the search.
   Else if the family contact applies filters that return no results, Then the system must display "No medications match the selected filters" with a clear-filters option.

5. If the medication data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load medication data. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Medication section displays the resident selector showing "Devon Lane" and the Active tab is selected by default.
2. Verify that the Active tab displays 5 medications in a table with correct Medication names, Rx numbers, Start Dates, End Dates, Diagnosis Codes, Provider avatars, and Status badges.
3. Verify that an ongoing medication displays a green "Ongoing" status badge in the Status column.
4. Verify that searching for "Lisinopril" in the search bar filters the table to show only the matching medication entry.
5. Verify that clicking the Pending tab displays 2 medications awaiting pharmacy fulfillment with pending status indicators.
6. Verify that clicking the Past tab displays 3 discontinued medications with end dates and discontinuation reasons.
7. Verify that clicking on a medication row opens a detail view showing dosage, frequency, prescribing provider, and pharmacy information.

Negative:
8. Verify that a resident with no active medications sees "No active medications found for this resident." in the Active tab.
9. Verify that searching for a non-existent medication name displays "No medications match your search" with a clear search option.
10. Verify that when the medication API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load medication data. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the Medication page displays the resident selector at the top, followed by Active | Pending | Past tabs, a search bar, filter controls, and the medication data table.
12. Verify that the Provider column displays an avatar image alongside the provider name and the Status column uses color-coded badges (green for Ongoing).

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a resident with 50+ active medications displays a paginated table without performance issues.
14. Verify that a medication with no end date displays a dash or "N/A" in the End Date column.

Security:
15. Verify that an Approved Family Contact can only view medications for residents they are linked to and cannot access another resident's medication data through URL manipulation.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Medication list loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 30 active medications.
Verify that switching between Active, Pending, and Past tabs refreshes the table within 1.5 seconds.
Verify that search and filter operations return results within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view my loved one's recorded vitals (e.g., blood pressure, temperature) in the portal
Description of User Story: The Vitals feature allows Approved Family Contacts to view their loved one's recorded vital signs within the Family Portal. The section presents vitals data in multiple view modes: a table view and a chart view. The table view lists recorded vitals chronologically with columns for date and time, vital type (blood pressure, temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, weight), recorded value, and a color-coded status indicator showing whether the reading is within normal range (green), borderline (yellow), or abnormal (red). The chart view provides a graphical representation of vital trends over time, allowing the family contact to visualize patterns and changes in specific vital signs. Each vital type can be selected individually or in combination for charting. An "Add Vitals" modal is available for authorized staff to enter new readings (family contacts have view-only access). The page supports date-range filtering to review vitals from specific periods and provides a summary card at the top showing the most recent readings for key vital signs.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Vitals section, Then the system must display the summary card with the most recent vital readings and the table view by default.

2. If the resident has recorded vitals, Then the system must display them in reverse chronological order with color-coded status indicators.
   Else if no vitals have been recorded for the resident, Then the system must display "No vitals data available for this resident."

3. If a vital reading is within the normal range for the vital type, Then the status indicator must be green.
   Else if a vital reading is borderline (slightly outside normal range), Then the status indicator must be yellow.
   Else if a vital reading is abnormal (significantly outside normal range), Then the status indicator must be red.

4. If the family contact switches to chart view and selects multiple vital types, Then the chart must display each type as a separate data series with distinct colors and a legend.
   Else if only one vital type is selected, Then the chart must display a single trend line with data points.

5. If the family contact applies a date-range filter that returns no results, Then the system must display "No vitals recorded for the selected period" with a clear-filter option.

6. If the vitals data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load vitals data. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Vitals section displays a summary card showing the most recent blood pressure (120/80 mmHg), temperature (98.6 F), heart rate (72 bpm), and oxygen saturation (98%).
2. Verify that the Vitals table lists 15 recorded entries in reverse chronological order with date/time, vital type, recorded value, and status indicators.
3. Verify that a blood pressure reading of 120/80 mmHg displays a green status indicator for normal range.
4. Verify that a temperature reading of 101.2 F displays a red status indicator for abnormal range.
5. Verify that switching to chart view shows a line chart with blood pressure trend data over the past 30 days.
6. Verify that selecting both heart rate and oxygen saturation in chart view displays two separate trend lines with a legend.
7. Verify that applying a date-range filter for March 1-31, 2026 updates the table to show only vitals recorded in that period.

Negative:
8. Verify that a resident with no recorded vitals sees "No vitals data available for this resident."
9. Verify that applying a date-range filter with no results displays "No vitals recorded for the selected period" with a clear-filter option.
10. Verify that when the vitals API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load vitals data. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the Vitals page displays a summary card at the top, view toggle (table/chart), date-range filter, and the data view below.
12. Verify that status indicators use consistent color coding: green for normal, yellow for borderline, and red for abnormal across all vital types.
13. Verify that the chart view displays labeled axes, data point tooltips on hover, and a legend identifying each vital type series.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that a resident with 200+ vitals records can paginate through the table view without performance issues.
15. Verify that a vital reading with a missing value displays a dash or "N/A" instead of a blank cell.

Security:
16. Verify that vitals data is accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident and cannot be accessed by unauthorized users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Vitals table loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 100 recorded vitals.
Verify that switching between table and chart views renders within 1.5 seconds.
Verify that the chart view renders trend data for 90 days of readings within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view my loved one's progress notes in the portal
Description of User Story: The Progress Notes feature allows Approved Family Contacts to view clinical progress notes documented by healthcare providers for their loved one. The section displays a chronological list of progress notes, each showing the note date, author (provider name and role), note type or category (e.g., Nursing Note, Physician Note, Therapy Note, Social Services Note), and a brief summary or excerpt. Clicking on a note expands or opens the full note content, which includes the complete narrative text, any associated care plan updates, vital signs recorded at the time of the note, and the provider's electronic signature. The list supports filtering by note type, author, and date range. A search bar allows the family contact to find notes containing specific keywords. The most recent notes appear at the top by default. A note count indicator shows the total number of notes available. Family contacts have read-only access and cannot add, edit, or delete progress notes.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to Progress Notes, Then the system must display all available notes in reverse chronological order with summaries.

2. If the resident has progress notes on record, Then the system must display them with date, author, type, and a brief excerpt.
   Else if no progress notes exist for the resident, Then the system must display "No progress notes available for this resident."

3. If the family contact filters by note type and matching notes exist, Then the system must display the filtered results with an updated note count.
   Else if the filter returns no results, Then the system must display "No notes match the selected criteria" with a clear-filter option.

4. If the family contact searches for a keyword that appears in multiple notes, Then the system must highlight the matching keyword in the search results.
   Else if the search term matches no notes, Then the system must display "No notes found for your search term."

5. If a progress note contains sensitive clinical information restricted by facility policy, Then the system must display a redacted version with a notice "Some content is restricted per facility policy."

6. If the notes data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load progress notes. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Progress Notes section displays a list of 10 notes in reverse chronological order with correct dates, authors, note types, and summary excerpts.
2. Verify that clicking on a Nursing Note dated March 28, 2026 expands to show the full narrative text, care plan updates, and the nurse's electronic signature.
3. Verify that filtering by note type "Physician Note" displays 3 matching notes and updates the note count indicator.
4. Verify that filtering by date range March 1-15, 2026 shows only notes from that period.
5. Verify that searching for "physical therapy" returns 2 notes containing that keyword.
6. Verify that the note count indicator shows "10 notes" when no filters are applied.

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no progress notes sees "No progress notes available for this resident."
8. Verify that filtering by a note type with no entries displays "No notes match the selected criteria" with a clear-filter button.
9. Verify that searching for a non-existent keyword displays "No notes found for your search term."
10. Verify that when the notes API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load progress notes. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the Progress Notes page displays a note count indicator, filter controls (note type, author, date range), a search bar, and the notes list with expandable rows.
12. Verify that each note entry shows the provider name and role, note type as a category tag, and a 2-line summary excerpt before expansion.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a resident with 100+ progress notes can paginate or infinitely scroll through the list without performance issues.
14. Verify that a note with restricted content displays a redacted view with "Some content is restricted per facility policy."

Security:
15. Verify that the family contact has read-only access and no options to add, edit, or delete progress notes are available.
16. Verify that progress notes are accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Progress Notes list loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 50 notes.
Verify that expanding a full note renders the complete content within 1 second.
Verify that search and filter operations return results within 1.5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view important alerts related to my loved one in the portal
Description of User Story: The Alerts feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a centralized view of important alerts and notifications related to their loved one's care. The alerts section displays a list of all active and resolved alerts, each showing the alert title, severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low), date and time of the alert, category (Clinical, Administrative, Safety, Medication), a brief description, and the current status (Active, Acknowledged, Resolved). Critical and high-severity alerts are visually prominent with color-coded badges and positioned at the top of the list. Each alert can be expanded to view full details including the triggering event, actions taken by staff, resolution notes (if resolved), and any follow-up recommendations. The section supports filtering by severity, category, status, and date range. An alert count badge on the navigation menu indicates the number of unread or active alerts. Alerts are displayed in reverse chronological order, with the most recent at the top. Family contacts receive push notifications or emails for critical and high-severity alerts based on their notification preferences.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Alerts section, Then the system must display all alerts in reverse chronological order with critical and high-severity active alerts prioritized at the top.

2. If the resident has active alerts, Then the system must display them with severity badges and visual prominence for Critical (red) and High (orange) severity.
   Else if no alerts exist for the resident, Then the system must display "No alerts for this resident at this time."

3. If a critical alert is generated, Then the system must send a push notification or email to the family contact based on their notification preferences.
   Else if a high-severity alert is generated, Then the system must send a notification within 15 minutes.
   Else if a medium or low-severity alert is generated, Then the system must include it in the daily alert summary.

4. If the family contact expands a resolved alert, Then the system must display the resolution notes, date of resolution, and the staff member who resolved it.
   Else if the alert is still active, Then the system must display the current actions being taken.

5. If the family contact applies filters that return no results, Then the system must display "No alerts match the selected criteria" with a clear-filter option.

6. If the alerts data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load alerts. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Alerts section displays 8 alerts in reverse chronological order with critical alerts "Fall Risk Detected" and "Medication Interaction" at the top.
2. Verify that a Critical alert displays a red severity badge, and a High alert displays an orange severity badge.
3. Verify that expanding the "Fall Risk Detected" alert shows the triggering event, actions taken by nursing staff, and a follow-up recommendation for physical therapy assessment.
4. Verify that filtering by status "Active" displays 4 active alerts and hides resolved ones.
5. Verify that filtering by category "Medication" displays 2 medication-related alerts.
6. Verify that the navigation menu badge shows "4" for 4 active unread alerts.

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no alerts sees "No alerts for this resident at this time."
8. Verify that filtering by severity "Critical" when no critical alerts exist displays "No alerts match the selected criteria" with a clear-filter button.
9. Verify that when the alerts API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load alerts. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Alerts page displays filter controls (severity, category, status, date range) at the top, followed by the alert list with expandable cards showing title, severity badge, date/time, category, description, and status.
11. Verify that severity badges use consistent color coding: red for Critical, orange for High, yellow for Medium, and blue for Low.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that a resident with 50+ alerts can scroll or paginate through the list without performance issues.
13. Verify that a resolved alert that was previously critical still shows the red severity badge with a "Resolved" status tag.

Security:
14. Verify that alerts are accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident.
15. Verify that critical alert notifications are sent only to the designated family contact and not to unauthorized parties.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Alerts section loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 30 alerts.
Verify that expanding an alert detail renders the full content within 1 second.
Verify that critical alert push notifications are delivered within 30 seconds of alert generation.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view my loved one's allergy information in the portal
Description of User Story: The Allergies feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a comprehensive view of their loved one's documented allergy information. The section displays a list of all known allergies categorized by type: Drug Allergies, Food Allergies, Environmental Allergies, and Other Allergies. Each allergy entry shows the allergen name, allergy type, severity (Mild, Moderate, Severe, Life-Threatening), reaction description (e.g., rash, anaphylaxis, swelling), date of onset or when it was first recorded, and the status (Active, Inactive, Resolved). Severe and life-threatening allergies are visually highlighted with prominent warning indicators. The section includes a summary banner at the top listing the count of active allergies by severity level. Each allergy entry can be expanded to view additional details such as the documenting provider, any cross-reactivity notes, treatment protocols for allergic reactions, and historical reactions. Family contacts have read-only access and cannot modify allergy records.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Allergies section, Then the system must display all documented allergies categorized by type with a summary banner.

2. If the resident has documented allergies, Then the system must display them grouped by allergy type with severity badges.
   Else if no allergies are documented for the resident, Then the system must display "No known allergies documented for this resident" with a "NKA" (No Known Allergies) indicator.

3. If an allergy has a severity of "Life-Threatening", Then the system must display it with a red warning icon and position it at the top of its category.
   Else if an allergy has a severity of "Severe", Then the system must display it with an orange warning badge.
   Else if an allergy is "Moderate" or "Mild", Then the system must display it with a standard severity indicator.

4. If an allergy has cross-reactivity notes, Then the expanded detail must prominently display the cross-reactive substances.
   Else if no cross-reactivity information is available, Then the expanded detail must show "No cross-reactivity information documented."

5. If the allergies data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load allergy information. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Allergies section displays a summary banner showing "1 Life-Threatening, 2 Severe, 3 Moderate, 1 Mild" active allergies.
2. Verify that the Drug Allergies category lists "Penicillin" with severity "Life-Threatening", reaction "Anaphylaxis", and a red warning indicator.
3. Verify that the Food Allergies category lists "Peanuts" with severity "Severe", reaction "Swelling, difficulty breathing", and an orange badge.
4. Verify that expanding the Penicillin allergy shows the documenting provider, cross-reactivity notes for Amoxicillin and Ampicillin, treatment protocol for anaphylaxis, and date first recorded.
5. Verify that filtering by allergy type "Environmental Allergies" displays 2 environmental allergens.
6. Verify that the summary banner updates to reflect filtered results when a type filter is applied.

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no documented allergies sees "No known allergies documented for this resident" with an "NKA" indicator.
8. Verify that when the allergies API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load allergy information. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the Allergies page displays a summary banner at the top with severity counts, followed by allergy entries grouped under Drug, Food, Environmental, and Other categories.
10. Verify that life-threatening allergies display a red warning icon, severe allergies an orange badge, and moderate/mild allergies use standard indicators.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that an allergy with status "Inactive" or "Resolved" is displayed with a muted visual style and does not appear in the active allergy count.
12. Verify that an allergy entry with no cross-reactivity information shows "No cross-reactivity information documented." in the expanded view.

Security:
13. Verify that the family contact has read-only access and no options to add, edit, or delete allergy records are available.
14. Verify that allergy data is accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Allergies section loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 20 documented allergies.
Verify that expanding an allergy detail renders the full information within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view incident reports related to my loved one in the portal
Description of User Story: The Incidents feature allows Approved Family Contacts to view incident reports documented for their loved one within the facility. The section displays incidents in a card-based layout organized under two tabs: Active and Resolved. Each incident card shows the incident title (e.g., "Medication Delay"), a severity badge color-coded by level (red for "High", orange for "Medium", green for "Low"), the date and time of the incident, the location where it occurred (e.g., "Dining Hall"), and a brief description excerpt. Clicking on an incident card opens a detail panel showing the full incident information including the incident number (e.g., #I21234), comprehensive description, staff involved, actions taken, outcome, and any follow-up items. The Active tab lists incidents that are still under investigation or pending resolution, while the Resolved tab shows completed incidents with resolution details. The section supports date-range filtering and search by incident title or number. Family contacts have read-only access to incident reports shared by the facility.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Incidents section, Then the system must display the Active tab by default with all active incident cards loaded.

2. If the resident has active incidents, Then the system must display them as cards sorted by date descending with severity badges.
   Else if no active incidents exist, Then the system must display "No active incidents for this resident."

3. If the family contact clicks the Resolved tab and resolved incidents exist, Then the system must display completed incident cards with resolution dates and outcomes.
   Else if no resolved incidents exist, Then the system must display "No resolved incidents found."

4. If an incident has a "High" severity, Then the card must display a red severity badge.
   Else if the severity is "Medium", Then the card must display an orange severity badge.
   Else if the severity is "Low", Then the card must display a green severity badge.

5. If the family contact searches for an incident number or title that does not exist, Then the system must display "No incidents match your search" with a clear-search option.

6. If the incidents data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load incident reports. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Incidents section displays the Active tab by default with 3 active incident cards.
2. Verify that the "Medication Delay" incident card shows a red "High" severity badge, date "March 30, 2026 2:15 PM", location "Dining Hall", and a brief description excerpt.
3. Verify that clicking the "Medication Delay" card opens a detail panel showing incident #I21234, full description, involved staff names, actions taken, outcome, and follow-up recommendations.
4. Verify that the Resolved tab displays 5 completed incident cards with resolution dates and outcome summaries.
5. Verify that searching for "I21234" returns the matching incident card.
6. Verify that filtering by date range March 1-31, 2026 shows only incidents from that period.

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no active incidents sees "No active incidents for this resident." in the Active tab.
8. Verify that a resident with no resolved incidents sees "No resolved incidents found." in the Resolved tab.
9. Verify that searching for a non-existent incident number displays "No incidents match your search" with a clear-search option.
10. Verify that when the incidents API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load incident reports. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
11. Verify that the Incidents page displays Active | Resolved tabs, a search bar, date-range filter, and incident cards in a card-based layout.
12. Verify that severity badges use consistent color coding: red for High, orange for Medium, and green for Low.
13. Verify that the detail panel displays the incident number prominently at the top, followed by structured sections for description, staff, actions, outcome, and follow-up.

Edge Cases:
14. Verify that a resident with 30+ incidents can scroll or paginate through cards without performance issues.
15. Verify that an incident with no follow-up items displays "No follow-up items documented" in the detail panel.

Security:
16. Verify that incident reports are accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident.
17. Verify that family contacts have read-only access and cannot modify or delete incident reports.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Incidents section loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 20 active incidents.
Verify that opening an incident detail panel renders the full content within 1 second.
Verify that switching between Active and Resolved tabs refreshes the view within 1.5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view the inventory of my loved one's personal belongings tracked by the facility
Description of User Story: The Inventory feature allows Approved Family Contacts to view the personal belongings of their loved one that are tracked and managed by the facility. The section presents items in a grid view with item photos, name, and serial number for each belonging. Each item card displays a thumbnail photo of the belonging, the item name, the serial number or identification code, the category (Clothing, Electronics, Medical Equipment, Personal Items), condition status (Good, Fair, Needs Replacement), and the date it was logged into the facility's inventory system. An "Add Inventory" modal is available for authorized staff to register new items (family contacts have view-only access). The family contact can switch between grid and list/detail views. The list view provides a more compact format with sortable columns, while the detail view shows comprehensive information for a selected item including description, storage location, value estimate, and photo gallery. A search bar allows locating items by name or serial number, and filters support narrowing by category and condition.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Inventory section, Then the system must display all tracked personal belongings in the grid view by default.

2. If the resident has tracked belongings, Then the system must display them as item cards with photos, names, and serial numbers.
   Else if no belongings are tracked for the resident, Then the system must display "No personal belongings are currently tracked for this resident."

3. If an item has a condition of "Needs Replacement", Then the system must display a visual indicator such as an orange or red badge on the item card.
   Else if the condition is "Good", Then the system must display a green condition indicator.
   Else if the condition is "Fair", Then the system must display a yellow condition indicator.

4. If the family contact searches for an item that does not exist in the inventory, Then the system must display "No items match your search" with a clear-search option.
   Else if filters are applied that return no results, Then the system must display "No items match the selected filters" with a clear-filter option.

5. If an item has no photo uploaded, Then the system must display a default placeholder image for that item category.

6. If the inventory data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load inventory data. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Inventory section displays a grid view with 8 personal belonging cards showing photos, item names, and serial numbers.
2. Verify that an item card for "iPad Pro" shows a photo thumbnail, serial number "SN-20260101-004", category "Electronics", condition "Good" with a green indicator, and date logged "January 15, 2026".
3. Verify that switching to list view displays items in a sortable table with columns for Photo, Name, Serial Number, Category, Condition, and Date Logged.
4. Verify that clicking on "iPad Pro" opens the detail view showing description, storage location "Room 204 - Drawer B", value estimate "$800.00", and a photo gallery.
5. Verify that searching for "SN-20260101-004" returns the matching item.
6. Verify that filtering by category "Clothing" displays 3 clothing items.

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no tracked belongings sees "No personal belongings are currently tracked for this resident."
8. Verify that searching for a non-existent serial number displays "No items match your search" with a clear-search option.
9. Verify that when the inventory API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load inventory data. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Inventory page displays a view toggle (grid/list), search bar, category and condition filter controls, and the items view below.
11. Verify that grid view cards show the item photo prominently with name and serial number below, and condition badges use color coding (green for Good, yellow for Fair, orange/red for Needs Replacement).
12. Verify that an item with no photo displays a category-appropriate placeholder image.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a resident with 50+ tracked items can scroll or paginate through the grid without performance issues.
14. Verify that an item with a very long name or description is properly truncated in grid view with full text visible in detail view.

Security:
15. Verify that inventory data is accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident.
16. Verify that family contacts have read-only access and the "Add Inventory" modal is not accessible to them.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Inventory grid view loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 30 tracked items including photo thumbnails.
Verify that switching between grid and list views renders within 1.5 seconds.
Verify that the detail view with photo gallery loads within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view the upcoming events and activity calendar for my loved one in the portal
Description of User Story: The Events feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a view of upcoming events, activities, and scheduled programs for their loved one at the facility. The section displays an activity calendar showing daily, weekly, and monthly views of scheduled events. Each event entry shows the event title, date and time, location, event type or category (Recreational, Therapeutic, Social, Educational, Religious), duration, and a brief description. Events can be color-coded by category for easy visual identification. A list view alongside or below the calendar shows upcoming events in chronological order with all key details visible at a glance. Clicking on an event opens the full details including the complete description, facilitator or staff leading the event, maximum capacity, current enrollment count, any special requirements or instructions, and whether the resident is confirmed as attending. The family contact can filter events by category, date range, and participation status (Attending, Not Attending, Pending). A notification option allows the family contact to opt in for reminders about events the resident is scheduled to attend.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Events section, Then the system must display the activity calendar in the weekly view by default with upcoming events loaded.

2. If the resident has upcoming scheduled events, Then the system must display them on the calendar and in the list view.
   Else if no upcoming events exist, Then the system must display "No upcoming events scheduled for this resident."

3. If the family contact switches between daily, weekly, and monthly calendar views, Then the system must re-render the calendar with appropriate event data for the selected view.

4. If an event has reached maximum capacity, Then the system must display a "Full" indicator and the resident's waitlist position if applicable.
   Else if the event has available spots, Then the system must display the current enrollment count and remaining capacity.

5. If the family contact filters by participation status "Attending" and the resident has confirmed events, Then only those events must be shown.
   Else if no events match the filter, Then the system must display "No events match the selected criteria" with a clear-filter option.

6. If the events data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load events data. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Events section displays a weekly activity calendar with 6 upcoming events color-coded by category.
2. Verify that the list view shows upcoming events in chronological order with titles, dates, times, locations, categories, and durations.
3. Verify that a "Group Art Therapy" event displays under the Therapeutic category with a corresponding color, date "April 5, 2026 10:00 AM", location "Activity Room B", and duration "1 hour".
4. Verify that clicking on "Group Art Therapy" shows the full description, facilitator "Sarah Johnson, OTR", capacity "12/15", special requirements "Wear comfortable clothing", and resident status "Attending".
5. Verify that filtering by category "Recreational" displays 3 recreational events.
6. Verify that switching to the monthly calendar view shows all April 2026 events.

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no upcoming events sees "No upcoming events scheduled for this resident."
8. Verify that filtering by a category with no events displays "No events match the selected criteria" with a clear-filter button.
9. Verify that when the events API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load events data. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Events page displays a calendar with daily/weekly/monthly view toggle, category filters, and a list view of upcoming events.
11. Verify that events on the calendar are color-coded by category with a legend visible for reference.
12. Verify that an event at full capacity displays a "Full" badge and events with availability show the enrollment fraction (e.g., "12/15").

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a calendar month with 30+ scheduled events renders all events without overlapping or layout issues.
14. Verify that an event spanning multiple days (e.g., a weekend retreat) displays correctly across the relevant calendar days.

Security:
15. Verify that event data and resident participation status are accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident.
16. Verify that family contacts cannot modify the resident's event enrollment or attendance status.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Events calendar loads within 2 seconds for a month with up to 40 scheduled events.
Verify that switching between daily, weekly, and monthly views renders within 1.5 seconds.
Verify that opening event details loads the full information within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view summary of daily participation
Description of User Story: The Daily Participation Summary feature provides Approved Family Contacts with an overview of their loved one's daily engagement and participation in scheduled activities, therapies, and programs at the facility. The section displays a day-by-day summary showing the date, total number of scheduled activities for that day, number of activities the resident participated in, number of activities missed or declined, and an overall participation rate as a percentage. Each daily entry can be expanded to show the individual activities with their time, name, category, participation status (Attended, Missed, Declined, Excused), and any staff notes regarding participation. A weekly and monthly aggregate view provides participation trends over time, displayed as a bar chart or summary statistics. The family contact can filter by date range and activity category. A participation score or trend indicator (improving, stable, declining) is displayed at the top to give a quick assessment of the resident's engagement level. This feature helps family contacts stay informed about their loved one's daily life and level of engagement at the facility.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Daily Participation Summary, Then the system must display the participation trend indicator and daily summaries in reverse chronological order.

2. If the resident has participation data, Then the system must display daily summaries with activity counts and participation rates.
   Else if no participation data is available, Then the system must display "No participation data available for this resident."

3. If the resident's participation trend is improving (higher rates over recent weeks), Then the trend indicator must display "Improving" with an upward arrow in green.
   Else if the trend is stable, Then the indicator must display "Stable" with a horizontal arrow in blue.
   Else if the trend is declining, Then the indicator must display "Declining" with a downward arrow in orange.

4. If a daily entry shows 0% participation, Then the system must highlight the day with a visual indicator and display the reasons from staff notes.
   Else if participation is 100%, Then the system must display a green checkmark badge for that day.

5. If the family contact applies filters that return no data, Then the system must display "No participation data for the selected criteria" with a clear-filter option.

6. If the participation data service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Unable to load participation data. Please try again later." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Daily Participation Summary displays a trend indicator showing "Stable" with a blue horizontal arrow and 14 daily summary entries.
2. Verify that the entry for April 1, 2026 shows 6 scheduled activities, 5 attended, 1 missed, and a participation rate of 83%.
3. Verify that expanding April 1, 2026 shows individual activities: "Morning Exercise" (Attended), "Art Therapy" (Attended), "Group Lunch" (Attended), "Music Session" (Attended), "Evening Social" (Attended), and "Afternoon Walk" (Missed) with staff note "Resident declined due to fatigue".
4. Verify that switching to the weekly aggregate view shows a bar chart of participation rates for each of the past 4 weeks.
5. Verify that the monthly aggregate view shows an average participation rate of 78% for March 2026.
6. Verify that filtering by category "Therapeutic" updates the summary to reflect only therapeutic activity participation data.

Negative:
7. Verify that a resident with no participation data sees "No participation data available for this resident."
8. Verify that filtering by a date range with no data displays "No participation data for the selected criteria" with a clear-filter option.
9. Verify that when the participation API is unavailable, the page shows "Unable to load participation data. Please try again later." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Daily Participation Summary page displays a participation trend indicator at the top, view toggles (daily/weekly/monthly), date-range and category filters, and the summary data below.
11. Verify that daily entries with 100% participation show a green checkmark badge and entries with 0% show a red highlight.
12. Verify that the bar chart in the weekly/monthly view uses color-coded bars (green for high participation, yellow for moderate, red for low).

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a resident with 90 days of participation data can scroll through daily summaries without performance issues.
14. Verify that a day with no scheduled activities displays "No activities scheduled" instead of a 0% participation rate.

Security:
15. Verify that participation data is accessible only to Approved Family Contacts linked to the specific resident.
16. Verify that family contacts have read-only access and cannot modify participation records or staff notes.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Daily Participation Summary loads within 2 seconds for a resident with up to 30 days of data.
Verify that switching between daily, weekly, and monthly views renders within 1.5 seconds.
Verify that the bar chart for monthly aggregate data renders within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to create a new ticket by filling in a form with Title and Body (description), selecting the linked resident, and submitting, so that I can report issues to the facility.
Description of User Story: The Create Ticket feature allows Approved Family Contacts to submit support tickets to the facility on behalf of a linked resident. From the Tickets page, clicking the blue "+ Create Ticket" button opens the "Create New Ticket" modal. The modal form contains the following fields: Resident Name (auto-filled based on the linked resident, but selectable if the family contact is linked to multiple residents), Ticket Title (required, free-text input), Priority (required, dropdown with options such as Low, Medium, High, Urgent), and Message (required, textarea for the detailed description of the issue). The form enforces validation on all required fields before submission. Upon clicking "Save", the system creates the ticket, assigns it a unique Ticket ID following the format "#TKT-XXXXX" (e.g., #TKT-10102), sets the initial status to "Open", records the submission date and time, and associates the ticket with the selected resident. A success notification confirms ticket creation and the new ticket appears at the top of the Open tickets list. Clicking "Cancel" closes the modal without saving.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact clicks "+ Create Ticket", Then the system must open the "Create New Ticket" modal with Resident Name auto-filled and all required fields empty and ready for input.
