2. If the family contact fills in all required fields (Ticket Title, Priority, Message) and clicks "Save", Then the system must create the ticket with a unique Ticket ID in "#TKT-XXXXX" format, set the status to "Open", and display a success notification.
   Else if the Ticket Title field is left empty, Then the system must display a validation error "Ticket Title is required" and prevent submission.
   Else if the Priority field is not selected, Then the system must display a validation error "Priority is required" and prevent submission.
   Else if the Message field is left empty, Then the system must display a validation error "Message is required" and prevent submission.

3. If the family contact is linked to multiple residents, Then the Resident Name field must display a dropdown allowing selection of the target resident.
   Else if the family contact is linked to only one resident, Then the Resident Name field must be auto-filled and read-only.

4. If the ticket creation fails due to a server error, Then the system must display "Unable to create ticket. Please try again." with a retry option and must not lose the entered form data.

5. If the family contact clicks "Cancel" after entering data, Then the system must close the modal without saving and discard all entered data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "+ Create Ticket" button opens the "Create New Ticket" modal with Resident Name auto-filled as "Devon Lane", and empty fields for Ticket Title, Priority dropdown, and Message textarea.
2. Verify that filling in Ticket Title "Room temperature issue", selecting Priority "High", entering Message "The heating in Room 204 is not working properly", and clicking "Save" creates a ticket with a unique Ticket ID in #TKT-XXXXX format and shows a success notification.
3. Verify that after ticket creation the new ticket appears at the top of the Open tab with the correct title, Ticket ID, "Open" status badge, and current date/time.
4. Verify that a family contact linked to multiple residents can select the appropriate resident from the Resident Name dropdown.

Negative:
5. Verify that submitting the form with an empty Ticket Title displays "Ticket Title is required" and blocks submission.
6. Verify that submitting the form without selecting a Priority displays "Priority is required" and blocks submission.
7. Verify that submitting the form with an empty Message displays "Message is required" and blocks submission.
8. Verify that when the ticket creation API fails, the error message "Unable to create ticket. Please try again." is displayed and the form data is preserved.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the "Create New Ticket" modal displays a clean form layout with labeled fields: Resident Name (auto-filled), Ticket Title*, Priority* dropdown, Message* textarea, and "Cancel" and "Save" buttons at the bottom.
10. Verify that the blue "+ Create Ticket" button is prominently visible on the Tickets page.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a ticket title with 200 characters is accepted and displayed without truncation in the creation confirmation.
12. Verify that a message body with 2000 characters is accepted and saved completely.
13. Verify that clicking "Cancel" after filling in all fields closes the modal and discards all entered data.

Security:
14. Verify that only Approved Family Contacts can access the Create Ticket functionality and unauthenticated users are denied access.
15. Verify that the ticket creation API validates the family contact's authorization to create tickets for the selected resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the "Create New Ticket" modal opens within 1 second after clicking the "+ Create Ticket" button.
Verify that ticket submission and confirmation display complete within 3 seconds under normal server conditions.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to track the status and response of any support ticket I have submitted.
Description of User Story: The Ticket Tracking feature enables Approved Family Contacts to monitor the lifecycle of their submitted support tickets from creation through resolution. Each ticket displays its current status, which progresses through defined stages such as Open, In Progress, Awaiting Response, and Closed. The ticket detail view shows a timeline or activity log of all status changes, including the date, time, and the staff member or system that changed the status. When facility staff respond to a ticket, the response appears in the ticket detail as a threaded message with the responder's name, role, timestamp, and message content. The family contact receives a notification (in-app or email) when a ticket status changes or a new response is added. The ticket detail panel also displays the current assignee handling the ticket, the priority level, and the expected response time based on the priority. This allows the family contact to stay informed about the progress of their reported issues without needing to call the facility.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact opens a submitted ticket, Then the system must display the current status, activity timeline, staff responses, assignee, priority, and expected response time.

2. If the ticket status is "Open" and no staff has been assigned, Then the system must display "Pending Assignment" in the assignee field.
   Else if the ticket is "In Progress", Then the system must display the assigned staff member's name and role.
   Else if the ticket is "Awaiting Response", Then the system must display a prompt indicating the facility is waiting for the family contact's reply.

3. If a new staff response is added to the ticket, Then the system must send a notification to the family contact and display the response in the ticket's message thread.
   Else if the ticket status changes, Then the system must update the activity log and send a notification.

4. If the ticket has been open longer than the expected response time, Then the system must display a visual indicator such as "Response overdue" on the ticket.

5. If the ticket detail fails to load, Then the system must display "Unable to load ticket details. Please try again." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that opening ticket #TKT-10102 displays the current status "In Progress", assigned staff "Dr. Sarah Miller - Facility Manager", priority "High", and expected response time "24 hours".
2. Verify that the activity timeline shows status changes: "Open" on March 30, 2026 at 10:00 AM, "In Progress" on March 30, 2026 at 2:15 PM with assignee name.
3. Verify that a staff response from "Dr. Sarah Miller" with timestamp and message "We have dispatched maintenance to address the issue" appears in the ticket thread.
4. Verify that when the ticket status changes from "In Progress" to "Awaiting Response", the family contact receives an in-app notification.
5. Verify that a ticket with an email notification preference sends an email when a new response is added.

Negative:
6. Verify that a ticket with no staff assigned displays "Pending Assignment" in the assignee field.
7. Verify that when the ticket detail API is unavailable, the message "Unable to load ticket details. Please try again." is shown with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the ticket detail panel displays the status badge, assignee card, priority level, expected response time, activity timeline, and threaded messages in a clear, organized layout.
9. Verify that status changes in the timeline are displayed with icons, timestamps, and descriptions in chronological order.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a ticket open longer than the expected response time displays a "Response overdue" indicator.
11. Verify that a ticket with 20+ staff responses displays all messages in a scrollable thread without performance degradation.

Security:
12. Verify that an Approved Family Contact can only view tickets they have submitted and cannot access other users' tickets through URL or API manipulation.
13. Verify that ticket responses from staff are visible only to the submitting family contact and not to other family contacts linked to the same resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the ticket detail panel with activity timeline and staff responses loads within 2 seconds.
Verify that real-time notifications for status changes are delivered within 5 seconds of the status update.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to toggle between 'Open' and 'Closed' tabs on the Tickets page, so that I can view active and resolved tickets separately.
Description of User Story: The Tickets page provides two distinct tabs at the top of the ticket list area: "Open" and "Closed". The Open tab displays all tickets that have an active status (Open, In Progress, Awaiting Response), while the Closed tab displays all tickets that have been resolved and marked as Closed. The currently selected tab is visually highlighted to indicate which view is active. When the family contact toggles between tabs, the ticket list updates immediately to show only tickets matching the selected status category. The count of tickets in each tab is displayed alongside the tab label (e.g., "Open (5)" and "Closed (12)") to give a quick overview without needing to click. The default view when landing on the Tickets page is the Open tab. Each tab maintains its own scroll position independently so that switching back and forth does not lose the user's place.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact navigates to the Tickets page, Then the system must display the Open tab as the default selected tab with all active tickets loaded.

2. If the Open tab has no tickets, Then the system must display "No open tickets" with an option to create a new ticket.
   Else if the Closed tab has no tickets, Then the system must display "No closed tickets" message.

3. If the family contact switches between tabs rapidly, Then the system must cancel any pending data requests and load only the data for the most recently selected tab.

4. If the ticket count changes while the user is on the page (e.g., a ticket is closed by staff), Then the tab counts must update to reflect the latest numbers.

5. If the ticket list fails to load when switching tabs, Then the system must display "Unable to load tickets. Please try again." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Tickets page loads with the "Open" tab selected by default, showing 5 active tickets.
2. Verify that clicking the "Closed" tab switches the list to display 12 closed tickets.
3. Verify that clicking back to the "Open" tab restores the list of active tickets.
4. Verify that the tab labels show accurate counts: "Open (5)" and "Closed (12)".
5. Verify that the Open tab includes tickets with statuses Open, In Progress, and Awaiting Response.

Negative:
6. Verify that when there are no open tickets, the Open tab displays "No open tickets" with a "+ Create Ticket" option.
7. Verify that when there are no closed tickets, the Closed tab displays "No closed tickets".
8. Verify that if the ticket list API fails on tab switch, the message "Unable to load tickets. Please try again." is displayed with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the currently selected tab is visually highlighted (e.g., bold text, underline, or contrasting background) to clearly indicate the active view.
10. Verify that the Open and Closed tabs are positioned at the top of the ticket list area with ticket counts displayed in parentheses next to each label.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that rapidly toggling between Open and Closed tabs does not cause duplicate or mixed ticket data to display.
12. Verify that scroll position is preserved independently for each tab when switching back and forth.
13. Verify that when a ticket is closed by staff while the family contact is on the Open tab, the tab counts update to reflect the change.

Security:
14. Verify that the ticket list in both tabs only contains tickets submitted by the logged-in family contact and does not expose other users' tickets.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that switching between the Open and Closed tabs loads the corresponding ticket list within 1 second.
Verify that the tab counts update within 2 seconds when ticket statuses change in the background.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view ticket cards showing Ticket ID, title, brief description, status badge (Open/Closed), date, and time, so that I can quickly scan all my tickets.
Description of User Story: Each ticket on the Tickets page is rendered as a card component that provides a scannable summary of the ticket at a glance. The ticket card displays the following information: Ticket ID in the format "#TKT-XXXXX" (e.g., #TKT-10102), the ticket title, a brief description (first 100-150 characters of the message body, truncated with an ellipsis if longer), a color-coded status badge (red for "Open", green for "Closed"), and the submission date and time. The cards are listed vertically in reverse chronological order with the most recently submitted or updated ticket at the top. The card layout is consistent across both the Open and Closed tabs. Each card is clickable, allowing the family contact to navigate to the full ticket detail panel. The design uses clear typography and spacing to ensure readability, with the Ticket ID and status badge prominently visible for quick identification.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the ticket list loads on the Tickets page, Then the system must render each ticket as a card showing Ticket ID, title, truncated description, status badge, and submission date/time.

2. If the ticket status is "Open", Then the status badge must be displayed in red with the text "Open".
   Else if the ticket status is "Closed", Then the status badge must be displayed in green with the text "Closed".

3. If the ticket message body is longer than 150 characters, Then the card must truncate the description at 100-150 characters and append an ellipsis ("...").
   Else if the message body is 150 characters or fewer, Then the card must display the full description without truncation.

4. If there are no tickets to display in the current tab, Then the system must display an appropriate empty state message instead of blank cards.

5. If the ticket data fails to load, Then the system must display "Unable to load tickets. Please try again." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that ticket card for #TKT-10102 displays the Ticket ID "#TKT-10102", title "Room temperature issue", a brief description, red "Open" status badge, and submission date "March 30, 2026 10:00 AM".
2. Verify that ticket card for a closed ticket displays the Ticket ID, title, description, green "Closed" status badge, and date/time.
3. Verify that a ticket with a 300-character message body shows a truncated description ending with "..." on the card.
4. Verify that a ticket with a 100-character message body shows the full description on the card without truncation.
5. Verify that ticket cards are listed in reverse chronological order with the most recently submitted ticket at the top.

Negative:
6. Verify that when no tickets exist under the selected tab, an empty state message is displayed instead of blank cards.
7. Verify that when the ticket data API is unavailable, the message "Unable to load tickets. Please try again." is shown with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that each ticket card has a consistent layout with Ticket ID and status badge prominently visible at the top, title in bold, truncated description below, and date/time at the bottom or side.
9. Verify that the red "Open" badge and green "Closed" badge have sufficient color contrast for accessibility.
10. Verify that card spacing and typography allow quick scanning of multiple tickets without visual clutter.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a ticket with a very long title (200 characters) is displayed without breaking the card layout, with truncation or wrapping as appropriate.
12. Verify that a ticket submitted at midnight (12:00 AM) displays the correct date and time on the card.

Security:
13. Verify that ticket cards only display data for tickets belonging to the authenticated family contact and no data leakage occurs from other users' tickets.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the ticket card list renders within 2 seconds for up to 50 tickets on the Tickets page.
Verify that scrolling through 100+ ticket cards is smooth without noticeable lag or jank.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to click on a ticket to view its full detail panel showing Ticket ID, status badge, linked resident, complete description, and date/time of creation, so that I can review the full context.
Description of User Story: Clicking on a ticket card from the Tickets page opens a full ticket detail panel that presents the complete information for the selected ticket. The detail panel displays the Ticket ID in "#TKT-XXXXX" format (e.g., #I21212 as shown in Figma), a color-coded status badge (red for Open, green for Closed), the linked resident's name (e.g., "Devon Lane"), the complete ticket description without any truncation, and the date and time of ticket creation. The detail panel may open as a side panel, modal, or a dedicated detail view depending on the design implementation. Additional information visible in the detail panel includes the priority level, the ticket title, and any staff responses or activity timeline if applicable. A back button or close icon allows the family contact to return to the ticket list. The detail panel layout is optimized for readability with clearly labeled sections and adequate spacing between information blocks.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact clicks on a ticket card, Then the system must open the detail panel displaying Ticket ID, status badge, linked resident, complete description, priority, and creation date/time.

2. If the ticket status is "Open", Then the status badge must be red.
   Else if the ticket status is "Closed", Then the status badge must be green.

3. If the ticket has staff responses, Then the detail panel must display the responses in a threaded message format below the description.
   Else if the ticket has no responses yet, Then the detail panel must display "No responses yet" or an empty message area.

4. If the ticket detail fails to load, Then the system must display "Unable to load ticket details. Please try again." with a retry option.

5. If the family contact navigates away and returns to the same ticket, Then the detail panel must display the latest data including any new responses or status changes.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking on ticket #TKT-10102 opens the detail panel showing Ticket ID "#TKT-10102", red "Open" status badge, linked resident "Devon Lane", complete description, and creation date "March 30, 2026 10:00 AM".
2. Verify that the detail panel displays the full ticket description without truncation for a 500-character message.
3. Verify that a closed ticket's detail panel shows a green "Closed" status badge.
4. Verify that the linked resident name "Devon Lane" is displayed clearly in the detail panel.
5. Verify that clicking the back button or close icon returns the user to the ticket list.

Negative:
6. Verify that when the ticket detail API fails, the message "Unable to load ticket details. Please try again." is displayed with a retry button.
7. Verify that a ticket with no staff responses displays "No responses yet" in the message area.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the ticket detail panel displays Ticket ID and status badge at the top, followed by linked resident, full description, and creation timestamp in a well-organized layout.
9. Verify that the detail panel has a visible back or close button for easy navigation back to the ticket list.
10. Verify that the detail panel sections (Ticket Info, Description, Responses) are clearly labeled with adequate spacing.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a ticket with a very long description (5000 characters) is displayed fully in the detail panel with scrolling enabled.
12. Verify that returning to the same ticket after a status change reflects the updated status in the detail panel.

Security:
13. Verify that the ticket detail panel only loads data for tickets owned by the authenticated family contact and rejects unauthorized ticket ID access.
14. Verify that the ticket detail API does not expose internal system identifiers or staff personal information beyond name and role.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the ticket detail panel loads within 2 seconds after clicking a ticket card.
Verify that the detail panel renders the complete description and any responses within 2 seconds for a ticket with up to 10 staff responses.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to edit an open ticket using the Edit button on the ticket detail panel, so that I can update or add information.
Description of User Story: The Edit Ticket feature allows Approved Family Contacts to modify the details of a ticket that is currently in "Open" status. On the ticket detail panel, an "Edit" button is visible for tickets with an Open status. Clicking the "Edit" button transforms the detail view into an editable form where the family contact can update the Ticket Title, Priority, and Message (description) fields. The Resident Name and Ticket ID remain non-editable. The form pre-populates with the current ticket data so the family contact can see and modify existing values. After making changes, clicking "Save" submits the updates and returns the view to the read-only detail panel with the updated information. A "Cancel" button discards any unsaved changes and reverts to the read-only view. The system records the edit in the ticket activity log with the timestamp of the modification. The Edit button is not available for tickets with a "Closed" status, preventing modifications to resolved tickets.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact clicks "Edit" on an open ticket, Then the system must switch the detail panel to an editable form with Ticket Title, Priority, and Message fields pre-populated with current values.

2. If the family contact modifies the fields and clicks "Save", Then the system must update the ticket, record the edit in the activity log with a timestamp, and return to the read-only detail view.
   Else if the family contact clicks "Cancel", Then the system must discard changes and revert to the read-only view.

3. If the ticket status is "Closed", Then the "Edit" button must not be displayed or must be disabled on the detail panel.
   Else if the ticket status is "In Progress" or "Awaiting Response", Then the "Edit" button must still be available since the ticket is not yet closed.

4. If the family contact attempts to save with an empty Ticket Title, Then the system must display "Ticket Title is required" and block the update.
   Else if the Message field is empty, Then the system must display "Message is required" and block the update.

5. If the update fails due to a server error, Then the system must display "Unable to update ticket. Please try again." and preserve the edited form data.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "Edit" button on an open ticket #TKT-10102 switches the detail panel to an editable form with current title, priority, and message pre-populated.
2. Verify that changing the Ticket Title from "Room temperature issue" to "Room temperature and ventilation issue" and clicking "Save" updates the ticket and shows the new title in the read-only view.
3. Verify that changing the Priority from "High" to "Urgent" and saving reflects the updated priority in the detail panel.
4. Verify that clicking "Cancel" after making changes discards the edits and shows the original ticket data.
5. Verify that the activity log records an edit entry with the timestamp after a successful update.

Negative:
6. Verify that the "Edit" button is not displayed on a ticket with "Closed" status.
7. Verify that clearing the Ticket Title and clicking "Save" displays "Ticket Title is required" and blocks the update.
8. Verify that clearing the Message field and clicking "Save" displays "Message is required" and blocks the update.
9. Verify that when the update API fails, the error message "Unable to update ticket. Please try again." is displayed and the form data is preserved.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the editable form retains the same layout as the detail panel, with Ticket ID and Resident Name displayed as read-only and Title, Priority, and Message as editable fields.
11. Verify that "Save" and "Cancel" buttons are clearly visible at the bottom of the editable form.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that editing a ticket while a staff member simultaneously responds does not overwrite the staff response.
13. Verify that a ticket in "In Progress" or "Awaiting Response" status still shows the "Edit" button and allows editing.

Security:
14. Verify that only the family contact who created the ticket can edit it and other contacts cannot access the edit functionality.
15. Verify that the edit API rejects requests for tickets not owned by the authenticated family contact.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that switching to edit mode loads the editable form within 1 second after clicking "Edit".
Verify that saving the edited ticket completes and returns to read-only view within 3 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to search tickets by keyword on the Tickets page.
Description of User Story: The Tickets page includes a search bar at the top of the ticket list that allows Approved Family Contacts to search their tickets by keyword. The search functionality matches the entered keyword against the ticket title, description, and Ticket ID fields. As the family contact types in the search bar, the ticket list filters in real-time (or after a brief debounce period) to show only tickets that contain the keyword. The search is case-insensitive and supports partial matches. The search operates within the currently active tab (Open or Closed), filtering only the tickets visible in that tab. If the search returns no results, a "No tickets found for [keyword]" message is displayed. A clear button (X icon) in the search bar allows the family contact to reset the search and restore the full ticket list. The search bar placeholder text provides guidance such as "Search by title, description, or ticket ID".
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact types a keyword in the search bar, Then the system must filter the ticket list in the active tab to show only tickets matching the keyword in title, description, or Ticket ID.

2. If the search keyword matches one or more tickets, Then the system must display only the matching ticket cards.
   Else if the search keyword matches no tickets, Then the system must display "No tickets found for [keyword]".

3. If the family contact is on the Open tab, Then the search must filter only open tickets.
   Else if the family contact is on the Closed tab, Then the search must filter only closed tickets.

4. If the family contact clears the search bar using the X icon, Then the system must restore the full ticket list for the active tab.

5. If the family contact types rapidly, Then the system must debounce the search input and only execute the search after the user pauses typing to avoid excessive API calls.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that typing "temperature" in the search bar filters the ticket list to show tickets with "temperature" in the title or description.
2. Verify that searching for "#TKT-10102" returns the specific ticket with that ID.
3. Verify that the search is case-insensitive by typing "TEMPERATURE" and seeing the same results as "temperature".
4. Verify that searching on the Open tab only returns matching open tickets and does not include closed tickets.
5. Verify that clicking the clear (X) button restores the full ticket list.

Negative:
6. Verify that searching for a keyword with no matching tickets displays "No tickets found for [keyword]".
7. Verify that searching with only spaces or special characters returns no results or handles gracefully without errors.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the search bar is positioned at the top of the ticket list with placeholder text "Search by title, description, or ticket ID".
9. Verify that the clear (X) icon appears in the search bar only when text has been entered.
10. Verify that search results update in real-time or within a brief debounce period as the user types.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a search keyword matching a partial word (e.g., "temp") returns tickets containing "temperature" or "temporary".
12. Verify that searching with a very long keyword (200 characters) does not break the UI or cause errors.
13. Verify that rapidly typing and clearing the search bar does not cause duplicate or stale results.

Security:
14. Verify that the search only returns tickets belonging to the authenticated family contact and does not expose other users' ticket data.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that search results appear within 1 second after the user stops typing for a list of up to 100 tickets.
Verify that clearing the search and restoring the full ticket list completes within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to filter tickets on the Tickets page.
Description of User Story: The Tickets page provides a filtering mechanism that allows Approved Family Contacts to narrow down the displayed tickets based on specific criteria. Available filter options include: Priority (Low, Medium, High, Urgent), Date Range (start date and end date picker), and Linked Resident (dropdown of all residents linked to the family contact). Filters can be combined to create more precise views, for example filtering for all High-priority tickets for a specific resident within a date range. The active filters are displayed as removable chips or tags above the ticket list so the family contact can see which filters are applied and remove individual filters by clicking the X on each chip. A "Clear All Filters" option resets all filters at once and restores the full ticket list. The filter functionality works in conjunction with the Open/Closed tabs and the search bar, allowing the family contact to use all three features together for maximum flexibility in finding specific tickets.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Approved Family Contact applies one or more filters, Then the system must update the ticket list to display only tickets matching all applied filter criteria.

2. If the applied filters return matching tickets, Then the system must display the filtered ticket cards with active filter chips shown above the list.
   Else if the applied filters return no results, Then the system must display "No tickets match the selected filters" with a "Clear All Filters" option.

3. If the family contact removes a single filter chip, Then the system must re-apply the remaining filters and update the ticket list.
   Else if the family contact clicks "Clear All Filters", Then the system must remove all filters and restore the full ticket list.

4. If filters are applied in combination with the Open/Closed tab and search bar, Then the system must apply all criteria together (tab + filters + search) to produce the final result set.

5. If the family contact is linked to only one resident, Then the Linked Resident filter must be auto-selected and optionally hidden or disabled.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that filtering by Priority "High" displays only tickets with High priority.
2. Verify that filtering by date range March 1-31, 2026 displays only tickets created within that period.
3. Verify that filtering by Linked Resident "Devon Lane" displays only tickets associated with that resident.
4. Verify that combining Priority "High" and Resident "Devon Lane" filters returns only high-priority tickets for Devon Lane.
5. Verify that active filters appear as removable chips above the ticket list.
6. Verify that clicking X on a Priority filter chip removes it and updates the list with remaining filters applied.
7. Verify that clicking "Clear All Filters" removes all active filters and restores the full ticket list.

Negative:
8. Verify that applying filters that match no tickets displays "No tickets match the selected filters" with a "Clear All Filters" option.
9. Verify that selecting an invalid date range (end date before start date) displays a validation error and prevents the filter from being applied.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the filter controls (Priority dropdown, Date Range picker, Linked Resident dropdown) are clearly accessible on the Tickets page.
11. Verify that active filter chips are displayed above the ticket list with an X button on each chip and a "Clear All Filters" link.
12. Verify that filter controls work visually alongside the Open/Closed tabs and search bar without overlapping.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that applying filters on the Open tab and then switching to the Closed tab retains the filter settings and applies them to the closed tickets.
14. Verify that a family contact linked to only one resident sees the Linked Resident filter auto-selected or hidden.
15. Verify that using all three features together (tab, filter, and search) produces the correct combined result set.

Security:
16. Verify that filter results only include tickets belonging to the authenticated family contact and do not expose other users' data.
17. Verify that the Linked Resident dropdown only shows residents linked to the authenticated family contact.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that applying or removing filters updates the ticket list within 1.5 seconds for a total of 100 tickets.
Verify that combining multiple filters and search with tab selection renders the final result set within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a notification (email/SMS/push) when the facility shares a new document requiring my review or signature.
Description of User Story: When the facility uploads or shares a document that requires a family contact's review or signature (e.g., consent forms, care plans, policy updates), the notification service triggers an alert across all configured channels: email, SMS, and push notification. The email contains the document title, a brief description of the required action (review or sign), the name of the facility staff who shared it, and a deep-link button directing the family contact to the document within the portal. The SMS includes a condensed message with the document title and a shortened link. The push notification appears on the family contact's device with the document title and required action. The notification is generated in real time when the document status changes to "Shared" or "Pending Signature". The family contact's notification preferences (email, SMS, push, or any combination) stored in their profile settings determine which channels are used. If the document is not reviewed within a configurable period (e.g., 48 hours), a follow-up reminder notification is sent.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the facility shares a document requiring review or signature, Then the system must send a notification to the Approved Family Contact on all configured channels.

2. If the family contact has email notifications enabled, Then the system must send an email with document title, required action, staff name, and deep-link.
   Else if the family contact has only SMS enabled, Then the system must send an SMS with the document title and shortened link.
   Else if the family contact has only push enabled, Then the system must send a push notification with the document title and action.
   Else if no notification channel is enabled, Then the system must still create an in-app notification visible on the portal's notification bell.

3. If the document requires a signature, Then the notification must include "Signature Required" in the subject/title.
   Else if the document requires only a review, Then the notification must include "Review Required" in the subject/title.

4. If the notification delivery fails on any channel, Then the system must retry delivery up to 3 times with exponential backoff and log the failure.

5. If the family contact has not reviewed the document within 48 hours, Then the system must send a follow-up reminder notification.
   Else if the document has been reviewed or signed, Then the system must suppress the reminder.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when a facility staff member shares a consent form titled "Annual Care Plan Review", the linked Approved Family Contact receives an email notification with the title, action "Review Required", staff name, and deep-link.
2. Verify that the family contact receives an SMS containing the document title "Annual Care Plan Review" and a shortened link when SMS is enabled.
3. Verify that a push notification appears on the family contact's device with the title "Annual Care Plan Review — Signature Required" when the document requires a signature.
4. Verify that clicking the deep-link in the email navigates directly to the document detail page in the portal.
5. Verify that a follow-up reminder is sent after 48 hours if the document has not been reviewed.

Negative:
6. Verify that if all notification channels are disabled, the notification still appears as an in-app alert on the notification bell.
7. Verify that if the email delivery fails, the system retries up to 3 times before logging the failure.
8. Verify that if the document is reviewed within 48 hours, no follow-up reminder is sent.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app notification displays the document title, required action badge ("Review" or "Sign"), and the timestamp of when it was shared.
10. Verify that the notification bell icon shows an unread count badge when a new document notification arrives.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that when multiple documents are shared simultaneously, the family contact receives separate notifications for each document.
12. Verify that a notification for a document shared at 11:59 PM is delivered with the correct date and timestamp.

Security:
13. Verify that the deep-link in the notification requires authentication before displaying the document.
14. Verify that the SMS and email do not expose PHI beyond the document title and action required.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the notification is delivered to all configured channels within 30 seconds of the document being shared.
Verify that the in-app notification appears on the notification bell within 5 seconds of the document being shared.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a notification when an incident has been reported for my loved one, so that I am immediately informed.
Description of User Story: When an incident is reported for a resident linked to an Approved Family Contact, the notification service immediately dispatches an alert to the family contact. An incident may include falls, medical emergencies, behavioral events, medication errors, or elopement attempts. The notification is sent via all configured channels (email, SMS, push) and includes the incident type, the resident's name, the date and time of the incident, a brief summary, and a deep-link to the full incident report in the portal. The email provides a more detailed summary including the facility's immediate response actions. The SMS and push notification convey a concise alert with the incident type and resident name. Due to the urgency of incidents, notifications are dispatched with high priority so that push notifications bypass do-not-disturb settings where supported. The in-app notification is also created and marked as high-priority with a red indicator.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When an incident is reported for a linked resident, Then the system must immediately dispatch a high-priority notification to the Approved Family Contact on all configured channels.

2. If the incident is classified as "Critical" (e.g., medical emergency, elopement), Then the notification must be flagged as urgent with a red badge and bypass do-not-disturb.
   Else if the incident is classified as "Moderate" (e.g., minor fall, behavioral event), Then the notification must be sent with standard priority.
   Else if the incident is classified as "Low" (e.g., minor property damage), Then the notification must be sent with normal priority.

3. If the family contact has no notification channels enabled, Then the system must create an in-app notification and attempt to send an email to the registered email address regardless of preference.

4. If the notification delivery fails on any channel, Then the system must retry immediately up to 3 times due to incident urgency.

5. If multiple incidents are reported for the same resident within a short window, Then the system must send separate notifications for each incident without batching.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when a fall incident is reported for "Devon Michael Lane", the Approved Family Contact receives an email with incident type "Fall", resident name, date/time, summary, and deep-link.
2. Verify that an SMS is sent with "Incident Alert: Fall reported for Devon Michael Lane" and a shortened link.
3. Verify that a high-priority push notification is delivered for a critical incident even when the device is in do-not-disturb mode.
4. Verify that clicking the deep-link in the notification opens the full incident report in the portal.
5. Verify that the in-app notification displays a red high-priority indicator for the incident.

Negative:
6. Verify that if all notification channels are disabled, the system still sends an email to the registered address and creates an in-app notification.
7. Verify that if the notification service is temporarily unavailable, the system retries delivery up to 3 times.
8. Verify that a family contact not linked to the resident does not receive the incident notification.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app incident notification displays a red urgency badge, incident type, resident name, and timestamp.
10. Verify that the notification bell count increments when a new incident notification arrives.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that two incidents reported within 1 minute for the same resident generate two separate notifications.
12. Verify that an incident reported at midnight is delivered with the correct date and time.

Security:
13. Verify that the incident report deep-link requires authentication before displaying details.
14. Verify that the notification content does not include detailed medical information in SMS or push channels.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that incident notifications are dispatched within 10 seconds of the incident being reported.
Verify that the in-app notification appears on the bell icon within 5 seconds of the incident report submission.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a notification when a new message is posted in the group chat, so that I don't miss any important communication.
Description of User Story: When a new message is posted in a group chat that includes an Approved Family Contact, the notification service sends an alert to ensure the family contact does not miss important communications. The notification is delivered via the family contact's configured channels (email, SMS, push) and includes the group chat name, the sender's name, a preview of the message (first 100 characters), and a deep-link to the chat conversation. Push notifications appear with the group name as the title and the message preview as the body. To prevent notification fatigue, the system batches multiple messages from the same group within a short window (e.g., 2 minutes) into a single notification summarizing the number of new messages. An in-app notification badge is updated in real time on the Chat menu item in the sidebar. The family contact can mute notifications for specific group chats from the chat settings without affecting other notification types.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a new message is posted in a group chat, Then the system must send a notification to all family contacts in the group on their configured channels.

2. If only one message is posted within the batching window (2 minutes), Then the system must send a notification with the sender name and message preview.
   Else if multiple messages are posted within the batching window, Then the system must send a single notification stating "X new messages in [Group Name]".

3. If the family contact has muted the specific group chat, Then the system must suppress notifications for that group but still update the in-app unread badge.
   Else if the family contact has muted all chat notifications, Then the system must suppress all chat notifications but still update in-app badges.

4. If the message contains an attachment, Then the notification preview must display "[Attachment] filename" instead of text content.

5. If the notification delivery fails, Then the system must retry up to 2 times and ensure the in-app badge is still updated regardless of external channel delivery.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when "Dr. Sarah Johnson" posts a message in "Devon Care Team" group chat, the family contact receives a push notification with title "Devon Care Team" and the message preview.
2. Verify that the email notification contains the group name "Devon Care Team", sender "Dr. Sarah Johnson", first 100 characters of the message, and a deep-link to the chat.
3. Verify that 5 messages posted within 2 minutes result in a single notification stating "5 new messages in Devon Care Team".
4. Verify that clicking the notification deep-link opens the group chat conversation at the latest message.
5. Verify that the Chat sidebar badge updates in real time when a new message arrives.

Negative:
6. Verify that muting "Devon Care Team" group suppresses all notifications from that group but the unread badge still updates.
7. Verify that a family contact not in the group chat does not receive any notification.
8. Verify that if the notification service is unavailable, the in-app badge count still updates correctly.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the push notification displays the group chat avatar, group name, and a truncated message preview.
10. Verify that the in-app notification on the bell icon shows the group name, sender, and message preview with a timestamp.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a message containing only an image attachment shows "[Attachment] photo.jpg" in the notification preview.
12. Verify that a very long message (500+ characters) is truncated to 100 characters in the notification preview with an ellipsis.

Security:
13. Verify that the notification deep-link requires authentication before displaying the chat.
14. Verify that message content in push and SMS notifications does not expose sensitive medical details.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the push notification is delivered within 15 seconds of the message being posted.
Verify that the in-app Chat badge updates within 3 seconds of a new message arriving.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a notification when my support ticket status is updated or resolved by the facility.
Description of User Story: When a support ticket submitted by or associated with an Approved Family Contact is updated or resolved by the facility, the notification service sends an alert to the family contact. Ticket updates include status changes (e.g., "In Progress", "Awaiting Information", "Resolved", "Closed"), new comments added by facility staff, and priority changes. The notification includes the ticket number, the previous status, the new status, a brief comment or resolution note if provided, and a deep-link to the ticket detail page. Email notifications provide the full update context, while SMS and push notifications provide a concise summary. When a ticket is resolved, the notification includes the resolution summary and an invitation to reopen the ticket if the issue persists. The in-app notification is created for every status change and displayed under the notification bell with the ticket number and new status.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a support ticket status is updated, Then the system must notify the Approved Family Contact on all configured channels with ticket number, old status, new status, and deep-link.

2. If the ticket status changes to "Resolved", Then the notification must include the resolution summary and a "Reopen Ticket" prompt.
   Else if the ticket status changes to "Awaiting Information", Then the notification must prompt the family contact to provide the requested information.
   Else if the ticket status changes to "In Progress", Then the notification must confirm that the facility is working on the issue.

3. If facility staff adds a comment to the ticket, Then the system must send a notification with the comment preview (first 150 characters) and a deep-link.

4. If the ticket priority is escalated, Then the notification must include a "Priority Escalated" label.
   Else if the priority is de-escalated, Then the notification must reflect the updated priority.

5. If multiple status changes occur within 5 minutes, Then the system must send a single consolidated notification with the latest status.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when ticket #TKT-2045 status changes from "Open" to "In Progress", the family contact receives an email with ticket number, status change, and staff comment "Reviewing your request".
2. Verify that when ticket #TKT-2045 is resolved, the notification includes "Resolved: Issue has been addressed. Please reopen if the problem persists."
3. Verify that an SMS is sent with "Ticket #TKT-2045 updated: In Progress" when the status changes.
4. Verify that a staff comment "Please provide additional details" triggers a notification with the comment preview and deep-link.
5. Verify that clicking the deep-link navigates to the ticket detail page showing the latest status and comments.

Negative:
6. Verify that a family contact who did not submit the ticket and is not associated with it does not receive the notification.
7. Verify that if 3 status changes occur within 5 minutes, only one consolidated notification with the latest status is sent.
8. Verify that when the notification service fails, the in-app notification is still created.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app notification shows the ticket number, new status badge (color-coded), and timestamp.
10. Verify that resolved tickets display a green "Resolved" badge in the notification.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a ticket resolved and reopened within the same day generates separate notifications for each status change.
12. Verify that a ticket with a very long resolution note (1000+ characters) truncates the preview to 150 characters in the notification.

Security:
13. Verify that the ticket deep-link requires authentication and only the ticket owner can view the details.
14. Verify that ticket notifications do not expose other users' ticket data or internal staff notes.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the ticket status update notification is delivered within 20 seconds of the status change.
Verify that the in-app notification for a ticket update appears within 5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact/Patient, I should receive a friendly payment reminder between the 1st and 5th of the month if my invoice has not yet been paid.
Description of User Story: The payment reminder notification system automatically sends a friendly reminder to Approved Family Contacts and patients who have unpaid invoices. The reminder is triggered by a scheduled job that runs daily between the 1st and 5th of each month, checking for accounts with outstanding invoices from the previous billing cycle. The reminder is delivered via the configured notification channels (email, SMS, push) and uses a friendly, non-threatening tone. The email includes the invoice number, the outstanding amount, the due date, a "Pay Now" deep-link to the billing section, and accepted payment methods. The SMS includes a brief message with the amount due and a shortened link. The push notification displays the amount due and a prompt to pay. Only one reminder per invoice is sent during the 1st-5th window to avoid repetition. If the invoice is paid at any point during this window, any pending scheduled reminders are automatically cancelled.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the system scheduler runs between the 1st and 5th of the month, Then the system must identify all accounts with unpaid invoices and send a friendly payment reminder.

2. If the invoice has already been paid before the scheduler runs, Then the system must skip sending a reminder for that invoice.
   Else if the invoice is partially paid, Then the system must send a reminder for the remaining balance only.
   Else if the invoice is fully unpaid, Then the system must send a reminder for the full amount.

3. If the family contact/patient has already received a reminder for this invoice during the current 1st-5th window, Then the system must not send a duplicate reminder.

4. If the invoice is paid after the reminder is sent but before the 5th, Then the system must cancel any further pending reminders for that invoice.

5. If the family contact has disabled billing notifications, Then the system must still send the payment reminder via email as a financial communication override.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that on January 2nd, a family contact with unpaid invoice #INV-3021 for $350.00 receives an email reminder with the invoice number, amount, due date, and "Pay Now" link.
2. Verify that the email uses a friendly tone such as "Just a friendly reminder that your invoice is due."
3. Verify that the SMS contains "Reminder: $350.00 due for invoice #INV-3021" with a payment link.
4. Verify that the push notification displays "$350.00 due — Tap to pay now".
5. Verify that a partially paid invoice with $150.00 remaining triggers a reminder for $150.00 only.

Negative:
6. Verify that a family contact who paid invoice #INV-3021 on December 30th does not receive a January reminder.
7. Verify that a reminder sent on January 2nd is not duplicated on January 3rd or 4th for the same invoice.
8. Verify that no payment reminders are sent on January 6th or later for the same billing cycle.
9. Verify that even if billing notifications are disabled, the payment reminder email is still delivered.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the in-app payment reminder notification shows the invoice number, amount due, and a "Pay Now" action button.
11. Verify that clicking "Pay Now" in the email or notification navigates to the billing payment page with the invoice pre-selected.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that if an invoice is paid on the same day the reminder is scheduled, the reminder is cancelled before delivery.
13. Verify that a family contact with 3 unpaid invoices receives separate reminders for each invoice.

Security:
14. Verify that the "Pay Now" deep-link requires authentication before displaying billing details.
15. Verify that the reminder email does not include full payment card details or sensitive financial data.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the reminder scheduler processes all eligible accounts and dispatches notifications within 15 minutes of execution.
Verify that each individual reminder notification is delivered within 30 seconds of being queued.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact/Patient, I should be notified when a late fee has been applied to my account after the payment due date has passed.
Description of User Story: When a late fee is applied to a family contact's or patient's account due to a missed payment after the due date, the notification service sends an immediate alert. The late fee notification is delivered via all configured channels (email, SMS, push) and contains the invoice number, the original due date, the late fee amount, the new total balance, and a deep-link to the billing section. The email notification includes a detailed breakdown showing the original invoice amount, the late fee, and the updated total, along with information about how to make a payment to avoid further charges. The SMS provides a concise notice of the late fee amount and new balance. The push notification alerts the family contact of the late fee with the updated balance. An in-app notification is also created and displayed with a warning indicator. The notification uses a professional but firm tone to communicate the financial obligation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a late fee is applied to the family contact's/patient's account, Then the system must immediately send a notification on all configured channels with the fee details and updated balance.

2. If the late fee is applied for the first time on an invoice, Then the system must send a notification with the initial late fee amount.
   Else if an additional late fee is applied (compounding), Then the system must send a notification with the cumulative late fee and updated total.

3. If the family contact makes a payment after the late fee is applied, Then the system must not send another late fee notification unless a new late fee is applied in a subsequent period.

4. If the family contact has disabled billing notifications, Then the system must override the preference and send the late fee notification as a mandatory financial alert.

5. If the late fee notification delivery fails, Then the system must retry up to 3 times and ensure the in-app notification is created regardless.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when a $25.00 late fee is applied to invoice #INV-3021 (original amount $350.00), the family contact receives an email showing late fee $25.00, new total $375.00, original due date, and payment link.
2. Verify that the SMS notification reads "Late fee of $25.00 applied to invoice #INV-3021. New balance: $375.00."
3. Verify that the push notification displays "Late fee applied: $25.00. Balance: $375.00 — Pay now."
4. Verify that clicking the billing deep-link opens the billing section with invoice #INV-3021 showing the updated total.
5. Verify that the in-app notification displays a warning (amber/red) indicator with the late fee details.

Negative:
6. Verify that if the invoice was paid before the late fee date, no late fee notification is sent.
7. Verify that billing notification preferences are overridden and the late fee alert is always delivered.
8. Verify that a family contact not associated with the invoice does not receive the late fee notification.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app late fee notification shows a warning icon, invoice number, late fee amount, and updated balance.
10. Verify that the late fee email uses a professional tone with clear breakdown: original amount, late fee, and new total.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a second late fee applied in the next month generates a new notification with the cumulative fee amount.
12. Verify that a late fee of $0.00 (waived) does not trigger a notification.

Security:
13. Verify that the billing deep-link requires authentication before displaying invoice details.
14. Verify that the notification does not expose full account numbers or payment method details.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the late fee notification is dispatched within 30 seconds of the fee being applied.
Verify that the in-app notification appears within 5 seconds of the late fee being recorded.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a notification when a new invoice is generated.
Description of User Story: When a new billing invoice is generated for an account linked to an Approved Family Contact, the notification service sends an alert to inform the family contact. The notification is delivered via all configured channels (email, SMS, push) and contains the invoice number, the billing period, the total amount due, the due date, and a deep-link to view the invoice in the billing section. The email includes a summary of the key line items (up to 5), the total amount, payment due date, accepted payment methods, and a "View Invoice" button. The SMS provides a brief message with the invoice number, amount due, and due date. The push notification displays the amount due and due date. An in-app notification is created with the invoice details under the notification bell. The notification is generated immediately when the invoice status changes to "Issued" in the billing system.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a new invoice is generated and its status changes to "Issued", Then the system must send a notification to the Approved Family Contact on all configured channels.

2. If the invoice contains fewer than 5 line items, Then the email must list all line items.
   Else if the invoice contains 5 or more line items, Then the email must list the top 5 line items and include a "View full invoice" link for the rest.

3. If the family contact has no notification channels configured, Then the system must create an in-app notification and send an email to the registered address.

4. If the invoice amount is $0.00 (e.g., fully covered by insurance), Then the system must send a notification indicating "No payment required for this billing period."

5. If the notification delivery fails, Then the system must retry up to 3 times and log the failure.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when invoice #INV-3050 for $450.00 is generated for billing period March 2026, the family contact receives an email with invoice number, 4 line items, total $450.00, due date April 15, 2026, and "View Invoice" button.
2. Verify that the SMS reads "New invoice #INV-3050: $450.00 due by April 15, 2026."
3. Verify that the push notification displays "$450.00 due by April 15, 2026."
4. Verify that clicking "View Invoice" in the email navigates to the invoice detail page in the billing section.
5. Verify that the in-app notification shows invoice #INV-3050, $450.00, and the due date.

Negative:
6. Verify that a $0.00 invoice generates a notification stating "No payment required for this billing period."
7. Verify that a family contact not linked to the account does not receive the invoice notification.
8. Verify that if the email channel fails, the system retries up to 3 times.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app notification displays the invoice number, amount in bold, due date, and a "View" action link.
10. Verify that the notification bell count increments when a new invoice notification arrives.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that an invoice with 20 line items shows only the top 5 in the email with a "View full invoice" link.
12. Verify that two invoices generated on the same day produce two separate notifications.

Security:
13. Verify that the "View Invoice" deep-link requires authentication before displaying invoice details.
14. Verify that invoice amounts and line items in the email are transmitted over an encrypted connection.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the invoice notification is dispatched within 30 seconds of the invoice being issued.
Verify that the in-app notification appears within 5 seconds of invoice generation.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a notification when a new alert is added to my loved one's record.
Description of User Story: When a new clinical or administrative alert is added to a resident's record, the notification service sends an alert to the linked Approved Family Contact. Alerts may include changes in health status, new care restrictions, dietary changes, allergy updates, fall risk level changes, or other clinically significant notes flagged for family visibility. The notification is delivered via all configured channels (email, SMS, push) and includes the alert type, the resident's name, the date the alert was added, a brief description of the alert, and a deep-link to the resident's record in the portal. The email provides more context including the category of the alert and any recommended actions for the family. Only alerts marked as "Family Visible" by facility staff are shared with family contacts. The in-app notification is created with the appropriate severity indicator (info, warning, or critical) based on the alert category.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a new alert is added to a resident's record and marked as "Family Visible", Then the system must notify the linked Approved Family Contact on all configured channels.

2. If the alert is classified as "Critical" (e.g., health status deterioration), Then the notification must use a critical severity indicator and high-priority delivery.
   Else if the alert is classified as "Warning" (e.g., new fall risk), Then the notification must use a warning severity indicator.
   Else if the alert is classified as "Info" (e.g., dietary preference update), Then the notification must use a standard info indicator.

3. If the alert is not marked as "Family Visible", Then the system must not send any notification to the family contact.

4. If multiple alerts are added to the same resident simultaneously, Then the system must send separate notifications for each alert to ensure none are missed.

5. If the notification delivery fails, Then the system must retry up to 3 times and always create the in-app notification.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when a "Fall Risk Level Changed to High" alert is added for "Devon Michael Lane" and marked as "Family Visible", the family contact receives an email with alert type, resident name, date, description, and deep-link.
2. Verify that the push notification displays "New Alert: Fall Risk — Devon Michael Lane".
3. Verify that the SMS contains the alert type and resident name with a shortened link.
4. Verify that clicking the deep-link opens the resident's record page showing the new alert.
5. Verify that a critical alert shows a red indicator in the in-app notification.

Negative:
6. Verify that an alert not marked as "Family Visible" does not generate any notification to the family contact.
7. Verify that a family contact not linked to the resident does not receive the alert notification.
8. Verify that if the notification delivery fails, the in-app notification is still created.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app notification displays the alert type, severity icon (info/warning/critical), resident name, and timestamp.
10. Verify that critical alerts appear at the top of the notification list with a red badge.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that 3 alerts added simultaneously for the same resident produce 3 separate notifications.
12. Verify that an alert added after business hours is still delivered immediately.

Security:
13. Verify that the deep-link requires authentication and only authorized family contacts can view the alert.
14. Verify that clinical details in SMS/push are limited to the alert type without detailed medical information.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the alert notification is dispatched within 15 seconds of the alert being added to the record.
Verify that the in-app notification appears within 5 seconds of the alert creation.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a notification when a new facility event is published.
Description of User Story: When the facility publishes a new event (e.g., family visiting day, holiday celebration, health seminar, support group meeting, activity program), the notification service sends an alert to all Approved Family Contacts linked to residents at the facility. The notification is delivered via configured channels (email, SMS, push) and includes the event name, date and time, location within the facility, a brief description, and a deep-link to the event details page. The email provides a more detailed description including any RSVP requirements, dress code, or items to bring. The SMS includes the event name, date, and a shortened link. The push notification displays the event name and date. An in-app notification is also created under the notification bell. Events are published by facility administrators and the notification is triggered when the event status changes to "Published".
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a new event is published by the facility, Then the system must notify all Approved Family Contacts linked to residents at that facility.

2. If the event requires RSVP, Then the email notification must include an "RSVP Now" button linking to the event page.
   Else if no RSVP is required, Then the notification must state "No RSVP required".

3. If the event is cancelled after the notification was sent, Then the system must send a follow-up cancellation notification with the event name and cancellation reason.

4. If the event date is updated after publication, Then the system must send an update notification with the old and new date/time.

5. If the family contact has disabled event notifications, Then the system must respect the preference and only create an in-app notification.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that when "Spring Family Visiting Day" on April 20, 2026 at 10:00 AM in the Main Hall is published, all linked family contacts receive an email with full event details and RSVP link.
2. Verify that the SMS reads "New Event: Spring Family Visiting Day — April 20, 2026" with a link.
3. Verify that the push notification displays "Spring Family Visiting Day — April 20, 2026".
4. Verify that clicking the event deep-link opens the event details page in the portal.
5. Verify that the in-app notification shows the event name, date, and location.

Negative:
6. Verify that if the event is cancelled, a cancellation notification is sent with the event name and reason.
7. Verify that a family contact who has disabled event notifications does not receive email/SMS/push but sees an in-app notification.
8. Verify that family contacts linked to a different facility do not receive the event notification.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app event notification displays an event icon, event name, date/time, and location.
10. Verify that the email includes a calendar-friendly format with event details that can be added to a personal calendar.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that an event date change from April 20 to April 25 triggers an update notification stating the old and new dates.
12. Verify that two events published on the same day generate separate notifications.

Security:
13. Verify that the event deep-link requires authentication to access event details.
14. Verify that event notifications are only sent to family contacts with active linked residents at the facility.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that event notifications are dispatched to all linked family contacts within 2 minutes of the event being published.
Verify that the in-app notification appears within 5 seconds of the event publication.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should receive a reminder notification before an upcoming appointment or meeting.
Description of User Story: The appointment reminder notification system sends proactive reminders to Approved Family Contacts before upcoming appointments or meetings related to their linked residents. Reminders are sent at two intervals: 24 hours before and 1 hour before the scheduled appointment. The notification includes the appointment type (e.g., care plan review, physician consultation, family meeting), the date and time, the location or virtual meeting link, the resident's name, and the attending staff or physician name. The email provides the full appointment details with a calendar attachment (ICS file) for easy addition to personal calendars. The SMS includes a concise reminder with date, time, and location. The push notification displays the appointment type and time remaining. The family contact can snooze or dismiss the reminder from the in-app notification panel. If the appointment is rescheduled or cancelled, the reminder is automatically updated or cancelled accordingly.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When an appointment is 24 hours away, Then the system must send a reminder notification to the Approved Family Contact on all configured channels.

2. When an appointment is 1 hour away, Then the system must send a second reminder notification.

3. If the appointment is a virtual meeting, Then the notification must include the virtual meeting link.
   Else if the appointment is in-person, Then the notification must include the facility location and room.

4. If the appointment is cancelled before the reminder is sent, Then the system must cancel the pending reminders and send a cancellation notice instead.
   Else if the appointment is rescheduled, Then the system must cancel old reminders, send an update notice with old and new times, and schedule new reminders.

5. If the family contact dismisses the 24-hour reminder, Then the system must still send the 1-hour reminder unless explicitly snoozed or dismissed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that 24 hours before a "Care Plan Review" appointment on April 10, 2026 at 2:00 PM in Room 301, the family contact receives an email reminder with all details and an ICS attachment.
2. Verify that 1 hour before the appointment, a push notification displays "Care Plan Review — in 1 hour at Room 301".
3. Verify that the SMS reads "Reminder: Care Plan Review for Devon Michael Lane — Tomorrow at 2:00 PM, Room 301."
4. Verify that a virtual appointment reminder includes the meeting link in all notification channels.
5. Verify that dismissing the 24-hour reminder still delivers the 1-hour reminder.

Negative:
6. Verify that if the appointment is cancelled, the pending reminders are cancelled and a cancellation notice is sent.
7. Verify that rescheduling the appointment from April 10 to April 12 triggers an update notification and re-schedules reminders.
8. Verify that a family contact not linked to the appointment's resident does not receive the reminder.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the in-app reminder notification displays the appointment type, date/time, location, and "Snooze" and "Dismiss" action buttons.
10. Verify that clicking the reminder navigates to the appointment detail page.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that an appointment scheduled less than 1 hour from now only sends the 1-hour reminder (skipping the 24-hour one).
12. Verify that an appointment rescheduled multiple times generates correct reminders for the final scheduled time only.

Security:
13. Verify that the virtual meeting link in the notification is accessible only to authorized attendees.
14. Verify that appointment details in SMS/push do not include detailed medical information.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the 24-hour reminder is dispatched within 5 minutes of the 24-hour mark.
Verify that the 1-hour reminder is dispatched within 2 minutes of the 1-hour mark.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to participate in group chat and Individual chat conversations.
Description of User Story: The Messaging module provides Approved Family Contacts with the ability to participate in both group chat and individual (one-on-one) chat conversations within the portal. The chat interface is divided into three panels. The left panel displays the contact list showing all available chat contacts including staff members, care team members, and group conversations, each with an avatar, name, last message preview, and timestamp. The middle panel is the active chat thread where messages are displayed in a conversation view with sent messages (blue bubbles, right-aligned) and received messages (grey bubbles, left-aligned), each with timestamps. At the bottom of the middle panel is a text input field with a send button. The right panel shows the contact or group detail information. Group chats are created by facility staff and include relevant care team members and family contacts. Individual chats allow direct messaging with specific staff members. The family contact can switch between conversations by selecting a contact or group from the left panel.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact navigates to the Chat section, Then the system must display the three-panel layout with the contacts list, chat thread, and contact/group details.

2. If the family contact selects a group chat, Then the system must load the full conversation history in the middle panel with all participants' messages.
   Else if the family contact selects an individual contact, Then the system must load the one-on-one conversation history.

3. If the family contact sends a message, Then the system must display it as a blue right-aligned bubble with a timestamp and deliver it to all participants in real time.
   Else if the message fails to send, Then the system must display a red "Failed to send" indicator with a retry option.

4. If the chat thread has more than 50 messages, Then the system must load the most recent 50 and provide a "Load earlier messages" option for pagination.

5. If the family contact has no existing conversations, Then the system must display "No conversations yet. Start a new chat by selecting a contact." in the middle panel.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Chat section displays the three-panel layout: contacts list (left), chat thread (middle), and contact details (right).
2. Verify that selecting the "Devon Care Team" group chat loads the conversation thread with messages from all group members.
3. Verify that typing "Hello, how is Devon doing today?" and clicking send displays the message as a blue right-aligned bubble with the current timestamp.
4. Verify that selecting "Dr. Sarah Johnson" from the contacts list loads the individual chat history.
5. Verify that a received message from Dr. Sarah Johnson appears as a grey left-aligned bubble with her name and timestamp.
6. Verify that the right panel shows the group name "Devon Care Team" and lists all members when a group chat is active.

Negative:
7. Verify that if a message fails to send due to network error, a red "Failed to send" indicator appears with a retry button.
8. Verify that a family contact with no conversations sees "No conversations yet. Start a new chat by selecting a contact."
9. Verify that a family contact cannot send messages to staff members or groups they are not authorized to contact.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that sent messages are displayed as blue bubbles right-aligned and received messages as grey bubbles left-aligned, matching the Figma design.
11. Verify that the contacts list shows avatars, names, last message previews, and timestamps for each contact.
12. Verify that the text input field is at the bottom of the middle panel with a send button icon.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a conversation with 200 messages loads the latest 50 and provides a "Load earlier messages" option.
14. Verify that sending a very long message (2000+ characters) wraps correctly within the bubble without overflow.

Security:
15. Verify that chat messages are transmitted over an encrypted connection.
