16. Verify that a family contact cannot access conversations of other family contacts through URL manipulation.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the chat thread loads within 2 seconds when selecting a conversation with up to 50 messages.
Verify that a sent message appears in the thread within 1 second of clicking send.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view a list of all chat contacts (staff members, care team, groups) with their names, avatars, last message preview, and timestamp, so that I can select who to message.
Description of User Story: The chat contacts list panel on the left side of the Chat section displays all available contacts and group conversations for the Approved Family Contact. Each entry in the list includes a circular avatar (profile photo or initials), the contact or group name, a preview of the last message in the conversation (truncated to one line), and a relative timestamp showing when the last message was sent (e.g., "2m ago", "Yesterday", "Mar 28"). Contacts are sorted by most recent activity, with the most recently active conversation at the top. Unread conversations are visually distinguished with a bold name, bold message preview, and an unread count badge. The list is categorized or filterable by type: "All", "Groups", "Staff", "Care Team". Group entries display a group avatar (composite of member photos or a group icon) and the group name. Individual contacts show their role or title below their name (e.g., "Nurse", "Social Worker", "Physician").
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Chat page loads, Then the system must display the contacts list sorted by most recent activity with avatars, names, last message previews, and timestamps.

2. If a conversation has unread messages, Then the system must display the contact name and message preview in bold with an unread count badge.
   Else if all messages in a conversation are read, Then the system must display the contact name and preview in normal weight without a badge.

3. If the family contact applies the "Groups" filter, Then the system must show only group conversations.
   Else if the "Staff" filter is applied, Then the system must show only staff contacts.
   Else if the "Care Team" filter is applied, Then the system must show only care team members.
   Else if "All" is selected, Then the system must show all contacts and groups.

4. If a contact has no conversation history, Then the system must display "No messages yet" as the preview text.

5. If the contact's avatar image fails to load, Then the system must display a circular avatar with the contact's initials.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the contacts list displays "Dr. Sarah Johnson" with avatar, role "Physician", last message "Devon's vitals are stable...", and timestamp "2m ago".
2. Verify that the "Devon Care Team" group shows a group avatar, name, last message preview, and timestamp "Yesterday".
3. Verify that conversations are sorted by most recent activity with the latest conversation at the top.
4. Verify that an unread conversation shows the contact name in bold, message preview in bold, and an unread badge showing "3".
5. Verify that applying the "Groups" filter shows only group conversations and hides individual contacts.
6. Verify that applying the "Care Team" filter shows only care team member contacts.

Negative:
7. Verify that a contact with no conversation history displays "No messages yet" as the preview.
8. Verify that a contact whose avatar fails to load displays initials in a circular placeholder.
9. Verify that a family contact cannot see contacts from other facilities or unlinked care teams.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that each contact entry shows a circular avatar on the left, name and role stacked on the right, and the timestamp aligned to the far right, matching the Figma design.
11. Verify that the last message preview is truncated to one line with an ellipsis if the text exceeds the available width.
12. Verify that filter tabs ("All", "Groups", "Staff", "Care Team") are displayed at the top of the contacts list.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that a family contact with 50+ contacts can scroll through the entire list without performance issues.
14. Verify that a message sent "just now" shows timestamp "Just now" and transitions to "1m ago" after a minute.

Security:
15. Verify that the contacts list API only returns contacts authorized for the logged-in family contact.
16. Verify that message previews do not expose PHI in the contacts list visible area.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the contacts list loads within 2 seconds with up to 50 contacts.
Verify that switching between filter tabs updates the list within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to search for specific contacts or conversations using the search bar in the Chat section.
Description of User Story: The search functionality in the Chat section allows Approved Family Contacts to quickly find specific contacts, groups, or conversations. A search bar is positioned at the top of the contacts list panel. As the family contact types in the search bar, the system performs a real-time search filtering the contacts list to show matching results. The search matches against contact names, group names, roles/titles, and message content within conversations. Results are displayed in the same contact list format showing matching contacts first, followed by conversations containing matching message content. Matching text is highlighted in the results. If the search matches a message within a conversation, the result shows the contact/group name and a snippet of the matching message with the search term highlighted. Clearing the search bar restores the full contacts list in its original sorted order. The search is case-insensitive and supports partial matches.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact types in the search bar, Then the system must perform a real-time case-insensitive search across contact names, group names, roles, and message content.

2. If the search matches contact or group names, Then the system must display matching contacts at the top of the results.
   Else if the search matches message content only, Then the system must display the conversation entry with a highlighted message snippet.

3. If the search returns no results, Then the system must display "No contacts or conversations found for '[search term]'".

4. If the family contact clears the search bar, Then the system must restore the full contacts list sorted by most recent activity.

5. If the search term is fewer than 2 characters, Then the system must wait until at least 2 characters are entered before executing the search to avoid excessive filtering.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that typing "Sarah" in the search bar filters the contacts list to show "Dr. Sarah Johnson" with the name highlighted.
2. Verify that searching "Care Team" returns the "Devon Care Team" group with the group name highlighted.
3. Verify that searching "vitals" returns a conversation result showing the contact name and a snippet "...Devon's vitals are stable..." with "vitals" highlighted.
4. Verify that clearing the search bar restores the full contacts list sorted by recent activity.
5. Verify that searching "sarah" (lowercase) matches "Dr. Sarah Johnson" (case-insensitive).

Negative:
6. Verify that searching "xyz123" displays "No contacts or conversations found for 'xyz123'".
7. Verify that typing a single character "S" does not trigger a search until a second character is entered.
8. Verify that the search does not return contacts from other facilities or unauthorized conversations.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the search bar is positioned at the top of the contacts list panel with a search icon and clear button.
10. Verify that matching text in search results is visually highlighted (e.g., bold or colored background).

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that searching for a contact who has been deactivated does not return results.
12. Verify that searching with special characters (e.g., "@", "#") does not cause errors and returns "No contacts or conversations found".

Security:
13. Verify that the search API only returns results from conversations the family contact is authorized to access.
14. Verify that the search does not expose message content from conversations the family contact is not a participant of.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that search results appear within 500 milliseconds of typing the second character.
Verify that clearing the search restores the full contacts list within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to initiate a voice call from a group chat conversation using the phone icon, so that I can quickly switch to a real-time conversation.
Description of User Story: The voice call feature allows Approved Family Contacts to initiate a voice call directly from a group chat conversation by clicking the phone icon in the chat header. When the phone icon is clicked, the system initiates a voice call session that connects the family contact with the group members or a designated staff contact. A calling interface overlay appears showing the group name or contact name, a call timer, and call controls (mute, speaker, end call). The call is connected via VoIP or a telephony bridge depending on the platform capabilities. Before the call connects, the system briefly shows a "Calling..." state with the recipient's name and a cancel option. If the call is not answered within 30 seconds, it times out and the family contact is notified. The phone icon is visible in the chat header area as shown in the Figma design, positioned near the group name or contact name at the top of the chat thread panel.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the phone icon in the chat header, Then the system must initiate a voice call and display the calling interface overlay.

2. If the call is answered by the recipient, Then the system must connect the call, start the call timer, and display call controls (mute, speaker, end call).
   Else if the call is not answered within 30 seconds, Then the system must display "Call not answered" and return to the chat view.
   Else if the recipient declines the call, Then the system must display "Call declined" and return to the chat view.

3. If the family contact clicks "Cancel" during the "Calling..." state, Then the system must abort the call attempt and return to the chat view.

4. If the network connection is lost during a call, Then the system must display "Connection lost. Attempting to reconnect..." and retry for up to 10 seconds before ending the call.

5. If the family contact's device does not have microphone permission, Then the system must display "Microphone access is required to make a call. Please enable it in your device settings."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the phone icon in the "Devon Care Team" group chat header shows a "Calling Devon Care Team..." overlay with a cancel button.
2. Verify that when the call is answered, the call timer starts and mute, speaker, and end call buttons are visible.
3. Verify that clicking the mute button during a call mutes the microphone and changes the icon to indicate muted state.
4. Verify that clicking the speaker button toggles speakerphone mode on and off.
5. Verify that clicking the end call button terminates the call and returns to the group chat conversation.

Negative:
6. Verify that if the call is not answered within 30 seconds, a "Call not answered" message is displayed.
7. Verify that if the recipient declines the call, a "Call declined" message is displayed.
8. Verify that if the device lacks microphone permission, the system displays "Microphone access is required to make a call."
9. Verify that clicking "Cancel" during the calling state aborts the call and returns to chat.

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the phone icon is positioned in the chat header near the group/contact name as per the Figma design.
11. Verify that the calling overlay displays the group name or contact name prominently with a pulsing animation during the "Calling..." state.
12. Verify that the call controls (mute, speaker, end call) are displayed as circular buttons at the bottom of the calling overlay.

Edge Cases:
13. Verify that if the network connection drops during a call, the system shows "Connection lost. Attempting to reconnect..." and retries for 10 seconds.
14. Verify that initiating a call while already on another call displays a warning "You are already on a call".

Security:
15. Verify that voice calls are transmitted over an encrypted connection.
16. Verify that only authorized group members or contacts can receive calls initiated from the chat.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the calling overlay appears within 1 second of clicking the phone icon.
Verify that the call connects within 5 seconds after the recipient answers.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to see timestamps on chat messages showing when each message was sent, so that I can track the conversation timeline.
Description of User Story: Each message in the chat thread displays a timestamp indicating when the message was sent. Timestamps are shown below or adjacent to each message bubble, displaying the time in a relative format for recent messages (e.g., "2:35 PM", "Yesterday at 3:10 PM") and a full date-time format for older messages (e.g., "Mar 28, 2026 at 11:00 AM"). Messages sent on the same day are grouped under a date separator header (e.g., "Today", "Yesterday", "March 28, 2026") that appears between message groups when the date changes. Individual message timestamps show the time only (e.g., "2:35 PM") for messages within the current day. The timestamps are displayed in the family contact's local timezone based on their device or profile settings. The timestamp text uses a smaller, lighter font compared to the message text to maintain readability without visual clutter.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a message is displayed in the chat thread, Then the system must show a timestamp below or adjacent to the message bubble.

2. If the message was sent today, Then the system must display only the time (e.g., "2:35 PM").
   Else if the message was sent yesterday, Then the system must display "Yesterday at [time]".
   Else if the message was sent within the current week, Then the system must display the day name and time (e.g., "Monday at 10:00 AM").
   Else if the message was sent more than a week ago, Then the system must display the full date and time (e.g., "Mar 28, 2026 at 11:00 AM").

3. If the conversation spans multiple days, Then the system must insert a date separator header between messages from different dates.

4. If the family contact's timezone differs from the server, Then the system must convert and display timestamps in the family contact's local timezone.

5. If two consecutive messages from the same sender are sent within 1 minute, Then the system must display the timestamp only on the last message in the cluster to reduce clutter.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that a message sent at 2:35 PM today displays "2:35 PM" below the message bubble.
2. Verify that a message sent yesterday at 3:10 PM displays "Yesterday at 3:10 PM".
3. Verify that a message sent on March 28, 2026 at 11:00 AM displays "Mar 28, 2026 at 11:00 AM".
4. Verify that a date separator "Today" appears above today's messages and "Yesterday" above yesterday's messages.
5. Verify that two messages sent by the same person within 30 seconds show the timestamp only on the last message.

Negative:
6. Verify that a message with a missing or null timestamp displays a fallback text such as "Time unavailable".
7. Verify that the date separator does not appear between consecutive messages from the same date.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that timestamps are displayed in a smaller, lighter font (e.g., grey, 11px) compared to the message text.
9. Verify that date separator headers are centered between message groups with a horizontal line on either side.
10. Verify that timestamps on sent messages (blue bubbles) are right-aligned and on received messages (grey bubbles) are left-aligned.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a family contact in Pacific timezone sees the correct local time for a message sent at 5:00 PM Eastern.
12. Verify that a message sent on Monday at 11:59 PM and a response on Tuesday at 12:01 AM show a date separator between them.

Security:
13. Verify that timestamps cannot be manipulated or spoofed by modifying client-side data.
14. Verify that server-side timestamps are used as the source of truth for all displayed times.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that timestamps render correctly for all messages within a conversation of 100+ messages without layout lag.
Verify that date separator headers insert correctly within 500 milliseconds of loading the conversation.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to see unread message count badges (e.g., '4') next to the Chat menu item in the sidebar, so that I know when new messages are waiting.
Description of User Story: The unread message count badge is displayed next to the "Chat" menu item in the portal sidebar navigation. The badge shows a numeric count of the total unread messages across all conversations (group and individual) for the Approved Family Contact. The badge is a small circular or pill-shaped indicator, typically in red or the portal's accent color, positioned to the right of the "Chat" label. When new messages arrive, the badge count updates in real time without requiring a page refresh. Clicking the Chat menu item navigates to the Chat section, and once all messages are read, the badge disappears. If the unread count exceeds 99, the badge displays "99+" to maintain a compact size. The badge is visible on all portal pages as it is part of the persistent sidebar navigation. The unread count is calculated server-side to ensure accuracy even across multiple devices.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact has unread messages, Then the system must display a badge with the total unread count next to the "Chat" menu item in the sidebar.

2. If the unread count is between 1 and 99, Then the system must display the exact numeric count.
   Else if the unread count exceeds 99, Then the system must display "99+".
   Else if the unread count is 0, Then the system must hide the badge entirely.

3. If a new message arrives while the family contact is on a non-Chat page, Then the system must update the badge count in real time via WebSocket or polling.

4. If the family contact reads all messages in a conversation, Then the system must decrement the badge count by the number of messages read.
   Else if the family contact reads all messages across all conversations, Then the system must remove the badge.

5. If the family contact is logged in on multiple devices, Then the badge count must synchronize across devices based on the server-side unread count.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Chat menu item in the sidebar displays a badge with "4" when there are 4 unread messages.
2. Verify that the badge count updates in real time to "5" when a new message arrives without page refresh.
3. Verify that navigating to the Chat section and reading all messages removes the badge from the sidebar.
4. Verify that the badge is visible on all portal pages (Dashboard, Billing, Profile, etc.) as part of the sidebar.
5. Verify that reading 2 out of 4 unread messages decrements the badge to "2".

Negative:
6. Verify that when there are 0 unread messages, no badge is displayed next to the Chat menu item.
7. Verify that a family contact who has muted a group chat still sees unread badges for that group's messages.
8. Verify that messages from blocked or deactivated contacts do not increment the badge count.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the unread badge is displayed as a small red circular or pill-shaped indicator positioned to the right of the "Chat" label in the sidebar.
10. Verify that the badge displays "99+" when there are more than 99 unread messages, maintaining a compact size.
11. Verify that the badge has sufficient contrast against the sidebar background for readability.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that reading messages on one device updates the badge count on another logged-in device in real time.
13. Verify that rapidly receiving 10 messages within 5 seconds correctly updates the badge count to reflect all 10.
14. Verify that the badge count recalculates correctly after the portal is refreshed.

Security:
15. Verify that the unread count API returns data only for the authenticated family contact's conversations.
16. Verify that the badge count cannot be manipulated via client-side scripts or API tampering.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the unread badge count updates within 3 seconds of a new message arriving.
Verify that the badge renders correctly on initial page load within 1 second across all portal pages.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view documents shared by the facility and digitally sign documents that require my consent.
Description of User Story: The Document Viewing and Digital Signing feature allows Approved Family Contacts to access documents shared by the care facility and provide digital consent signatures where required. Upon navigating to the Documents section, the family contact sees a list of all documents shared by the facility, each displaying the document name, type, shared date, and a signing status indicator (Pending Signature, Signed, Not Required). Documents requiring consent display a prominent "Sign" button. When the family contact clicks "Sign", the system opens the document in a preview pane showing the full content, followed by a digital signature area at the bottom. The signature area supports drawing a signature on a canvas pad or typing a name to generate a signature. Before signing, the family contact must acknowledge a consent checkbox confirming they have read and agree to the document contents. Upon submission, the system records the digital signature along with a timestamp, the signer's name, IP address, and device information for audit purposes. The signed document is then updated to "Signed" status and a confirmation message is displayed. The family contact can also view previously signed documents and their signatures at any time.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact navigates to the Documents section, Then the system must display all facility-shared documents with their current signing status.

2. If the document requires consent and the family contact has not signed it, Then the system must display the document with a "Pending Signature" badge and a "Sign" button.
   Else if the document has already been signed by the family contact, Then the system must display a "Signed" badge with the signature date.
   Else if the document does not require a signature, Then the system must display a "Not Required" badge with no sign action.

3. If the family contact attempts to submit a signature without checking the consent acknowledgment checkbox, Then the system must display "Please acknowledge that you have read and agree to the document contents" and block submission.
   Else if the signature canvas is empty and no typed signature is provided, Then the system must display "Please provide your signature before submitting".

4. If the digital signature submission fails due to a server error, Then the system must display "Unable to submit signature. Please try again." with a retry option and must not mark the document as signed.

5. If the document content has been updated by the facility after the family contact previously signed it, Then the system must require a new signature and display "This document has been updated. Please review and sign the latest version."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating to the Documents section displays a list of 5 facility-shared documents with correct names, types, shared dates, and signing statuses.
2. Verify that clicking "Sign" on a document requiring consent opens the full document preview with a digital signature area at the bottom.
3. Verify that drawing a signature on the canvas pad, checking the consent checkbox, and clicking "Submit Signature" records the signature and updates the status to "Signed".
4. Verify that typing a name in the signature field generates a stylized signature that can be submitted.
5. Verify that a signed document displays the recorded signature, signer name, and signature date when viewed again.

Negative:
6. Verify that attempting to submit a signature without checking the consent acknowledgment checkbox displays "Please acknowledge that you have read and agree to the document contents".
7. Verify that attempting to submit with an empty signature canvas displays "Please provide your signature before submitting".
8. Verify that when the signature submission service fails, the system displays "Unable to submit signature. Please try again." and does not change the document status.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that documents requiring signature display an orange "Pending Signature" badge and a "Sign" button, signed documents display a green "Signed" badge, and documents not requiring signature display a grey "Not Required" badge.
10. Verify that the signing view displays the document content in a scrollable preview pane above the signature area with clear "Draw" and "Type" tabs for signature input.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that if a facility updates a previously signed document, the status reverts to "Pending Signature" with a message "This document has been updated. Please review and sign the latest version."
12. Verify that the audit record includes the timestamp, signer name, IP address, and device information after signing.

Security:
13. Verify that the digital signature is stored with tamper-proof audit metadata and the signed document cannot be modified after signature.
14. Verify that only the authorized Approved Family Contact can sign documents and another user cannot forge a signature through API manipulation.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Documents section loads the list of facility-shared documents within 2 seconds for up to 50 documents.
Verify that the document preview and signature area renders within 3 seconds for a document up to 10 pages.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to upload documents (e.g., insurance cards, hospital papers).
Description of User Story: The Document Upload feature allows Approved Family Contacts to upload personal documents such as insurance cards, hospital discharge papers, medical records, and other supporting files to the portal. The upload interface provides a drag-and-drop zone and a "Browse Files" button to select files from the local device. Supported file formats include PNG, JPG, and PDF with a maximum file size of 5MB per file. The family contact can upload multiple files in a single session. Each uploaded file displays a preview thumbnail (for images) or a file icon (for PDFs), the file name, file size, and a delete icon to remove the file before saving. A document name field allows the family contact to provide a descriptive name for each uploaded file. Upon clicking "Upload" or "Save", the files are transmitted to the server with a progress bar showing the upload percentage and estimated time remaining. After successful upload, the documents appear in the family contact's My Space section with the provided names, upload date, and file type indicators.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact initiates a file upload, Then the system must validate the file format and size before adding it to the upload queue.

2. If the file format is PNG, JPG, or PDF and the file size is under 5MB, Then the system must add the file to the upload queue and display a preview.
   Else if the file format is not supported, Then the system must display "Unsupported file format. Please upload PNG, JPG, or PDF files only."
   Else if the file size exceeds 5MB, Then the system must display "File size exceeds the 5MB limit. Please select a smaller file."

3. If the upload succeeds, Then the system must display a success message and add the document to My Space.
   Else if the upload fails due to a network error, Then the system must display "Upload failed. Please check your connection and try again." with a retry option.

4. If the family contact does not provide a document name, Then the system must use the original file name as the document name.

5. If the family contact uploads a file with the same name as an existing document, Then the system must prompt "A document with this name already exists. Do you want to replace it or keep both?"
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the upload interface displays a drag-and-drop zone with instructional text and a "Browse Files" button.
2. Verify that dragging and dropping a 2MB PNG file into the upload zone adds it to the queue with a thumbnail preview, file name, size "2 MB", and a delete icon.
3. Verify that selecting a JPG file via "Browse Files" adds it to the upload queue with correct details.
4. Verify that entering the document name "Insurance Card Front" and clicking "Upload" uploads the file successfully with a progress bar reaching 100%.
5. Verify that after successful upload, the document "Insurance Card Front" appears in My Space with the current date and PNG file type indicator.

Negative:
6. Verify that uploading a .docx file displays "Unsupported file format. Please upload PNG, JPG, or PDF files only."
7. Verify that uploading a 7MB file displays "File size exceeds the 5MB limit. Please select a smaller file."
8. Verify that when a network error occurs during upload, the system displays "Upload failed. Please check your connection and try again." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the drag-and-drop zone highlights with a dashed blue border when a file is dragged over it and shows accepted file format hints (.png, .jpg, up to 5MB).
10. Verify that the upload queue displays each file with a thumbnail or file icon, name, size, and a red delete icon aligned to the right.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that uploading a file without entering a document name uses the original file name as the document name.
12. Verify that uploading a file with a duplicate name prompts "A document with this name already exists. Do you want to replace it or keep both?"
13. Verify that uploading 10 files simultaneously queues them all and uploads them sequentially with individual progress bars.

Security:
14. Verify that uploaded files are scanned for malware before being stored and malicious files are rejected with an appropriate error message.
15. Verify that uploaded documents are accessible only to the authenticated family contact and authorized facility staff.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that a 5MB file uploads completely within 10 seconds on a standard broadband connection.
Verify that the upload queue renders previews for up to 10 files within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view the signing status of documents.
Description of User Story: The Document Signing Status feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a clear overview of the signing status of all documents that require their consent or acknowledgment. The Documents section displays each document with a status indicator showing one of the following states: "Pending Signature" (awaiting the family contact's signature), "Signed" (the family contact has signed the document), "Expired" (the document's signing deadline has passed without a signature), or "Not Required" (no signature needed). The status is displayed as a color-coded badge next to each document entry. For documents with "Signed" status, the family contact can view the signature date and the name of the signer. For "Pending Signature" documents, the system shows the due date if applicable. The family contact can filter documents by signing status to quickly find documents requiring action. A summary counter at the top shows the number of documents in each status category, allowing the family contact to identify pending items at a glance.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact views the Documents section, Then the system must display a signing status badge for every document that requires or required a signature.

2. If the document status is "Pending Signature" and a due date is set, Then the system must display the due date alongside the badge.
   Else if the document status is "Pending Signature" and the due date is within 48 hours, Then the system must display the badge with an urgent visual indicator (e.g., red border or exclamation icon).
   Else if the document status is "Expired", Then the system must display "Signing deadline has passed" and disable the sign action.

3. If the family contact filters by a status that has no matching documents, Then the system must display "No documents found with the selected status" with a clear-filter option.

4. If a document's signing deadline is extended by the facility, Then the system must update the status from "Expired" back to "Pending Signature" with the new due date.

5. If multiple family contacts are required to sign the same document, Then the system must show each signer's status individually (e.g., "Signed by Jane Doe on March 20, 2026; Pending from John Doe").
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Documents section displays 8 documents with correct signing status badges: 3 Pending Signature, 3 Signed, 1 Expired, and 1 Not Required.
2. Verify that a document with "Signed" status shows the signature date "March 15, 2026" and signer name "Ralph Edwards" in its details.
3. Verify that a document with "Pending Signature" status shows the due date "April 10, 2026" next to the badge.
4. Verify that the summary counters at the top display "3 Pending", "3 Signed", "1 Expired", and "1 Not Required".
5. Verify that filtering by "Pending Signature" displays only the 3 documents awaiting signature.

Negative:
6. Verify that filtering by a status with no matching documents displays "No documents found with the selected status" with a clear-filter option.
7. Verify that an expired document disables the "Sign" button and displays "Signing deadline has passed".

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that status badges are color-coded: orange for Pending Signature, green for Signed, red for Expired, and grey for Not Required.
9. Verify that documents with a due date within 48 hours display an urgent visual indicator such as a red border or exclamation icon on the Pending Signature badge.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that when a facility extends a signing deadline, the document status updates from "Expired" back to "Pending Signature" with the new due date.
11. Verify that a document requiring signatures from two family contacts shows individual signer statuses (e.g., "Signed by Jane Doe; Pending from John Doe").

Security:
12. Verify that signing status information is accessible only to the authorized family contact and facility administrators.
13. Verify that the signing status cannot be tampered with via client-side manipulation or direct API calls.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Documents section with signing statuses loads within 2 seconds for up to 100 documents.
Verify that filtering documents by signing status refreshes the list within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view the 'Mandatory' tab listing all required documents (e.g., Intake Form, Contract Of Patient, State Specific Documents, TB Test, Adult Day Health Agreement) with owner and creation date, so that I know which documents are required.
Description of User Story: The Mandatory tab within the Documents section presents Approved Family Contacts with a comprehensive list of all required documents for the patient's care. This tab is the first of three tabs (Mandatory, My Space, Group Space) in the Documents section. The Mandatory tab displays documents in a list format with columns for Document Name, Owner (the person or facility department responsible for the document), and Created Date. Required documents include but are not limited to: Intake Form, Contract Of Patient, State Specific Documents, TB Test, and Adult Day Health Agreement. Each document row indicates whether the document has been completed, is pending, or requires action from the family contact. The list is pre-populated by the facility based on regulatory and organizational requirements. Documents that require the family contact's attention are visually highlighted. The family contact can click on any document to view its content, download it, or take required action such as signing.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact navigates to the Documents section, Then the system must display the Mandatory tab as the default active tab with all required documents loaded.

2. If all mandatory documents are completed, Then the system must display a "All required documents are complete" banner at the top of the list.
   Else if some mandatory documents are pending, Then the system must display them with a "Pending" or "Action Required" badge and sort them to the top of the list.
   Else if a mandatory document is overdue, Then the system must highlight it with a red "Overdue" badge.

3. If the facility has not yet assigned any mandatory documents to the patient, Then the system must display "No mandatory documents have been assigned yet."

4. If the family contact clicks on a mandatory document that requires signing, Then the system must open the document in a preview and signing view.
   Else if the document is read-only, Then the system must open it in view-only mode with a download option.

5. If a new mandatory document is added by the facility, Then the system must display a notification indicator on the Mandatory tab showing the count of new or unreviewed documents.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Documents section defaults to the Mandatory tab showing all required documents in a list format.
2. Verify that the Mandatory tab lists "Intake Form" with owner "Nemi Care Admin" and created date "March 1, 2026".
3. Verify that the list includes "Contract Of Patient", "State Specific Documents", "TB Test", and "Adult Day Health Agreement" with correct owners and dates.
4. Verify that clicking on "Intake Form" opens the document content in a preview pane with download and sign options.
5. Verify that documents requiring action are highlighted with an "Action Required" badge and sorted to the top.

Negative:
6. Verify that when no mandatory documents have been assigned, the system displays "No mandatory documents have been assigned yet."
7. Verify that attempting to access a mandatory document that has been removed by the facility displays an appropriate error message.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the Mandatory tab displays documents in a list with three columns: Document Name, Owner, and Created Date, with clear column headers.
9. Verify that documents with "Action Required" badges are visually distinct from completed documents, using color or icon differentiation.
10. Verify that the three tabs (Mandatory, My Space, Group Space) are displayed as a horizontal tab bar at the top of the Documents section.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that when a facility adds a new mandatory document, the Mandatory tab shows a notification indicator with the count of new documents.
12. Verify that a mandatory document marked as overdue displays a red "Overdue" badge with the overdue duration.

Security:
13. Verify that the Mandatory document list is populated only by the facility and cannot be altered by the family contact.
14. Verify that document content is served over an encrypted connection and requires authentication to access.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Mandatory tab loads all required documents within 2 seconds for up to 20 mandatory documents.
Verify that clicking on a mandatory document opens the preview within 3 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view the 'My Space' tab showing my personal folders and documents in both grid and list views, so that I can manage my own uploaded files.
Description of User Story: The My Space tab within the Documents section provides Approved Family Contacts with a personal file management area where they can view and organize their own uploaded documents. The tab displays personal folders and individual documents created or uploaded by the family contact. Two view modes are available: grid view shows folders and documents as visual cards with thumbnails or file type icons, names, and creation dates; list view shows them in a table format with columns for Name, Owner, and Created Date. A toggle control at the top allows switching between grid and list views. Folders are displayed with folder icons and documents with file type icons (image icon for PNG/JPG, document icon for PDF). The family contact can click on a folder to navigate into it and view its contents, or click on a document to preview or download it. The My Space tab supports creating new folders, uploading documents, and organizing files by moving them between folders. An empty state message appears when no folders or documents exist yet.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the My Space tab, Then the system must display all personal folders and documents belonging to the family contact in the last used view mode (grid or list).

2. If the family contact toggles to grid view, Then the system must render folders and documents as visual cards with thumbnails, names, and creation dates.
   Else if the family contact toggles to list view, Then the system must render them in a table with Name, Owner, and Created Date columns.

3. If My Space contains no folders or documents, Then the system must display "Your personal space is empty. Upload documents or create a folder to get started." with action buttons.

4. If the family contact clicks on a folder, Then the system must navigate into the folder and display its contents using the same view mode.
   Else if the family contact clicks on a document, Then the system must open a preview pane or download the file based on file type.

5. If the system fails to load My Space data, Then the system must display "Unable to load your documents. Please try again." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the "My Space" tab displays 3 personal folders and 2 individual documents belonging to the family contact.
2. Verify that toggling to grid view shows folders and documents as cards with thumbnail images for JPG files, folder icons for folders, and document icons for PDFs.
3. Verify that toggling to list view shows a table with Name, Owner ("Ralph Edwards"), and Created Date columns for all items.
4. Verify that clicking on a folder named "Insurance Documents" navigates into the folder and displays its 4 contents.
5. Verify that clicking on a PNG document opens a preview pane showing the image.

Negative:
6. Verify that when My Space has no folders or documents, the system displays "Your personal space is empty. Upload documents or create a folder to get started." with action buttons.
7. Verify that when the My Space data fails to load, the system displays "Unable to load your documents. Please try again." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the grid view displays folders with blue folder icons and documents with file-type-specific icons arranged in a responsive card layout.
9. Verify that the list view displays a properly aligned table with alternating row shading and sortable column headers.
10. Verify that the grid/list toggle control is positioned at the top-right of the My Space tab content area.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a folder containing 50 items displays all contents with proper pagination or infinite scroll.
12. Verify that the view mode preference (grid or list) persists across tab switches and page reloads.

Security:
13. Verify that My Space displays only the authenticated family contact's own files and does not expose other users' documents.
14. Verify that file access URLs require authentication and cannot be shared externally.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the My Space tab loads all folders and documents within 2 seconds for up to 100 items.
Verify that toggling between grid and list views re-renders within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view the 'Group Space' tab listing shared document groups with Group Name, Description, Owner, Members (with avatars), and Created Date, so that I can access shared document areas.
Description of User Story: The Group Space tab within the Documents section allows Approved Family Contacts to access shared document groups created by the facility or other authorized members. The tab displays a list of document groups, each presented as a card or row showing the Group Name, Description, Owner name, Members represented by avatar circles (with a "+N" indicator for additional members beyond the displayed count), and Created Date. Groups are shared workspaces where multiple family contacts or facility staff can collaborate on and share documents related to the patient's care. Each group card is clickable and navigates the family contact into the group to view its folders and documents. The Group Space tab enables the family contact to see all groups they have been added to, ensuring they can access shared care documents, insurance paperwork, and collaborative files. Groups they are not a member of are not visible.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the Group Space tab, Then the system must display all shared document groups the family contact is a member of.

2. If the family contact is not a member of any groups, Then the system must display "You are not a member of any shared groups. Contact the facility for access."
   Else if the family contact is a member of groups, Then the system must display each group with its Name, Description, Owner, Members, and Created Date.

3. If a group has more than 4 members, Then the system must display 4 member avatars and a "+N" badge showing the remaining count.
   Else if a group has 4 or fewer members, Then the system must display all member avatars without a "+N" badge.

4. If the family contact clicks on a group, Then the system must navigate into the group and display its contents (folders and documents).

5. If a group the family contact was previously a member of is now removed, Then the system must no longer display that group in the Group Space tab.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the Group Space tab displays 3 shared document groups the family contact is a member of.
2. Verify that a group card for "Patient Care Documents" shows the group name, description "Shared documents for patient care coordination", owner "Dr. Smith", 5 member avatars (4 displayed + "+1"), and created date "February 10, 2026".
3. Verify that clicking on the "Patient Care Documents" group navigates into the group showing its folders and documents.
4. Verify that a group with 3 members displays all 3 member avatars without a "+N" indicator.
5. Verify that groups the family contact is not a member of are not visible in the Group Space tab.

Negative:
6. Verify that a family contact who is not a member of any groups sees "You are not a member of any shared groups. Contact the facility for access."
7. Verify that a group removed from the family contact's membership no longer appears in the Group Space tab after page refresh.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that each group is displayed as a card with the group name in bold, description in regular text, owner label, member avatars in a horizontal row, and created date at the bottom.
9. Verify that member avatars are circular with initials or profile images and the "+N" badge is styled distinctly.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a group with no description displays the card without a description field, maintaining proper layout.
11. Verify that a group with 20 members displays 4 avatars and a "+16" badge.

Security:
12. Verify that the Group Space tab only displays groups where the authenticated family contact is an active member.
13. Verify that direct URL access to a group the family contact is not a member of returns an authorization error.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Group Space tab loads all group cards within 2 seconds for up to 20 groups.
Verify that navigating into a group and loading its contents completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to click on a group in Group Space to view its contents (folders and documents inside the group), so that I can access shared files.
Description of User Story: The Group Contents View allows Approved Family Contacts to navigate into a specific group within the Group Space tab and view all folders and documents contained within that group. When the family contact clicks on a group card, the system transitions to an inner view showing the group's contents organized into folders and individual documents. The view supports both grid and list modes, consistent with the My Space tab. Folders are displayed with folder icons and documents with file-type-specific icons. The group header area shows the group name, description, and member count. A breadcrumb navigation trail (e.g., "Documents > Group Space > Patient Care Documents") allows the family contact to navigate back to the Group Space tab or the main Documents page. The family contact can click on folders to drill deeper into nested structures and click on documents to preview or download them. The contents are displayed with Name, Owner, and Created Date columns in list view or as visual cards in grid view.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks on a group, Then the system must load and display all folders and documents within that group.

2. If the group contains no folders or documents, Then the system must display "This group has no documents yet."
   Else if the group has contents, Then the system must display them in the current view mode (grid or list).

3. If the family contact clicks a breadcrumb link, Then the system must navigate to the corresponding level (Group Space tab or Documents main page).

4. If a folder within the group is empty, Then the system must display "This folder is empty" when the family contact navigates into it.

5. If the group contents fail to load, Then the system must display "Unable to load group contents. Please try again." with a retry option.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking on the "Patient Care Documents" group displays 2 folders and 3 individual documents inside the group.
2. Verify that the group header shows "Patient Care Documents", description "Shared documents for patient care coordination", and "5 members".
3. Verify that the breadcrumb shows "Documents > Group Space > Patient Care Documents" and each segment is clickable.
4. Verify that clicking on a folder "Insurance Files" within the group shows its 3 documents.
5. Verify that clicking on a document within the group opens a preview pane with download option.

Negative:
6. Verify that a group with no contents displays "This group has no documents yet."
7. Verify that an empty folder within a group displays "This folder is empty."
8. Verify that when group contents fail to load, the system displays "Unable to load group contents. Please try again." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the group contents view maintains the same grid/list toggle and visual layout as the My Space tab for consistency.
10. Verify that the breadcrumb navigation is displayed at the top of the content area with clickable segments separated by ">" symbols.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that navigating 3 levels deep into nested folders (Group > Folder A > Subfolder B) displays correct breadcrumb trail and contents at each level.
12. Verify that clicking the "Group Space" breadcrumb link from a deeply nested folder returns to the Group Space tab correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that only group members can view the group's contents and non-members receive an authorization error.
14. Verify that document download URLs within a group require valid authentication and group membership.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that navigating into a group and loading its contents completes within 2 seconds for up to 50 items.
Verify that breadcrumb navigation between levels completes within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to create a new folder in My Space by entering a Folder Name and optionally uploading documents during creation, so that I can organize my files.
Description of User Story: The Create Folder feature allows Approved Family Contacts to create new folders within their My Space area to organize personal documents. When the family contact clicks a "Create Folder" or "New Folder" button, a modal dialog appears with a text input field for the Folder Name and an optional document upload area. The Folder Name field requires at least one character and does not allow special characters that are invalid in file system paths. The optional upload area within the modal supports drag-and-drop or browse functionality, allowing the family contact to upload documents directly into the new folder during creation. The family contact can create the folder empty (without uploading documents) or with one or more documents attached. Upon clicking "Create" or "Save", the system validates the folder name, creates the folder in My Space, and uploads any attached documents. The new folder appears immediately in the My Space view. If the family contact cancels the modal, no folder is created and no uploads occur.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks "Create Folder", Then the system must display a modal with a Folder Name input and an optional upload area.

2. If the family contact enters a valid folder name and clicks "Create", Then the system must create the folder and display it in My Space.
   Else if the folder name is empty, Then the system must display "Folder name is required" and block creation.
   Else if the folder name contains invalid characters (e.g., /, \, :, *, ?, <, >, |), Then the system must display "Folder name contains invalid characters" and block creation.

3. If the family contact uploads documents during folder creation, Then the system must create the folder first and then upload the documents into it.
   Else if no documents are uploaded, Then the system must create an empty folder.

4. If a folder with the same name already exists in My Space at the same level, Then the system must display "A folder with this name already exists. Please choose a different name."

5. If the folder creation fails due to a server error, Then the system must display "Unable to create folder. Please try again." and must not partially create the folder.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Create Folder" opens a modal with a Folder Name text input and an optional drag-and-drop upload area.
2. Verify that entering "Medical Records" and clicking "Create" creates a folder named "Medical Records" in My Space with today's date.
3. Verify that creating a folder with 2 documents attached results in the folder containing both documents after creation.
4. Verify that creating an empty folder (no documents uploaded) results in an empty folder appearing in My Space.
5. Verify that clicking "Cancel" on the Create Folder modal closes it without creating any folder.

Negative:
6. Verify that leaving the Folder Name field empty and clicking "Create" displays "Folder name is required".
7. Verify that entering a folder name with "/" or "\" characters displays "Folder name contains invalid characters".
8. Verify that creating a folder with a name that already exists displays "A folder with this name already exists. Please choose a different name."
9. Verify that when folder creation fails due to a server error, the system displays "Unable to create folder. Please try again."

UI Scenarios:
10. Verify that the Create Folder modal has a clear title "Create New Folder", a text input labeled "Folder Name", an optional upload area with drag-and-drop support, and "Create" and "Cancel" buttons.
11. Verify that the upload area within the modal shows accepted file format hints and size limits.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that a folder name with 255 characters is accepted and displayed correctly (truncated with ellipsis if needed in the UI).
13. Verify that uploading a document that exceeds the 5MB limit during folder creation shows the file size error within the modal.

Security:
14. Verify that folder creation API requires authentication and cannot be invoked by unauthenticated users.
15. Verify that folder names are sanitized to prevent path traversal or injection attacks.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the Create Folder modal opens within 1 second of clicking the button.
Verify that folder creation with 3 attached documents completes within 5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to upload a document within a folder by providing a Document Name and dragging-and-dropping or browsing for files (.png, .jpg, up to 5MB), so that I can add files to specific locations.
Description of User Story: The In-Folder Document Upload feature allows Approved Family Contacts to upload documents directly into a specific folder within My Space. When the family contact is inside a folder and clicks an "Upload" or "Add Document" button, an upload interface appears with a Document Name text field and a file selection area supporting drag-and-drop and browse functionality. The family contact enters a descriptive Document Name and selects a file from their device. Supported file formats are .png and .jpg with a maximum file size of 5MB. The upload area displays hints about accepted formats and size limits (e.g., "Accepted: .png, .jpg | Max size: 5MB"). Once a file is selected, it appears in the upload queue showing a thumbnail preview, file name, file size, and a delete icon to remove it before upload. The family contact can click "Upload" to begin the file transfer, which is tracked by a progress bar showing percentage complete and estimated time remaining. Upon successful upload, the document appears within the current folder with the provided name, the current date as the creation date, and the family contact as the owner.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks "Upload" inside a folder, Then the system must display the upload interface with a Document Name field and file selection area showing format and size hints.

2. If the selected file is .png or .jpg and under 5MB, Then the system must add it to the upload queue with a preview.
   Else if the file format is not .png or .jpg, Then the system must display "Unsupported file format. Please upload .png or .jpg files only."
   Else if the file exceeds 5MB, Then the system must display "File size exceeds the 5MB limit. Please select a smaller file."

3. If the Document Name field is left empty, Then the system must use the original file name as the document name.

4. If the upload is interrupted due to a network error, Then the system must display "Upload failed. Please check your connection and try again." with a retry option.

5. If the family contact clicks the delete icon on a queued file, Then the system must remove the file from the queue without any API call.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking "Upload" inside the "Insurance Documents" folder displays the upload interface with a Document Name field and drag-and-drop area showing "Accepted: .png, .jpg | Max size: 5MB".
2. Verify that entering "Insurance Card Front" and dropping a 1.5MB .png file adds it to the queue with thumbnail, name "insurance-card.png", size "1.5 MB", and a delete icon.
3. Verify that clicking "Upload" starts the transfer with a progress bar showing percentage (0% to 100%) and estimated time remaining.
4. Verify that after successful upload, "Insurance Card Front" appears in the folder with today's date and owner "Ralph Edwards".
5. Verify that clicking the delete icon on a queued file removes it from the queue.

Negative:
6. Verify that selecting a .docx file displays "Unsupported file format. Please upload .png or .jpg files only."
7. Verify that selecting a 6MB file displays "File size exceeds the 5MB limit. Please select a smaller file."
8. Verify that a network interruption during upload displays "Upload failed. Please check your connection and try again." with a retry button.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the drag-and-drop area has a dashed border, a cloud upload icon, and text "Drag and drop files here or browse" with format and size hints below.
10. Verify that the progress bar displays a green fill with percentage text and estimated time remaining below it.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that leaving the Document Name empty and uploading uses the original file name "insurance-card.png" as the document name.
12. Verify that uploading a file exactly 5MB in size is accepted and uploads successfully.

Security:
13. Verify that uploaded files are validated server-side for format and size, not just client-side.
14. Verify that the upload endpoint requires authentication and rejects requests from unauthenticated users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that a 5MB file uploads within 10 seconds on a standard broadband connection.
Verify that the upload progress bar updates in real time with less than 1 second lag.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to toggle between grid view and list view for documents, so that I can choose my preferred way of browsing files.
Description of User Story: The View Toggle feature provides Approved Family Contacts with the ability to switch between grid view and list view when browsing documents and folders in the My Space and Group Space tabs. A toggle control consisting of a grid icon and a list icon is positioned at the top-right of the document browsing area. In grid view, folders and documents are displayed as visual cards arranged in a responsive multi-column layout, each showing a thumbnail or file-type icon, the item name, and the creation date. In list view, folders and documents are displayed in a tabular format with columns for Name, Owner, and Created Date, with rows that are easily scannable. The selected view mode is visually indicated by the active icon being highlighted. The view preference persists within the session so that navigating between folders or tabs retains the chosen mode. Both views support the same actions: clicking to open, right-clicking for context menus, and selecting items.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the grid view icon, Then the system must re-render all folders and documents as visual cards in a responsive multi-column layout.

2. If the family contact clicks the list view icon, Then the system must re-render all items in a tabular format with Name, Owner, and Created Date columns.
   Else if the current view is already list view and the family contact clicks the list icon again, Then no change must occur.

3. If the family contact selects grid view and then navigates into a folder, Then the folder contents must render in grid view.
   Else if the family contact switches tabs, Then the new tab must render in the same view mode.

4. If the document list contains no items, Then both view modes must display the same empty state message.

5. If the view toggle fails to render due to a UI error, Then the system must default to list view as the fallback.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the grid view icon displays folders and documents as cards with thumbnails, names, and creation dates in a multi-column layout.
2. Verify that clicking the list view icon displays items in a table with Name, Owner, and Created Date columns.
3. Verify that the active view icon (grid or list) is highlighted with a blue background or border.
4. Verify that switching to grid view and navigating into a folder shows the folder contents in grid view.
5. Verify that the view mode persists when switching between My Space and Group Space tabs.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking the already-active view icon does not cause a re-render or flicker.
7. Verify that if the view toggle UI fails to load, the system defaults to list view.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the toggle control is positioned at the top-right of the document area with a grid icon (4-square) and a list icon (horizontal lines) side by side.
9. Verify that grid view cards are responsive and adjust from 4 columns on desktop to 2 columns on tablet and 1 column on mobile.
10. Verify that list view displays proper column alignment, alternating row shading, and hover effects on rows.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that toggling views with 100 items renders both views without layout issues or performance lag.
12. Verify that the view mode preference persists after navigating 3 levels deep into nested folders and back.

Security:
13. Verify that the view toggle is a client-side UI feature and does not trigger additional API calls for the same data.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that toggling between grid and list view re-renders up to 100 items within 1 second.
Verify that the view toggle control responds to clicks within 200 milliseconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to search for documents by name across all tabs.
Description of User Story: The Document Search feature provides Approved Family Contacts with the ability to search for documents by name across all three tabs (Mandatory, My Space, and Group Space) in the Documents section. A search bar is prominently placed at the top of the Documents section, above the tab navigation. When the family contact types a search query, the system performs a real-time search across all tabs and displays matching results grouped by their source tab. The search matches document names using a contains/partial-match approach, so typing "insur" would match "Insurance Card" and "Insurance Agreement". Search results display the document name, the tab it belongs to (Mandatory, My Space, or Group Space), the owner, and the file path or folder location. Clicking on a search result navigates the family contact directly to the document within its respective tab and folder. The search bar includes a clear button to reset the search and return to the normal tab view. Search is case-insensitive and supports searching across nested folders.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact types in the search bar, Then the system must perform a real-time search across Mandatory, My Space, and Group Space tabs.

2. If matching documents are found, Then the system must display results grouped by tab with document name, source tab, owner, and folder path.
   Else if no documents match the query, Then the system must display "No documents found matching '[query]'" with a suggestion to check the spelling.

3. If the family contact clicks on a search result, Then the system must navigate to the document's location within its respective tab and folder.

4. If the search query is fewer than 2 characters, Then the system must wait until at least 2 characters are entered before triggering the search.

5. If the search service is temporarily unavailable, Then the system must display "Search is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that typing "Intake" in the search bar returns "Intake Form" from the Mandatory tab with correct owner and date.
2. Verify that typing "insur" returns documents from multiple tabs: "Insurance Card" from My Space and "Insurance Agreement" from Mandatory.
3. Verify that clicking on a search result for a document in My Space > Insurance folder navigates directly to that folder with the document highlighted.
4. Verify that clicking the clear button resets the search and restores the normal tab view.
5. Verify that search is case-insensitive: typing "tb test" matches "TB Test" from Mandatory.

Negative:
6. Verify that searching for "xyz123" with no matching documents displays "No documents found matching 'xyz123'" with a spelling suggestion.
7. Verify that typing only 1 character does not trigger a search.
8. Verify that when the search service is down, the system displays "Search is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later."

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the search bar is positioned at the top of the Documents section above the tab navigation with a search icon and clear button.
10. Verify that search results are grouped by tab (Mandatory, My Space, Group Space) with section headers and show document name, source, owner, and path.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that searching for a document in a nested folder 3 levels deep shows the full path (e.g., "My Space > Folder A > Subfolder B > Document.png").
12. Verify that searching with a query matching 50+ documents displays results with proper pagination or scroll.

Security:
13. Verify that search results only include documents the authenticated family contact has access to and do not expose other users' private documents.
14. Verify that the search query is sanitized to prevent injection attacks.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that search results appear within 1.5 seconds of typing at least 2 characters for a document library of up to 500 documents.
Verify that navigating to a search result from a nested folder completes within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to filter documents on the Documents page.
Description of User Story: The Document Filter feature provides Approved Family Contacts with the ability to filter documents displayed on the Documents page based on various criteria. A filter icon or button is available in the Documents section, and clicking it reveals filter options. Filter criteria include document type (folder, image, PDF), date range (created date from/to), owner, and signing status (Pending, Signed, Not Required). The family contact can apply one or multiple filters simultaneously to narrow down the document list. Active filters are displayed as removable tags or chips above the document list, and a "Clear All Filters" option resets all applied filters. The filtered results update in real time as filters are applied or removed. Filters work within the currently active tab (Mandatory, My Space, or Group Space), filtering only the documents in that tab. The filter panel can be collapsed to reclaim screen space when not in use.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the filter icon, Then the system must display the filter panel with options for document type, date range, owner, and signing status.

2. If the family contact applies a filter that returns matching documents, Then the system must update the document list to show only matching items.
   Else if the applied filters return no results, Then the system must display "No documents match the applied filters" with a "Clear All Filters" option.

3. If the family contact removes a single filter chip, Then the system must re-apply the remaining filters and update the results.
   Else if the family contact clicks "Clear All Filters", Then the system must remove all filters and show the unfiltered document list.

4. If the family contact collapses the filter panel, Then the active filter chips must remain visible above the document list.

5. If the family contact switches tabs with active filters, Then the system must reset filters for the new tab to avoid confusion.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the filter icon reveals filter options for document type, date range, owner, and signing status.
2. Verify that filtering by document type "Image" on the My Space tab displays only PNG and JPG files.
3. Verify that filtering by date range "March 1-31, 2026" displays only documents created in March 2026.
4. Verify that applying two filters simultaneously (type: PDF, owner: "Nemi Care Admin") displays only matching documents.
5. Verify that active filters are displayed as removable chips and clicking "Clear All Filters" restores the full list.

Negative:
6. Verify that applying filters that match no documents displays "No documents match the applied filters" with a "Clear All Filters" link.
7. Verify that switching from My Space to Mandatory tab resets all previously applied filters.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the filter icon is positioned near the search bar and the filter panel slides in or drops down when clicked.
9. Verify that active filter chips display the filter label (e.g., "Type: Image") with an "x" remove button.
10. Verify that the filter panel can be collapsed and the active chips remain visible above the document list.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that applying all filter criteria simultaneously returns the correct intersection of results.
12. Verify that removing one filter from a multi-filter combination correctly updates the results.

Security:
13. Verify that filter parameters are validated server-side and cannot be manipulated to expose unauthorized documents.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that applying a filter updates the document list within 1.5 seconds for up to 200 documents.
Verify that clearing all filters and restoring the full list completes within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to star/favorite documents for quick access.
Description of User Story: The Star/Favorite Documents feature allows Approved Family Contacts to mark specific documents as favorites for quick access. Each document row or card in the Documents section displays a star icon that can be toggled on or off. Clicking the star icon on an unstarred document fills the star (turning it yellow or gold) and adds the document to the family contact's favorites list. Clicking the star again unfills it and removes the document from favorites. A "Favorites" or "Starred" quick-access section or filter is available, allowing the family contact to view all starred documents in one place regardless of which tab or folder they originate from. The star/favorite status persists across sessions, so starred documents remain marked when the family contact logs in again. The star icon is available in both grid and list views and works across all tabs (Mandatory, My Space, Group Space).
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the star icon on an unstarred document, Then the system must mark the document as a favorite and visually fill the star icon.

2. If the family contact clicks the star icon on an already starred document, Then the system must remove the favorite status and unfill the star icon.

3. If the family contact accesses the Favorites section with starred documents, Then the system must display all starred documents from all tabs and folders.
   Else if no documents are starred, Then the system must display "You have no starred documents. Star a document to access it quickly."

4. If the favorite toggle fails due to a server error, Then the system must revert the star icon to its previous state and display "Unable to update favorite status. Please try again."

5. If a starred document is deleted by the facility or removed from a group, Then the system must automatically remove it from the favorites list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that clicking the star icon on an unstarred document fills the star with gold color and the document appears in the Favorites section.
2. Verify that clicking the star icon on a starred document unfills the star and removes the document from the Favorites section.
3. Verify that starring documents from Mandatory, My Space, and Group Space tabs all appear together in the Favorites section.
4. Verify that the star/favorite status persists after logging out and logging back in.
5. Verify that the star icon is visible and functional in both grid view and list view.

Negative:
6. Verify that accessing the Favorites section with no starred documents displays "You have no starred documents. Star a document to access it quickly."
7. Verify that when the favorite toggle fails due to a server error, the star reverts to its previous state with an error message.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the star icon is displayed as an outline (unfilled) for non-favorited documents and a solid gold star for favorited documents.
9. Verify that the star icon in list view is positioned in the first column before the document name.
10. Verify that the star icon in grid view is positioned at the top-right corner of the document card.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a starred document deleted by the facility is automatically removed from the Favorites section.
12. Verify that starring 100 documents and viewing the Favorites section displays all without performance issues.

Security:
13. Verify that a user's starred documents are private and not visible to other family contacts or users.
14. Verify that the favorite status API endpoint requires authentication and operates only on the authenticated user's data.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that toggling the star/favorite status updates the UI within 500 milliseconds.
Verify that the Favorites section loads all starred documents within 2 seconds for up to 100 favorites.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to navigate through nested folders using breadcrumb navigation (e.g., Documents > Folder 1), so that I can easily move between folder levels.
Description of User Story: The Breadcrumb Navigation feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a hierarchical trail of clickable links that show the current folder location within the Documents section. As the family contact navigates into nested folders, the breadcrumb updates to display the full path from the root level (e.g., "Documents > My Space > Folder 1 > Subfolder A"). Each segment in the breadcrumb is clickable, allowing the family contact to jump directly to any parent level without using the back button repeatedly. The breadcrumb is displayed at the top of the document browsing area, below the tab navigation and above the document list. The current folder name (the last segment) is displayed in bold or a different style to indicate it is the active location. When the breadcrumb path becomes too long for the available screen width, the middle segments are truncated with an ellipsis (...) while keeping the first and last segments visible. The breadcrumb updates dynamically as the family contact navigates deeper or clicks back to a parent level.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact navigates into a folder, Then the system must append the folder name to the breadcrumb trail.

2. If the family contact clicks on a breadcrumb segment, Then the system must navigate to that level and update the breadcrumb to reflect the new location.
   Else if the family contact clicks on the last (current) breadcrumb segment, Then no navigation must occur since it is the current location.
