3. If the breadcrumb path exceeds the available width, Then the system must truncate middle segments with "..." while keeping the first segment ("Documents") and the last two segments visible.

4. If the family contact navigates from Group Space into a group and then into a folder, Then the breadcrumb must show "Documents > Group Space > [Group Name] > [Folder Name]".

5. If the breadcrumb data fails to load, Then the system must display a fallback "Documents" link allowing the family contact to return to the root level.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that navigating into "Folder 1" from My Space updates the breadcrumb to "Documents > My Space > Folder 1".
2. Verify that navigating into "Subfolder A" within "Folder 1" updates the breadcrumb to "Documents > My Space > Folder 1 > Subfolder A".
3. Verify that clicking "My Space" in the breadcrumb navigates to the My Space root and updates the breadcrumb to "Documents > My Space".
4. Verify that clicking "Documents" in the breadcrumb navigates to the Documents section root with the tab view.
5. Verify that the breadcrumb in Group Space shows "Documents > Group Space > Patient Care Documents > Files" when navigating into a group folder.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking the last (current) breadcrumb segment does not trigger any navigation or page reload.
7. Verify that if breadcrumb data fails to load, a fallback "Documents" link is displayed.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the breadcrumb is displayed at the top of the document area with segments separated by ">" symbols.
9. Verify that the last breadcrumb segment is styled in bold and non-clickable, while all parent segments are clickable with underline on hover.
10. Verify that a breadcrumb path 6 levels deep truncates middle segments with "..." while showing the first and last two segments.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that navigating back and forth between 5 folder levels correctly updates the breadcrumb at each step.
12. Verify that the breadcrumb handles folder names with spaces and special characters correctly.

Security:
13. Verify that the breadcrumb navigation cannot be manipulated to access folders the family contact does not have permission to view.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the breadcrumb updates within 500 milliseconds when navigating between folder levels.
Verify that clicking a breadcrumb segment and loading the target folder completes within 1.5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to view document metadata including Name, Owner, and Created Date in the list view.
Description of User Story: The Document Metadata Display feature ensures that when Approved Family Contacts view documents in list view, each document row displays essential metadata: the document Name, the Owner (the person who uploaded or created the document), and the Created Date (the date the document was uploaded or created). The list view presents this information in a tabular format with clearly labeled column headers for Name, Owner, and Created Date. The Name column displays the document name as a clickable link or text with a file-type icon (image icon for PNG/JPG, document icon for PDF, folder icon for folders). The Owner column shows the name of the user who owns the document. The Created Date column shows the date in a consistent format (e.g., "Mar 15, 2026"). The columns are sortable, allowing the family contact to sort by name alphabetically, by owner, or by date in ascending or descending order. Column widths are responsive and adjust based on screen size.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact switches to list view, Then the system must display all documents in a table with Name, Owner, and Created Date columns with labeled headers.

2. If the document is an image file (.png, .jpg), Then the Name column must display an image file icon next to the name.
   Else if the document is a PDF, Then the Name column must display a document icon next to the name.
   Else if the item is a folder, Then the Name column must display a folder icon next to the name.

3. If the family contact clicks a column header to sort, Then the system must sort the list by that column and toggle between ascending and descending on subsequent clicks.

4. If the Owner field is not available for a system-generated document, Then the system must display "System" as the owner.

5. If the Created Date is not available, Then the system must display "--" as a placeholder.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the list view displays columns for Name, Owner, and Created Date with proper column headers.
2. Verify that a PNG document row shows an image icon, the document name "Insurance Card", owner "Ralph Edwards", and created date "Mar 15, 2026".
3. Verify that a PDF document row shows a document icon next to the name.
4. Verify that a folder row shows a folder icon next to the folder name.
5. Verify that clicking the "Name" column header sorts documents alphabetically (A-Z) and clicking again reverses to Z-A.
6. Verify that clicking the "Created Date" header sorts documents from newest to oldest and vice versa.

Negative:
7. Verify that a system-generated document with no owner displays "System" in the Owner column.
8. Verify that a document with no created date displays "--" in the Created Date column.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that column headers are bold and clearly labeled, with a sort indicator (arrow icon) visible on the currently sorted column.
10. Verify that the list view is properly aligned with consistent row heights and alternating row backgrounds for readability.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a document with a very long name (100+ characters) is truncated with an ellipsis in the Name column and the full name is shown on hover.
12. Verify that column widths adjust responsively on smaller screens without overlapping.

Security:
13. Verify that metadata displayed in the list view corresponds only to documents the authenticated family contact has access to.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the list view renders metadata for up to 100 documents within 2 seconds.
Verify that sorting by any column updates the list within 1 second.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to see a progress bar when uploading documents showing percentage complete and estimated time remaining, so that I know the upload status.
Description of User Story: The Upload Progress Bar feature provides Approved Family Contacts with real-time visual feedback during document uploads. When a file upload begins, a progress bar appears below or within the upload area showing the percentage of the upload completed (0% to 100%) and the estimated time remaining (e.g., "2 seconds remaining"). The progress bar fills from left to right with a colored fill (e.g., green or blue) proportional to the upload progress. The percentage text updates dynamically as the upload progresses. The estimated time remaining is calculated based on the current upload speed and the remaining data to be transmitted. When the upload reaches 100%, the progress bar briefly shows a "Complete" state with a checkmark before transitioning to the uploaded state. If multiple files are being uploaded, each file has its own individual progress bar. The progress bar also displays the file name being uploaded for clarity. If the upload is paused or interrupted, the progress bar freezes at the current percentage with an error indicator.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a file upload begins, Then the system must display a progress bar with percentage (starting at 0%) and estimated time remaining.

2. If the upload progresses normally, Then the system must update the percentage and estimated time in real time based on current transfer speed.
   Else if the upload speed drops significantly, Then the system must recalculate and update the estimated time remaining accordingly.

3. If the upload reaches 100%, Then the system must display a "Complete" state with a green checkmark and transition the file to the uploaded state.

4. If the upload is interrupted by a network error, Then the system must freeze the progress bar at the current percentage, display a red error indicator, and show "Upload interrupted. Retry?" with a retry button.
   Else if the server rejects the file during upload, Then the system must display "Upload failed" on the progress bar with the rejection reason.

5. If multiple files are uploading, Then the system must display individual progress bars for each file with the respective file names.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that starting a file upload displays a progress bar beginning at 0% with an estimated time (e.g., "5 seconds remaining").
2. Verify that the progress bar updates in real time as the upload progresses, showing 25%, 50%, 75%, and reaching 100%.
3. Verify that the estimated time remaining decreases as the upload progresses and reflects the actual transfer speed.
4. Verify that upon 100% completion, the progress bar shows a green checkmark and "Complete" text.
5. Verify that uploading 3 files simultaneously shows 3 individual progress bars with respective file names.

Negative:
6. Verify that a network interruption during upload freezes the progress bar and displays an error indicator with a "Retry" button.
7. Verify that a server-rejected file displays "Upload failed" on the progress bar with the reason.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the progress bar has a colored fill (green or blue), percentage text, and estimated time text displayed clearly.
9. Verify that the file name is displayed above or alongside each progress bar for identification.
10. Verify that the completed state transitions smoothly from the progress fill to a checkmark icon.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that uploading a very small file (10KB) shows the progress bar briefly before jumping to 100% complete.
12. Verify that a slow upload (simulated low bandwidth) shows an increasing estimated time and the progress bar updates accurately.

Security:
13. Verify that the progress bar does not expose internal server details or file paths during the upload process.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the progress bar updates at least once per second during an active upload.
Verify that the estimated time calculation is accurate within a 20% margin of the actual upload duration.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to delete an uploaded document from the upload queue (before saving) using the delete icon, so that I can remove incorrect files.
Description of User Story: The Upload Queue Delete feature allows Approved Family Contacts to remove incorrectly selected files from the upload queue before the upload is saved or submitted to the server. When files are added to the upload queue (via drag-and-drop or browse), each file entry in the queue displays a thumbnail or file icon, the file name, file size, and a delete icon (trash can or "X" button). Clicking the delete icon immediately removes the file from the upload queue without any server call, as the file has not yet been uploaded. The removal is instant and the queue updates visually to reflect the change. If all files are removed from the queue, the upload area returns to its initial empty state with the drag-and-drop prompt. The family contact can add new files after removing incorrect ones. This feature is available in all upload contexts: standalone upload, in-folder upload, and folder creation with document upload.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the delete icon on a queued file, Then the system must immediately remove the file from the upload queue without any server call.

2. If the file is removed successfully, Then the system must update the queue visually to reflect the removal.
   Else if only one file was in the queue and it is removed, Then the system must display the initial empty upload state with drag-and-drop prompt.

3. If the family contact removes a file and then adds a new file, Then the system must add the new file to the queue alongside any remaining files.

4. If the delete icon is clicked on a file that is currently uploading (mid-transfer), Then the system must cancel the upload and remove the file from the queue.

5. If the queue contains multiple files and one is removed, Then the remaining files must retain their order and details without disruption.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that each file in the upload queue displays a thumbnail or icon, file name, file size, and a red delete icon.
2. Verify that clicking the delete icon on "wrong-file.png" immediately removes it from the queue.
3. Verify that after removing one file from a queue of 3, the remaining 2 files are displayed correctly with their details intact.
4. Verify that removing all files from the queue returns the upload area to the initial drag-and-drop state.
5. Verify that after removing a file, a new file can be added to the queue via drag-and-drop.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking delete on a file currently mid-upload cancels the upload and removes the file from the queue.
7. Verify that the delete action does not trigger any API call to the server.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the delete icon is a clearly visible trash can or "X" button positioned to the right of each file entry in the queue.
9. Verify that removing a file triggers a smooth visual transition (fade out or slide up) for the file being removed.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that rapidly clicking delete on multiple files in succession removes each one correctly without UI glitches.
11. Verify that the delete feature works identically in the standalone upload, in-folder upload, and folder creation modal upload contexts.

Security:
12. Verify that removed files are fully cleared from browser memory and no file data remains cached after deletion from the queue.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that clicking the delete icon removes the file from the queue within 300 milliseconds.
Verify that the upload queue correctly re-renders after deletion within 500 milliseconds for a queue of up to 20 files.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to navigate the portal using a left sidebar menu organized into sections: Home (Dashboard), Schedule (Appointments), Communication (Chat), Clinical (Medication, Vitals & Allergies, Documents), Billing (Statement, Payment, History), and Other (Inventory, Tickets, Incident), so that all features are easily accessible.
Description of User Story: The Left Sidebar Navigation provides Approved Family Contacts with a structured menu for accessing all portal features. The sidebar is displayed on the left side of the screen and is organized into clearly defined sections with expandable subsections. The top of the sidebar displays the Nemi Care logo. The menu sections are: Home containing Dashboard; Schedule containing Appointments; Communication containing Chat; Clinical containing Medication, Vitals & Allergies, and Documents; Billing containing Statement, Payment, and History; and Other containing Inventory, Tickets, and Incident. Each top-level section can be expanded or collapsed to reveal its sub-menu items. The currently active page is highlighted with a blue background or accent color to indicate the user's current location in the portal. Clicking a menu item navigates to the corresponding page. The sidebar is persistent across all pages, providing consistent navigation throughout the portal. At the bottom of the sidebar, the logged-in user's name and email are displayed.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the portal loads for an authenticated family contact, Then the system must render the left sidebar with the Nemi Care logo, all menu sections, and the logged-in user info at the bottom.

2. If the family contact clicks a section header with sub-items, Then the system must expand the section to show sub-menu items.
   Else if the section is already expanded, Then the system must collapse it.
   Else if the section has no sub-items (e.g., Home/Dashboard), Then clicking it must navigate directly to the page.

3. If the family contact navigates to a page, Then the system must highlight the corresponding menu item with a blue background and expand its parent section if collapsed.

4. If the portal is accessed on a smaller screen (tablet), Then the sidebar must collapse to an icon-only mode with a hamburger menu to expand it.

5. If a menu section is disabled or the family contact does not have access to a feature, Then the system must grey out the menu item and prevent navigation.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the left sidebar displays the Nemi Care logo at the top followed by sections: Home, Schedule, Communication, Clinical, Billing, and Other.
2. Verify that expanding the Clinical section reveals sub-items: Medication, Vitals & Allergies, and Documents.
3. Verify that expanding the Billing section reveals sub-items: Statement, Payment, and History.
4. Verify that expanding the Other section reveals sub-items: Inventory, Tickets, and Incident.
5. Verify that clicking "Dashboard" under Home navigates to the Dashboard page and highlights it with a blue background.
6. Verify that clicking "Documents" under Clinical navigates to the Documents page.

Negative:
7. Verify that a greyed-out or disabled menu item does not navigate anywhere when clicked.
8. Verify that an unauthenticated user does not see the sidebar navigation.

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the sidebar has a consistent width, the Nemi Care logo is centered at the top, section headers are bold, and sub-items are indented.
10. Verify that the active menu item has a blue highlight background that is clearly distinguishable from inactive items.
11. Verify that section expand/collapse uses a smooth animation with a chevron icon indicating the state.

Edge Cases:
12. Verify that navigating directly via URL to the Documents page highlights "Documents" under Clinical and expands the Clinical section in the sidebar.
13. Verify that on a tablet screen, the sidebar collapses to icon-only mode and can be expanded with a hamburger menu.

Security:
14. Verify that the sidebar menu only displays features the authenticated family contact has permission to access.
15. Verify that navigating to a restricted page via the sidebar redirects to an appropriate access-denied page.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the sidebar renders completely within 1 second on page load.
Verify that expanding and collapsing sidebar sections completes within 300 milliseconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to switch between linked residents using a dropdown in the page header (e.g., 'Devon Lane' dropdown) on any page, so that I can quickly change context without returning to the dashboard.
Description of User Story: The Resident Switcher Dropdown allows Approved Family Contacts who are linked to multiple residents to switch between them from any page in the portal without navigating back to the dashboard. The dropdown is located in the page header area and displays the currently selected resident's name (e.g., "Devon Lane"). Clicking the dropdown reveals a list of all residents linked to the family contact, each showing the resident's name and optionally their photo or avatar. Selecting a different resident from the dropdown immediately updates the entire page context to reflect the newly selected resident's data, including their medical records, schedules, billing, and documents. The dropdown is accessible from every page in the portal, ensuring seamless context switching. The currently selected resident is indicated with a checkmark or highlight in the dropdown list. If the family contact is linked to only one resident, the dropdown still displays the resident's name but does not expand since there are no other residents to switch to.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the resident dropdown, Then the system must display a list of all linked residents.

2. If the family contact selects a different resident, Then the system must update the entire page context to reflect the new resident's data (medical, billing, documents, etc.).
   Else if the family contact selects the already active resident, Then no context change must occur.

3. If the family contact is linked to only one resident, Then the system must display the resident name in the header but must not expand the dropdown on click.

4. If the context switch fails due to an API error, Then the system must display "Unable to switch resident. Please try again." and retain the previous resident's context.

5. If a linked resident's access is revoked while the family contact is logged in, Then the system must remove that resident from the dropdown on the next interaction.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the page header displays a dropdown with the current resident name "Devon Lane".
2. Verify that clicking the dropdown shows 3 linked residents: "Devon Lane", "Sarah Johnson", and "Mike Smith" with avatars.
3. Verify that selecting "Sarah Johnson" from the dropdown updates the current page to show Sarah Johnson's data.
4. Verify that after switching to "Sarah Johnson", navigating to the Billing page shows Sarah's billing data.
5. Verify that the currently selected resident "Devon Lane" shows a checkmark in the dropdown list.

Negative:
6. Verify that a family contact linked to only one resident sees the name in the header but the dropdown does not expand.
7. Verify that when the resident switch API fails, the system displays "Unable to switch resident. Please try again." and retains the current context.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the resident dropdown is positioned in the page header area with a downward arrow icon indicating it is expandable.
9. Verify that each resident in the dropdown list shows their name and a circular avatar or initials.
10. Verify that the dropdown closes when clicking outside of it or after selecting a resident.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that switching residents on a page with unsaved data prompts the user before proceeding.
12. Verify that a resident whose access is revoked is removed from the dropdown after the family contact's next page navigation.

Security:
13. Verify that the dropdown only displays residents the authenticated family contact is authorized to access.
14. Verify that switching residents does not expose cached data from the previously selected resident.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the resident dropdown expands and displays the list within 500 milliseconds.
Verify that switching residents and reloading the page context completes within 3 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to use a global search icon in the top navigation bar to search across the portal.
Description of User Story: The Global Search feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a universal search capability accessible from the top navigation bar. A search icon (magnifying glass) is permanently displayed in the top navigation bar. Clicking the icon opens a search overlay or expands a search input field. The family contact can type a search query to search across all portal sections including documents, appointments, medications, vitals, billing statements, tickets, and other records. Search results are categorized by section (e.g., Documents, Appointments, Billing) and displayed in a dropdown or overlay with matching items. Each result shows the item name, the section it belongs to, and a brief description or date. Clicking a result navigates directly to the relevant page and item. The search supports partial matching, is case-insensitive, and provides instant results as the user types (debounced). A "No results found" message appears when no items match the query. The search can be dismissed by pressing Escape or clicking outside the overlay.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the search icon, Then the system must open a search overlay or expand the search input field.

2. If the family contact types a query of 2 or more characters, Then the system must perform a debounced search across all portal sections and display categorized results.
   Else if the query is fewer than 2 characters, Then the system must not trigger a search.

3. If matching results are found, Then the system must display them categorized by section with item name, section label, and brief description.
   Else if no results match, Then the system must display "No results found for '[query]'".

4. If the family contact clicks a search result, Then the system must navigate to the result's page and close the search overlay.

5. If the search service is unavailable, Then the system must display "Search is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later."
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the top navigation bar displays a magnifying glass search icon on all portal pages.
2. Verify that clicking the search icon opens a search input field or overlay.
3. Verify that typing "medication" returns results categorized under "Clinical" with matching medication records.
4. Verify that clicking a search result for an appointment navigates to the Appointments page with the matching appointment highlighted.
5. Verify that pressing Escape closes the search overlay and returns to the current page.

Negative:
6. Verify that typing only 1 character does not trigger a search.
7. Verify that searching for "zzz999" displays "No results found for 'zzz999'".
8. Verify that when the search service is down, the system displays "Search is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later."

UI Scenarios:
9. Verify that the search icon is positioned in the top navigation bar alongside the notification bell, AI icon, and profile avatar.
10. Verify that search results display in a dropdown overlay with section headers, item names, and brief descriptions in a clean layout.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that searching while on a specific page (e.g., Documents) still returns results from all sections, not just Documents.
12. Verify that rapid typing triggers a debounced search (single API call) rather than multiple requests per keystroke.

Security:
13. Verify that global search results only include items the authenticated family contact has permission to view.
14. Verify that search queries are sanitized to prevent injection attacks.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that search results appear within 2 seconds of typing at least 2 characters.
Verify that the search overlay opens within 500 milliseconds of clicking the search icon.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to click a notification bell icon in the top navigation bar to view recent notifications.
Description of User Story: The Notification Bell feature provides Approved Family Contacts with a notification center accessible from the top navigation bar. A bell icon is displayed in the top navigation bar, and when there are unread notifications, a badge counter displays the number of unread items on the bell icon. Clicking the bell icon opens a dropdown panel or overlay showing a list of recent notifications in reverse chronological order. Each notification displays an icon or category indicator, a brief title, a description or message, and a timestamp (e.g., "2 hours ago"). Notification types include appointment reminders, document signing requests, billing updates, medication changes, new messages, and system announcements. Each notification is clickable and navigates the family contact to the relevant page or detail. Notifications can be marked as read individually or all at once via a "Mark All as Read" option. The unread badge counter updates in real time as new notifications arrive or as notifications are read.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the bell icon, Then the system must display a notification panel with recent notifications.

2. If there are unread notifications, Then the system must display a badge counter on the bell icon showing the unread count.
   Else if all notifications are read, Then the bell icon must be displayed without a badge counter.

3. If the family contact clicks on a notification, Then the system must navigate to the relevant page and mark the notification as read.
   Else if the family contact clicks "Mark All as Read", Then the system must mark all notifications as read and clear the badge counter.

4. If there are no notifications at all, Then the system must display "No notifications" in the notification panel.

5. If a new notification arrives while the panel is open, Then the system must add it to the top of the list and increment the badge counter.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the top navigation bar displays a bell icon with a badge showing "5" for 5 unread notifications.
2. Verify that clicking the bell icon opens a panel showing 5 notifications in reverse chronological order.
3. Verify that a notification displays an icon, title "Document Signing Required", description "Please sign the Intake Form", and timestamp "2 hours ago".
4. Verify that clicking the "Document Signing Required" notification navigates to the Documents page and marks it as read.
5. Verify that clicking "Mark All as Read" clears the badge counter and marks all notifications as read.

Negative:
6. Verify that when there are no notifications, the panel displays "No notifications".
7. Verify that when the notification service is unavailable, the bell icon is still displayed but shows no counter, and clicking it displays "Unable to load notifications."

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the notification bell icon is positioned in the top navigation bar between the search icon and the AI icon.
9. Verify that the badge counter is a small red circle with white text overlapping the top-right of the bell icon.
10. Verify that the notification panel has a scrollable list with a "Mark All as Read" link at the top.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that a new notification arriving while the panel is open appears at the top of the list in real time.
12. Verify that a family contact with 100+ notifications can scroll through the panel without performance issues.

Security:
13. Verify that notifications are specific to the authenticated family contact and do not expose other users' notifications.
14. Verify that notification content does not include sensitive PHI details in the preview text.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the notification panel opens and displays recent notifications within 1 second.
Verify that real-time notification updates appear within 3 seconds of the event occurring.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to see an AI assistant icon in the top navigation bar for quick assistance.
Description of User Story: The AI Assistant Icon feature places a visible AI assistant icon in the top navigation bar, providing Approved Family Contacts with quick access to AI-powered assistance. The icon is displayed alongside the search icon, notification bell, and profile avatar. The AI icon uses a recognizable design (such as a robot, sparkle, or brain icon) to indicate artificial intelligence capabilities. Clicking the AI icon opens an AI assistant interface, which could be a chat panel, a modal dialog, or a sidebar overlay where the family contact can type questions or requests. The AI assistant can help with navigating the portal, answering frequently asked questions about the care facility, explaining medical terms, providing guidance on completing forms, and offering general support. The icon may include a subtle animation or glow effect to draw attention to its availability. The AI assistant panel can be closed by clicking outside it, pressing Escape, or clicking a close button.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the AI assistant icon, Then the system must open the AI assistant interface.

2. If the family contact types a question and the AI can respond, Then the system must display a relevant and helpful response.
   Else if the AI cannot process the request, Then the system must display "I'm sorry, I couldn't understand your request. Please try rephrasing or contact support for help."

3. If the family contact clicks outside the AI panel or presses Escape, Then the system must close the AI interface.

4. If the AI service is unavailable, Then the system must display "AI assistant is currently unavailable. Please try again later." when the icon is clicked.

5. If the family contact reopens the AI assistant, Then the system must retain the previous conversation context within the same session.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the top navigation bar displays an AI assistant icon (robot, sparkle, or brain icon) on all portal pages.
2. Verify that clicking the AI icon opens an AI assistant chat panel or sidebar.
3. Verify that typing "How do I upload a document?" returns a helpful response with step-by-step guidance.
4. Verify that clicking a portal link within an AI response navigates to the referenced page.
5. Verify that closing and reopening the AI panel retains the previous conversation within the same session.

Negative:
6. Verify that when the AI service is unavailable, clicking the icon displays "AI assistant is currently unavailable. Please try again later."
7. Verify that entering an unrecognizable query displays "I'm sorry, I couldn't understand your request. Please try rephrasing or contact support for help."

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the AI icon is positioned in the top navigation bar between the notification bell and the profile avatar.
9. Verify that the AI assistant panel opens as a chat interface with a text input field, send button, and conversation area.
10. Verify that the AI icon has a subtle visual indicator (glow, pulse, or color) to draw attention to its availability.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that the AI assistant can handle 20 consecutive messages in a single session without losing conversation context.
12. Verify that pressing Escape closes the AI panel even if a response is still loading.

Security:
13. Verify that the AI assistant does not expose sensitive patient data beyond what the authenticated family contact is authorized to see.
14. Verify that AI conversation logs are stored securely and not accessible to other users.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the AI assistant panel opens within 1 second of clicking the icon.
Verify that the AI provides a response to a standard query within 5 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to click my profile avatar in the top navigation bar to access my profile and settings.
Description of User Story: The Profile Avatar Menu feature places the family contact's profile avatar in the top navigation bar as a clickable element that provides access to profile and settings pages. The avatar displays the family contact's profile photo or their initials in a circular format. Clicking the avatar opens a dropdown menu with options including "My Profile", "Settings", "Help & Support", and "Log Out". Selecting "My Profile" navigates to the profile page where the family contact can view and edit their personal information. Selecting "Settings" navigates to the settings page for notification preferences, display preferences, and account settings. The "Help & Support" option navigates to a help center or FAQ page. The "Log Out" option ends the session and redirects to the login page. The dropdown displays the family contact's name and email at the top for identification. The menu closes when the family contact clicks outside of it or selects an option.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the family contact clicks the profile avatar, Then the system must open a dropdown menu with profile, settings, help, and logout options.

2. If the family contact has a profile photo, Then the avatar must display the photo in a circular format.
   Else if no profile photo is set, Then the avatar must display the family contact's initials in a colored circle.

3. If the family contact clicks "My Profile", Then the system must navigate to the profile page and close the dropdown.
   Else if the family contact clicks "Settings", Then the system must navigate to the settings page.
   Else if the family contact clicks "Help & Support", Then the system must navigate to the help center.
   Else if the family contact clicks "Log Out", Then the system must end the session, clear session data, and redirect to the login page.

4. If the family contact clicks outside the dropdown, Then the system must close the dropdown without any navigation.

5. If the session has expired, Then clicking the avatar must redirect to the login page instead of opening the dropdown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the top navigation bar displays the profile avatar as a circular image or initials for the logged-in family contact.
2. Verify that clicking the avatar opens a dropdown showing the family contact's name "Ralph Edwards" and email "ralph.edwards@email.com" at the top.
3. Verify that the dropdown includes "My Profile", "Settings", "Help & Support", and "Log Out" options.
4. Verify that clicking "My Profile" navigates to the profile page.
5. Verify that clicking "Log Out" ends the session and redirects to the login page.

Negative:
6. Verify that clicking outside the dropdown closes it without navigation.
7. Verify that if the session has expired, clicking the avatar redirects to the login page.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the profile avatar is the rightmost element in the top navigation bar.
9. Verify that the dropdown menu has a clean layout with the name and email at the top, followed by menu items with icons (profile icon, gear icon, help icon, logout icon).
10. Verify that a family contact without a profile photo sees their initials "RE" in a colored circular avatar.

Edge Cases:
11. Verify that clicking "Log Out" clears all session data including cached resident data and search history.
12. Verify that the dropdown positions correctly on different screen sizes without being clipped by the viewport edge.

Security:
13. Verify that the "Log Out" action invalidates the session token on the server side, not just on the client.
14. Verify that after logging out, pressing the browser back button does not show cached portal pages.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the avatar dropdown menu opens within 300 milliseconds of clicking.
Verify that the "Log Out" action completes and redirects to the login page within 2 seconds.

User Story: As an Approved Family Contact, I should be able to see my name and email displayed at the bottom of the sidebar, so that I know which account is logged in.
Description of User Story: The Sidebar User Info feature displays the authenticated Approved Family Contact's name and email address at the bottom of the left sidebar navigation. This persistent display ensures the family contact always knows which account is currently logged in, which is particularly important for family contacts who may manage multiple accounts or share a device. The user info section shows the family contact's full name (e.g., "Ralph Edwards") on one line and their email address (e.g., "ralph.edwards@email.com") on a second line below the name. The text is styled in a smaller font size compared to the menu items to differentiate it from navigation elements. The user info section may include a small avatar or initials icon next to the name. The section is fixed at the bottom of the sidebar and remains visible even when the menu is scrolled. Clicking the name or email area may optionally navigate to the profile page.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the sidebar renders, Then the system must display the authenticated family contact's full name and email at the bottom of the sidebar.

2. If the family contact's name is longer than the sidebar width, Then the system must truncate the name with an ellipsis and show the full name on hover.
   Else if the email is longer than the sidebar width, Then the system must truncate the email with an ellipsis and show the full email on hover.

3. If the sidebar menu items exceed the sidebar height, Then the user info section must remain fixed at the bottom while the menu items scroll.

4. If the family contact has a profile photo, Then the user info section must display a small circular avatar next to the name.
   Else if no photo is set, Then the section must display the contact's initials in a small circular badge.

5. If the family contact clicks the user info section, Then the system may navigate to the profile page or do nothing (implementation-dependent).
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1. Verify that the bottom of the sidebar displays "Ralph Edwards" as the name and "ralph.edwards@email.com" as the email for the logged-in family contact.
2. Verify that a small circular avatar or initials icon is displayed next to the name in the user info section.
3. Verify that the user info section remains fixed at the bottom when the sidebar menu is scrolled.
4. Verify that the name and email text is styled in a smaller font than the sidebar menu items.
5. Verify that clicking the user info section navigates to the profile page (if implemented).

Negative:
6. Verify that the user info section does not display for unauthenticated users.
7. Verify that if the API fails to load user info, the section displays a placeholder "User" text.

UI Scenarios:
8. Verify that the user info section is visually separated from the navigation menu items with a divider line or spacing.
9. Verify that the name appears on the first line and the email on the second line in a lighter or muted text color.

Edge Cases:
10. Verify that a name with 40 characters is truncated with an ellipsis and the full name is shown on hover tooltip.
11. Verify that an email like "very.long.email.address@extremely-long-domain-name.com" is truncated with an ellipsis and the full email is shown on hover.
12. Verify that when the sidebar is in collapsed/icon-only mode (tablet), the user info section hides or shows only the avatar.

Security:
13. Verify that the user info section displays only the authenticated family contact's own name and email.
14. Verify that the name and email data are fetched from the authenticated session and not from client-side storage that could be tampered with.
Performance Scenarios:
Verify that the user info section renders within the sidebar's initial 1-second load time.
Verify that the user info remains responsive and does not cause layout shifts during page transitions.
